/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:2:source: docs/ORCHESTRATION.md (v1.1, 2026-04-23 + amendments)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:4:status: digest — read repo doc for full rules
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:7:# ORCHESTRATION contract — digest
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:9:`docs/ORCHESTRATION.md` is the authoritative agent-orchestration
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:10:contract for the CryoDAQ repo. This page is a digest. **Read the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:11:repo doc end-to-end at the start of every CC session** —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:12:[[_meta/glossary]] flags this as a memory invariant.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:14:## §1. Why this document exists
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:15:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:16:Between 2026-04-21 and 2026-04-23 the repo accumulated 4 parallel
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:17:feature branches (none merged), 9 duplicate commits, 12 untracked
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:18:review markdowns at root, 4 conflicting orchestrator state
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:19:directories, and a hardening-vs-feature collision that produced a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:20:misattribution. None of this happened because individual models
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:21:were bad. It happened because there was no central coordinator.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:22:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:23:## §2. Core principle
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:24:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:25:**Claude Code is the central coordinator.** Every other model —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:26:Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, GLM, Kimi, DeepSeek, metaswarm — operates in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:27:**consultant mode**: text in, text out, CC reads and converts to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:28:final action. Consultants never push, never branch, never write
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:29:files in the repo except via CC.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:30:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:31:## §3. Role matrix
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:32:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:33:Architect (Vladimir + web Claude during architect sessions) provides
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:34:mission and constraints. CC executes. Consultants advise. **One
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:35:coordinator at a time** — no parallel CC sessions on the same
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:36:branch.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:38:## §4. Consultant invocation protocol
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:40:Brief at
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:41:`artifacts/consultations/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<topic>/<consultant>.prompt.md`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:42:with mission / context files / numbered questions / expected output
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:43:format / response file path. Response lands at `*.response.md`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:44:After all consultations CC writes a `synthesis.md` covering
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:45:agreement / disagreement / final decision.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:46:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:47:**Synthesis is the durable artifact.** Raw consultant output can be
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:48:deleted after 30 days; synthesis stays.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:49:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:50:§4.4 anti-prompts: "review the entire repo" / "give me 10 things to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:51:improve" / "what do you think overall" — invite slop. Valid prompts
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:52:are scoped and concrete.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:53:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:54:## §5. Branch and merge discipline
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:55:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:56:| Type | Pattern | Lifetime |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:57:|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:58:| Main | `master` | forever |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:59:| Feature | `feat/<slug>` | ≤ 2 days |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:60:| Experiment | `experiment/<slug>` | ≤ 1 day |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:61:| Hotfix | `hotfix/<slug>` | ≤ 4 hours |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:62:| Architect fork | `arch/<yyyy-mm-dd>-<purpose>` | until merged |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:63:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:64:**One active feature branch.** Branches live ≤ 2 days. After 3+
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:65:days CC auto-aborts at next session. Worktree dirs in `.worktrees/`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:66:must match branch names exactly. Commit body must include
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:67:`Ref:`, `Batch:` (if multi-commit), and `Risk:` for changes to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:68:`launcher.py` / `engine.py` / `safety_manager.py` / `zmq_*.py`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:69:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:70:## §6. Artifact discipline
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:71:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:72:Strict root whitelist (`README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:73:`CODEX_ARCHITECTURE_CONTROL_PLANE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:74:`PROJECT_STATUS.md`, `RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:75:`DOC_REALITY_MAP.md` (historical, may be removed),
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:76:`LICENSE`, `THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md`, `CC_PROMPT_*.md`, `HANDOFF_*.md`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:77:`SESSION_DETAIL_*.md`). Audit / review / hardening output goes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:78:under `docs/audits/<date-slug>/` or
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:79:`artifacts/consultations/<date>/<topic>/`, never at root.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:81:Decision ledger: every session CC writes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:82:`docs/decisions/<YYYY-MM-DD>-session.md` with `## HH:MM —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:83:<decision>` blocks (Thesis / Reasoning / Decision / Consulted /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:84:Open). The ledger is the long-term memory.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:85:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:86:## §7. Hardening + feature interaction rule
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:87:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:88:**The rule that exists because of `b2b4fb5` vs IV.7.** Before
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:89:landing a hardening / tightening change, check whether it could
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:90:affect any active feature branch. If yes, hold or merge into the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:91:feature branch and retest. If no, document why not in the commit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:92:`Risk:` line. Active branches are visible via `git worktree list`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:93:— check before tightening shared infra. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:94:[[30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:95:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:96:## §8. Architect-absent operating mode
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:97:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:98:When architect is unavailable (quota window): CC continues
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:99:**only on previously-architected plans**. No new directions, no
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:100:new feature branches for initiative work. Bug / regression fixes on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:101:in-progress work are OK. End-of-session handoff goes to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:102:`artifacts/handoffs/<date>-cc-to-architect.md`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:103:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:104:## §9. Document evolution
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:105:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:106:This contract is authoritative but not immutable. Disagreement
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:107:goes through the architect via decision-ledger note → next
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:108:architect session → commit. Do **not** silently bypass.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:109:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:110:## §10. Session start checklist
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:111:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:112:Read this doc end-to-end + `CLAUDE.md`. `git status`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:113:`git branch --show-current`, `git worktree list`. Check
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:114:`artifacts/consultations/` for unsynthesized responses. Check
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:115:`docs/decisions/` for the latest session ledger. Stale
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:116:(>2 day) feature branch detected → block work, surface to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:117:architect.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:118:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:119:## §11. Known active plans
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:120:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:121:Tracked table — only plans listed here can be worked on. If a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:122:consultant claims to be working on something else, STOP.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:123:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:124:## §12. Failure-mode reference
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:125:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:126:Six historical failure modes are enumerated as the rationale
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:127:behind the rules:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:128:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:129:1. Parallel rewrites (`codex/b1-truth-recovery` vs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:130:   `codex/safe-merge-b1-truth-recovery`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:131:2. Root-directory markdown flood (12 ungoverned `.md` at root).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:132:3. Hardening / feature collision (`b2b4fb5` × IV.7).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:133:4. No-leader multi-agent drift (five agents reviewing without
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:134:   synthesis).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:135:5. Lost context (untracked architect dump for 3 days).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:136:6. Over-strict stopping — added retroactively after the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:137:   2026-04-23 cleanup-baseline session
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:138:   ([[40 Decisions/2026-04-23-cleanup-baseline]]).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:139:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:140:## §13. STOP discipline and autonomy band (added 2026-04-23)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:141:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:142:§13.1 — when CC must STOP: irreversible data loss / git rewrite,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:143:genuine semantic ambiguity, architect-domain decision, sensitive
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:144:material, file > 100 KB about to be silently lost, real test
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:145:failure, junk consultant response.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:146:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:147:§13.2 — when CC must NOT stop, adapt instead: 1-line plan
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:148:inaccuracy, mechanical detail differs, untracked file would be
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:149:lost (preserve to archive then proceed — the "no delete" rule is a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:150:PRESERVE rule, not a STOP rule).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:151:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:152:§13.3 — adaptations are recorded inline in the session ledger as
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:153:`Plan said / Reality / Adapted / Outcome`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:154:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:155:§13.4 — the preserve-vs-delete clarification: archive routine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:156:plan-document patterns and proceed; STOP only on > 100 KB,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:157:sensitive content, credential paths, or binary garbage.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:158:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:159:§13.5 — autonomy band explicitly granted for commit message
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:160:wording, file-path adjustments, mechanical step reordering, side-
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:161:commits to preserve untracked files. NOT granted for branch
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:162:merges outside plan, file deletions outside `git branch -D`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:163:targets, history rewrites, consultant invocations on already-
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:164:covered decisions, new feature branches under §8.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:165:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:166:§13.6 — calibration principle: rules that over-stop waste
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:167:architect time; rules that under-stop risk silent breakage. The
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:168:ledger + handoff are the mechanism that lets CC run with relaxed
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:169:stopping discipline; architect end-of-day review catches anything
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:170:that should have been a stop.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:171:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:172:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:173:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:174:- `~/Projects/cryodaq/docs/ORCHESTRATION.md` — full text (~600 lines)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:175:- [[50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation]] — skill that operates
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:176:  under §4
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:177:- [[50 Workflow/Calibration loops history]] — how prompts and CC
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:178:  behavior evolved into this contract
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:179:- [[40 Decisions/2026-04-23-cleanup-baseline]] — first session run
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:180:  under this contract
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:2:source: CC_PROMPT_OVERNIGHT_SWARM_2026-04-24.md; docs/decisions/2026-04-24-overnight-swarm-launch.md; .claude/skills/multi-model-consultation.md §6/§7
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:4:status: synthesized — pattern reusable for future overnight runs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:7:# Overnight swarm pattern
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:9:CC dispatching 10 scoped consultation jobs to Codex + Gemini in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:10:parallel, sleeping while they run, synthesizing in the morning.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:11:Pattern validated 2026-04-24 (10/10 jobs landed) and reused 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:12:for vault build audits.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:14:## When the pattern fits
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:15:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:16:- Architect has identified ≥ 4 independent questions where second-
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:17:  model judgment would help.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:18:- Each question has a scoped brief (one diff, one hypothesis, one
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:19:  module's drift, etc.) — not "review the entire repo" slop.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:20:- Architect is going to sleep / stepping away; the time can be
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:21:  spent productively or wasted entirely.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:22:- All briefs cleanly fit the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:23:  [[50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation|multi-model consultation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:24:  skill]] §1.x routing.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:25:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:26:## What the prompt looks like
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:28:A swarm prompt at root (`CC_PROMPT_OVERNIGHT_SWARM_<date>.md`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:29:enumerates jobs:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:30:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:31:- 5 Codex briefs (`codex-NN-<topic>`) — narrow adversarial review
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:32:  questions
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:33:- 5 Gemini briefs (`gemini-NN-<topic>`) — wide-context structural
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:34:  questions
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:36:Each brief follows skill §2 templates. Briefs have anti-anchoring
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:37:rules where the same question goes to both models (alphabetical
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:38:ordering of options, neutral tone, no hint at expected answer).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:40:## Dispatch (proven 2026-04-24)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:42:CLI-direct, not slash-command-driven. Codex parallel, Gemini
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:43:serial:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:44:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:45:```bash
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:46:# Codex — 5 in parallel
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:47:for n in 01 02 03 04 05; do
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:48:  nohup bash -c '
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:49:    codex exec \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:50:      -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:51:      --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:52:      --cd ~/Projects/cryodaq \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:53:      < BRIEFS/codex-'"$n"'-...prompt.md \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:54:      > RESPONSES/codex-'"$n"'-...response.md 2>&1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:55:  ' </dev/null >/dev/null 2>&1 &
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:56:done
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:58:# Gemini — single serial chain (rate limit + tool access)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:59:nohup bash -c '
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:60:  for brief in BRIEFS/gemini-*.prompt.md; do
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:61:    response=$(echo "$brief" | sed "s/BRIEFS/RESPONSES/; s/prompt/response/")
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:62:    gemini -m gemini-3.1-pro-preview --yolo \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:63:      -p "$(cat "$brief")" \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:64:      > "$response" 2>&1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:65:    sleep 30
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:66:  done
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:67:' </dev/null >/dev/null 2>&1 &
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:68:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:69:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:70:Tracking PIDs go to a `.pids` file under the artifacts dir for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:71:later sweep + diagnostic.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:72:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:73:## What worked (2026-04-24, 10/10)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:74:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:75:- Codex parallel held up. Each Codex (`gpt-5.5 / high`) ran in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:76:  ~1–10 min wall-clock per brief.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:77:- Codex `gpt-5.5` model name accepted on first try after a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:78:  3-token probe. §3.7 fallback to gpt-5.4 did not fire.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:79:- Briefs landed clean responses with file:line refs.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:81:## What didn't (and the workaround)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:82:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:83:- **Gemini parallel rate-limited.** 5 simultaneous gemini-2.5-pro
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:84:  calls exhausted per-minute capacity within ~20 s. Variants of
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:85:  "Your quota will reset after 0s".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:86:- **Gemini default approval mode blocked tool access.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:87:  `run_shell_command` not approved → Gemini couldn't read files /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:88:  run `git show` even though the brief said to.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:89:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:90:Workaround: kill the parallel Gemini wrapper PIDs (carefully —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:91:avoid pre-existing unrelated gemini processes), zero out partial
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:92:error response files, relaunch as a single serial chain with 30 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:93:gaps and `--yolo`. Single tracking PID.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:94:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:95:Cost: serial chain bottoms out at ~30–60 min for 5 jobs vs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:96:Codex's true-parallel ~10–15 min. Acceptable for overnight.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:97:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:98:## Morning retrieval (Phase 6–9 of the prompt)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:99:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:100:1. **Verify completion.** `.pids` file shows wrapper exit. Lost /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:101:   slop cases (skill §4.2: < 500 words, no file:line refs,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:102:   evasive) → retry once with tighter brief.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:103:2. **Per-stream synthesis.** Group Codex + Gemini briefs by topic
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:104:   stream (e.g. "B1 root cause" + "transport drift") into single
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:105:   `STREAM_SYNTHESES/<stream>.md`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:106:3. **Master summary.** `MASTER_SUMMARY.md` with stream decisions +
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:107:   priority action list + TL;DR.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:108:4. **Single commit** of the whole `artifacts/consultations/<date>/`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:109:   tree + synthesis + summary, batch-labeled.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:110:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:111:## Adaptations from plan (§13.3 ledger format)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:112:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:113:Adaptations recorded inline in the session ledger:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:114:`Plan said / Reality / Adapted / Outcome`. The 2026-04-24 launch
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:115:recorded three:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:116:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:117:- CLI-direct dispatch instead of `/codex:rescue` slash form.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:118:- Model probe before batch (gpt-5.5 + gemini-2.5-pro both passed
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:119:  3-token probe).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:120:- Gemini parallel → serial chain after rate-limit + tool-access
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:121:  failures.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:122:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:123:## Reuse pattern
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:124:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:125:The same shape works for any "scoped, parallel, can-tolerate-slop-
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:126:filter" review. Examples:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:127:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:128:- Vault audit gates (this 2026-04-26 build) — Codex literal +
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:129:  Gemini structural per phase.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:130:- Multi-stream architectural review (2026-04-24 syntheses).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:131:- D-bucket review (drop / merge classification across N branches).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:132:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:133:What does NOT fit the pattern:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:134:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:135:- Bug reproduction. Needs interactive iteration; no overnight
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:136:  acceleration.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:137:- Single ambiguous decision. One scoped Codex review is enough;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:138:  swarm is overkill.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:139:- Synthesis-heavy work. Models can't synthesize; CC has to do
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:140:  that next morning.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:141:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:142:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:143:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:144:- [[50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract]] §4 — consultation protocol
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:145:- [[50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation]] — skill that gates
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:146:  per-job decisions
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:147:- [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-overnight-swarm-launch]] — launch
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:148:  ledger from the proven run
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:149:- [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution]] — what came out of
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:150:  the syntheses
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:2:source: PROJECT_STATUS.md "A1 safety audit"; CLAUDE.md "Кодировка файлов"; docs/design-system/ rules; config/alarms_v3.yaml history
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:4:status: closed — regression test in place
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:7:# Cyrillic Т homoglyph in alarm config
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:9:A small story about a bug that tests passed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:10:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:11:## Story
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:13:`config/alarms_v3.yaml` is the YAML-driven alarm rule set
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:14:([[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]]). Channels in CryoDAQ are named
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:15:with Cyrillic Т (U+0422) prefix — `Т1`, `Т12`, etc. — because that's
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:16:the Russian convention in `config/instruments.yaml`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:17:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:18:At some point an LLM-generated edit introduced **Latin "T"** in the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:19:rule patterns where Cyrillic "Т" was meant. The two letters render
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:20:identically in most fonts; tests that exercised the alarm engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:21:mechanics (threshold logic, hysteresis, message formatting) all
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:22:passed because the engine logic was correct. The rule simply never
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:23:fired against any real channel — Latin T wasn't in the channel
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:24:namespace.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:25:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:26:Caught by adversarial second-model review. The fix added a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:27:regression test for the channel-prefix character class plus a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:28:Cyrillic-Т invariant in `docs/design-system/` rules.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:29:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:30:## Where the homoglyph lurks
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:31:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:32:- `config/alarms_v3.yaml` rule channel patterns (this incident).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:33:- `config/interlocks.yaml::overheat_cryostat::channel_pattern` — was
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:34:  fixed for Cyrillic Т earlier (commit `9feaf3e`). Current master
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:35:  pattern is `Т[1-8] .*`; Т4 exclusion exists at the alarm-group
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:36:  level (`alarms_v3.yaml` `uncalibrated`/`all_temp` groups omit Т4
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:37:  and Т8) rather than at the interlock regex level. Tighter
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:38:  `Т(1|2|3|5|6|7|8) .*` was discussed during 2026-04-20 lab work but
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:39:  is not in master at the time of this build's audit.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:40:- `config/interlocks.yaml::overheat_compressor::channel_pattern` —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:41:  separate `Т12 .*` pattern; same homoglyph hazard, addressed in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:42:  the A1 audit fix.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:43:- `docs/design-system/` rules involving "мбар" vs "mbar" (similar
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:44:  class — Latin `mbar` got flagged in `1c61268` CRITICAL domain
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:45:  cleanup).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:46:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:47:## Why it survives ordinary review
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:48:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:49:- **Visual identity** — Cyrillic Т and Latin T are visually
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:50:  indistinguishable in most monospace fonts. PR diff review by a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:51:  human reading the screen catches nothing.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:52:- **Test design** — alarm tests ran the engine on synthetic
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:53:  channels named in the test. They didn't cross-reference back to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:54:  the production `instruments.yaml` namespace.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:55:- **Encoding** — both characters are valid UTF-8, both serialize
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:56:  fine in YAML. The file never failed to load.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:58:## Defenses now in place
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:59:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:60:- **Regression test** that asserts every channel pattern in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:61:  `config/alarms_v3.yaml` matches at least one channel in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:62:  `config/channels.yaml` after canonical resolution.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:63:- **Design system rules** (`docs/design-system/rules/*-rules.md`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:64:  carry explicit Cyrillic-Т-vs-Latin-T invariant for any operator-
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:65:  facing rendered text.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:66:- **Adversarial second-model review** (`docs/CODEX_SELF_REVIEW_PLAYBOOK.md`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:67:  — Codex gpt-5.5/high catches homoglyph-class issues alongside
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:68:  semantic regressions.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:69:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:70:## Encoding policy (CLAUDE.md "Кодировка файлов")
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:71:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:72:- Python source / Markdown / YAML in repo: UTF-8 **without BOM**.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:73:- BOM is applied **only** to operator-facing CSV exports
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:74:  (`storage/csv_export.py`) — Excel on Russian Windows needs the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:75:  BOM prefix to read Cyrillic correctly.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:76:- Do not add BOM to any source file. External reviewers occasionally
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:77:  flag a BOM invariant for source — that's misapplication.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:78:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:79:## Lesson
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:81:Tests prove logic correctness, not identifier correctness. When a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:82:rule and the channel namespace it operates over both come from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:83:different files, the regression test must close that loop
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:84:explicitly. Visual review can never catch homoglyphs reliably.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:85:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:86:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:87:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:88:- [[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:89:- [[20 Drivers/LakeShore 218S]] — Cyrillic Т prefix origin
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:90:- [[30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis]] — different lesson
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config.md:91:  from the same year about model confidence vs tests
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:2:source: CHANGELOG.md `be51a24` IV.6 partial mitigation; ROADMAP.md "IV.6 partial mitigation outcome" + "watchdog regression"; docs/bug_B1_zmq_idle_death_handoff.md "Codex revised analysis"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:4:status: shipped — defense-in-depth, does NOT close B1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:7:# IV.6 — Cmd plane hardening
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:9:What shipped, what it didn't fix, why it stayed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:10:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:11:## What shipped (`be51a24`, 2026-04-20)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:13:The full Codex-proposed B1 fix plan (H2):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:14:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:15:1. **Per-command ephemeral REQ socket** in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:16:   `zmq_subprocess.cmd_forward_loop`. `_new_req_socket()` creates a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:17:   fresh REQ for each command; closed after reply or timeout.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:18:   Matches ZeroMQ Guide ch.4 canonical "poll / timeout / close /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:19:   reopen" pattern.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:20:2. **Launcher-side `command_channel_stalled()` watchdog** in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:21:   `_poll_bridge_data` — restarts bridge subprocess if cmd plane is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:22:   stalled while data plane stays alive.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:23:3. **`TCP_KEEPALIVE` reverted on command + PUB paths** (kept on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:24:   `sub_drain_loop` SUB as orthogonal safeguard). Keepalive added
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:25:   on the original idle-reap hypothesis (commit `f5f9039`); H1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:26:   falsified — Linux `tcp_keepalive_time = 7200 s` rules out kernel
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:27:   reaping at 120 s.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:28:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:29:Tests: 60/60 unit tests green; full subtree 1775/1776 (1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:30:pre-existing flaky).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:31:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:32:## What it didn't fix
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:33:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:34:B1 still reproduces structurally identical to pre-fix:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:36:| Diag | Pre-fix | Post-fix (IV.6) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:37:|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:38:| `diag_zmq_idle_hypothesis.py` SPARSE_0.33HZ | cmd #10 FAIL ~30 s | cmd #8 FAIL 56 s |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:39:| `diag_zmq_bridge_extended.py` | cmd #28 FAIL 92 s, 0/3 recovery | cmd #48 FAIL 82 s, 0/3 recovery |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:40:| RAPID_5HZ | 295/295 OK | 295/295 OK |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:42:Codex's H2 (shared-REQ-state) was empirically falsified. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:43:[[30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis]]. The bridge
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:44:subprocess's REQ-to-engine path goes silently unresponsive after
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:45:~30–90 s of bridge uptime while engine REP itself remains healthy
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:46:to direct in-process clients (0.001 s reply mid-failure) and the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:47:asyncio loop, data-plane PUB, heartbeats, scheduler writes, and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:48:plugin ticks all stay healthy. The wedge is in the bridge ↔ engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:49:loopback path, not the engine REP socket.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:50:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:51:## Watchdog regression + cooldown hotfix (2026-04-20 evening)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:52:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:53:The IV.6 watchdog initially had a bug: `_last_cmd_timeout` persisted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:54:across watchdog-triggered subprocess restart. A fresh subprocess
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:55:saw a stale timeout signal on the first poll cycle, triggered
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:56:another restart, etc. — restart storm (30–40 restarts/minute on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:57:Ubuntu lab PC).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:59:Hotfix in `src/cryodaq/launcher.py`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:60:- 60 s cooldown between command-watchdog restarts via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:61:  `_last_cmd_watchdog_restart` timestamp.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:62:- Missing `return` after restart added so no further checks run in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:63:  the same poll cycle.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:64:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:65:Effect: restart storm gone, but B1 itself still fires. System
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:66:reaches usable steady state of "works ~60–120 s, one restart, works
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:67:again" — workaround until IV.7 ipc:// or other root-cause fix.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:68:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:69:## Why it stayed in master
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:70:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:71:Even though IV.6 doesn't fix B1, the changes are kept:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:72:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:73:- **Architectural improvement.** Matches ZeroMQ Guide ch.4
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:74:  canonical reliable req-reply pattern. Future maintainers expect
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:75:  this shape.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:76:- **Removes a real brittle point.** Shared REQ state across
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:77:  commands was a long-term maintainability hazard regardless of
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:78:  whether it caused B1.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:79:- **Gives the launcher a cmd-channel watchdog.** Separate from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:80:  data-plane health detection, the launcher can now detect and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:81:  recover from any future command-only failure shape.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:82:- **Defense-in-depth.** B1 root cause may be deeper still; if a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:83:  future bug surfaces with a similar shape, IV.6 closes one of the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:84:  candidate failure modes.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:85:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:86:## Related fixes shipped alongside
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:87:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:88:- `aabd75f` — `engine: wire validate_checksum through Thyracont
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:89:  driver loader`. `_create_instruments()` was ignoring the YAML
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:90:  key; driver defaulted to `True` regardless of config. Resolves
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:91:  TopWatchBar pressure em-dash on Ubuntu lab PC (VSP206 vs VSP63D
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:92:  checksum). See [[20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:93:- `74dbbc7` — `reporting: xml_safe sanitizer for python-docx
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:94:  compatibility`. Keithley VISA resource strings contain `\x00`;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:95:  python-docx rejected them as XML 1.0 incompatible. New
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:96:  `src/cryodaq/utils/xml_safe.py` strips XML-illegal control chars
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:97:  at all add_paragraph / cell.text sites in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:98:  `reporting/sections.py`. See [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:99:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:100:## Lesson
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:101:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:102:Shipping a hypothesis-driven fix is OK even when it doesn't close
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:103:the bug, **provided** the change is independently defensible and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:104:documented as partial mitigation. The mistake would have been to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:105:celebrate IV.6 as a B1 fix and stop investigating; the audit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:106:record clearly says "partial — B1 still open".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:107:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:108:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:109:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:110:- [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:111:- [[30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening.md:112:- [[10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge]]

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/core/safety_manager.py; CLAUDE.md "Архитектура safety"; PROJECT_STATUS.md "Аппаратные / рантайм инварианты"; config/safety.yaml
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:7:# Safety FSM
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:9:`SafetyManager` is the single authority for source-on/off decisions in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:10:the engine. Source OFF is the default; running requires continuous
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:11:proof of health.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:13:## Problem solved
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:14:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:15:Before `SafetyManager`, source-on/off authority was distributed across
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:16:interlocks, alarm callbacks, and the GUI. Two failure modes followed:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:17:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:18:1. Race conditions between subsystems firing emergency-off in different
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:19:   orders during a fault cascade.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:20:2. No single place where "is it safe to run right now?" had a defensible
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:21:   answer at any moment.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:22:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:23:`SafetyManager` collapses authority into a single FSM, with a single
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:24:asyncio lock (`_cmd_lock`) serializing `_active_sources` mutations
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:25:across REQ clients (GUI subprocess + web dashboard + future operator
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:26:CLI). See `safety_manager.py:130`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:28:## Six-state FSM
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:29:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:30:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:31:SAFE_OFF ──────► READY ──────► RUN_PERMITTED ──────► RUNNING
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:32:   ▲              ▲                                     │
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:33:   │              │                                     │
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:34:   └─ MANUAL_RECOVERY ◄──── FAULT_LATCHED ◄────────────┘
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:35:                  (acknowledge_fault)    (any fault while RUNNING)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:36:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:38:- **SAFE_OFF** — default. No source active. Preconditions may not be met.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:39:- **READY** — preconditions met (Keithley present, critical channels
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:40:  fresh, no active interlock). Operator may request a run.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:41:- **RUN_PERMITTED** — operator requested run; manager has gated through
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:42:  preconditions; source-on transition pending. Active monitoring
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:43:  (stale, rate, heartbeat) is armed in this state.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:44:- **RUNNING** — source active. Active monitoring continues.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:45:- **FAULT_LATCHED** — entered on any fault while RUN_PERMITTED or
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:46:  RUNNING. Source forced OFF. No automatic recovery. Re-entry guard
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:47:  returns early on concurrent `_fault()` calls (added 2026-04-17).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:48:- **MANUAL_RECOVERY** — entered after `acknowledge_fault()`. Returns to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:49:  READY when preconditions restore.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:50:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:51:`request_run()` may skip `READY` and go directly toward `RUNNING`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:52:when all preconditions already hold, but it still records the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:53:`RUN_PERMITTED` step on the way (`safety_manager.py:314-321 → 366-370`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:54:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:55:## Fail-on-silence
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:56:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:57:`SafetyManager` consumes the `SafetyBroker` (dedicated channel,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:58:`overflow=FAULT`). Stale data — no reading on a critical channel for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:59:`stale_timeout_s` (default 10 s) — fires `_fault("stale data")`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:60:Active monitoring runs in **`RUN_PERMITTED` and `RUNNING`** (not
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:61:`RUNNING` only) so a stuck `start_source()` cannot silently disable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:62:the checks (`safety_manager.py:884`). Outside those two states stale
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:63:data blocks readiness via preconditions, not via fault.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:64:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:65:## Rate limit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:66:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:67:`dT/dt` is computed by [[_meta/glossary#FSM|`RateEstimator`]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:68:(`window_s=120.0`, `min_points=60` after Phase 2c CC I.3). When any
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:69:critical-channel rate exceeds `max_dT_dt_K_per_min` (default 5 K/min,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:70:configured in `safety.yaml`), `_fault()` fires.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:71:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:72:> Note: 5 K/min is the **configurable default**, not a hard-coded
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:73:> invariant — production tuning is allowed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:74:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:75:`min_points=60` over the `window_s=120 s` window means at least 60
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:76:critical-channel readings must have accumulated within the last 2 min
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:77:before the rate estimator returns a non-`None` rate; under the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:78:default 2 s LakeShore poll cadence (`config/instruments.yaml`) that's
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:79:~120 s of arrivals, matching the window length.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:81:## Crash-recovery guard at Keithley connect
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:82:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:83:`Keithley2604B.connect()` forces `OUTPUT_OFF` on both SMU channels
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:84:before assuming control. Best-effort: if force-OFF fails, logs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:85:CRITICAL and continues (no crash, but the safety guarantee weakens).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:86:Documented in [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:87:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:88:## Cancellation shielding
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:89:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:90:Phase 2d added `asyncio.shield()` around the post-fault sequence:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:91:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:92:- `emergency_off`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:93:- `fault_log_callback` (ordered BEFORE optional broker publish — Jules
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:94:  R2 fix; callback after publish would have allowed an escape path)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:95:- `_ensure_output_off` in `_safe_off`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:96:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:97:These survive task cancellation during a fault cascade, so a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:98:post-mortem log entry is always written even if the engine is being
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:99:torn down.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:100:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:101:## Re-entry guard
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:102:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:103:A `_fault()` re-entry guard added 2026-04-17 (`A1 safety audit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:104:eb267c4`):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:105:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:106:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:107:if self._state == SafetyState.FAULT_LATCHED:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:108:    return  # already faulted; do not overwrite reason or duplicate events
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:109:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:110:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:111:Prevents concurrent `_fault()` calls (e.g. multiple critical-channel
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:112:violations in the same poll cycle) from clobbering `_fault_reason`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:113:emitting duplicate events, or re-firing `emergency_off`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:114:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:115:## Fail-closed config
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:116:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:117:`load_config()` raises `SafetyConfigError` (distinct class for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:118:clean-exit recognition) when:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:119:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:120:- `safety.yaml` not found at the configured path
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:121:- file is malformed (not a mapping)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:122:- `critical_channels` is missing, not a list, or empty
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:123:- any pattern fails to compile
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:124:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:125:Engine exits with code 2; no auto-restart. Same fail-closed pattern
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:126:applies to `alarms_v3.yaml`, `interlocks.yaml`, `housekeeping.yaml`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:127:`channels.yaml`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:128:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:129:## What is NOT here
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:130:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:131:- **No firmware TSP watchdog.** P=const regulation is host-side in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:132:  `keithley_2604b.py`; the `tsp/p_const.lua` draft is **not loaded**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:133:  on the device. Phase 3 plan, requires hardware verification.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:134:- **No GUI-side authority.** GUI is read-only with respect to safety
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:135:  state. Operator commands flow as REQ → REP and the FSM is the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:136:  single decision point.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:137:- **No automatic recovery from FAULT_LATCHED.** Operator
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:138:  `acknowledge_fault()` is required.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:139:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:140:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:141:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:142:- [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] — readings reach SafetyBroker
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:143:  AFTER SQLite commit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:144:- [[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]] — separate from safety FSM;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:145:  operator-attention level, not source-control authority
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:146:- [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]] — crash-recovery guard implementation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:147:- [[40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant]] — same
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:148:  fail-closed-by-default ethos
