/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/analytics/base_plugin.py; analytics/plugin_loader.py history; CLAUDE.md "Аналитика"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:4:status: closed — rebuilt with isolation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:7:# Plugin isolation rebuild
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:9:A small story about why an architectural change was needed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:10:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:11:## Original architecture
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:13:Plugins were direct imports — modules in `analytics/` imported and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:14:called from the engine's hot path. A plugin's `process()` ran inline
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:15:on the engine event loop. Configuration was via `config/plugins.yaml`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:16:to flag-on individual modules.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:17:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:18:## Failure mode
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:19:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:20:A plugin throwing an exception inside `process()` propagated up the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:21:asyncio call chain, killed the scheduler task, and **crashed the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:22:engine** as a side effect. Single buggy plugin → entire data
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:23:acquisition stops.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:24:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:25:This is bad for a system whose value proposition is "long-running,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:26:reliable, no-touch acquisition during multi-day experiments".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:28:## Rebuild
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:29:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:30:Two changes landed together:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:31:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:32:1. **`AnalyticsPlugin` ABC** (`analytics/base_plugin.py`). Defines
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:33:   the contract: `process(readings) -> list[DerivedMetric]`. Plugin
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:34:   id is set in `__init__`. Optional `configure(config)` for YAML
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:35:   parameter ingestion.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:36:2. **`PluginPipeline`** (`analytics/plugin_loader.py`). Owns the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:37:   plugin lifecycle:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:38:   - Loads `*.py` files dynamically via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:39:     `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` + `exec_module`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:40:   - Subscribes once to `DataBroker` as `plugin_pipeline`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:41:   - Per-batch try/except around each plugin's `process()` —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:42:     **plugin exceptions logged and dropped, never propagated**.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:43:   - Hot reload via 5 s mtime polling (`_watch_loop`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:44:   - File-load failures log + skip; one bad file does not poison
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:45:     the pipeline.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:46:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:47:## Net effect
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:48:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:49:- Engine survives a plugin throwing.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:50:- Failed plugin's batches are skipped; other plugins keep running.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:51:- `numpy`/`scipy` allowed inside plugins (they live outside `core/`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:52:  and `drivers/`); the no-numpy rule for those layers stays.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:53:- `DerivedMetric.now(...)` factory provides a uniform timestamping
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:54:  pattern across plugins.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:55:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:56:## Surface area for plugin authors
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:58:A minimal compliant plugin is ~10 lines:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:59:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:60:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:61:class MyPlugin(AnalyticsPlugin):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:62:    async def process(self, readings):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:63:        if not readings:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:64:            return []
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:65:        return [DerivedMetric.now(self.plugin_id, "metric", value, "unit")]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:66:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:67:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:68:The pipeline gives the plugin a fresh batch every
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:69:`batch_interval_s` (default 1 s), capped at `_MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 500`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:70:readings. Returned metrics are republished into `DataBroker` as
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:71:synthetic `Reading`s; live broker subscribers (GUI, web, ZMQ, alarm
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:72:subscribers) treat them uniformly with raw readings. SQLite
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:73:persistence of plugin output is **not** wired in the current code
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:74:surface — `SQLiteWriter` runs in immediate mode from `Scheduler`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:75:not as a broker subscriber.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:76:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:77:## Where it lives now
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:78:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:79:- ABC: `analytics/base_plugin.py`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:80:- Pipeline: `analytics/plugin_loader.py`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:81:- Production plugin examples: `analytics/sensor_diagnostics.py`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:82:  `analytics/vacuum_trend.py`. (Cooldown predictor lives outside
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:83:  the plugin pipeline because it needs orchestration with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:84:  `core/cooldown_service.py`.)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:85:- Configuration: `config/plugins.yaml` controls which plugins are
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:86:  enabled; `<plugin_id>.yaml` siblings carry plugin-specific config.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:87:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:88:## Lesson
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:89:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:90:Engine survival is non-negotiable. Any extension point that lets a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:91:third party (operator-authored plugin) run code in the hot path
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:92:must isolate exceptions explicitly — Python's default exception
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:93:flow is to crash the loop, and that is the wrong default for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:94:acquisition systems.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:95:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:96:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:97:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:98:- [[10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture]] — full subsystem page
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:99:- [[40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC]] — synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:100:  ADR for this rebuild
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/core/alarm_v2.py; core/alarm_config.py; config/alarms_v3.yaml; CHANGELOG.md "alarm_v2.py:252 cooldown_stall"; CLAUDE.md alarm section
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:7:# Alarm engine v2
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:9:YAML-driven, phase-aware alarm engine. Replaces v1's hardcoded
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:10:threshold-with-hysteresis logic.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:12:## Components
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:14:`core/alarm_v2.py` defines:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:15:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:16:- **`AlarmEvent`** — frozen dataclass: `alarm_id`, `level`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:17:  `message`, `triggered_at`, `channels`, `values`, `acknowledged`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:18:- **`AlarmTransition` = TRIGGERED | CLEARED`** — typed literal.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:19:- **`PhaseProvider` / `SetpointProvider`** — duck-typed protocols
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:20:  feeding the evaluator.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:21:- **`AlarmEvaluator`** — pure function from `(alarm_id, alarm_config)`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:22:  to `AlarmEvent | None`, dispatching by `alarm_type`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:23:- **`AlarmStateManager`** — manages active/cleared transitions,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:24:  hysteresis, deduplication.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:25:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:26:## Alarm types
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:28:| `alarm_type` | Signal | Where used |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:29:|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:30:| `threshold` | scalar `value > threshold` (or `<`) | static safety/operational limits |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:31:| `composite` | AND/OR of sub-conditions, each typed | physically-motivated multi-channel rules |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:32:| `rate` | `dX/dt` over a configurable window via `RateEstimator` | trend-based anomaly (cooldown stall, runaway warming) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:33:| `stale` | last reading age > threshold | sensor / link health |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:34:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:35:Composite sub-conditions support `above`, `below`, `rate_above`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:36:`rate_below`, `rate_near_zero`, plus `phase_elapsed_s` as a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:37:synthetic channel for time-based gates within an experiment phase.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:38:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:39:## Phase-aware
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:40:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:41:`PhaseProvider.get_current_phase()` and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:42:`get_phase_elapsed_s()` let alarm rules be active only during
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:43:specific experiment phases. Example: `cooldown_stall` is meaningful
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:44:during the cooldown phase but not during disassembly.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:45:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:46:`SetpointProvider` lets thresholds be parameterized by experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:47:template (e.g. T_target = 4.2 K vs 77 K) so the same rule fires for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:48:the right physical regime per experiment.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:49:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:50:## Configuration
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:51:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:52:`config/alarms_v3.yaml` declares all alarms. `core/alarm_config.py`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:53:validates and parses. Fail-closed: malformed `alarms_v3.yaml` → engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:54:exit code 2.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:55:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:56:`src/cryodaq/core/alarm_providers.py` ships concrete `PhaseProvider`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:57:and `SetpointProvider` wired to `ExperimentManager`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:59:## Cyrillic Т homoglyph regression
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:60:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:61:`config/alarms_v3.yaml` previously contained a Latin "T" where
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:62:Cyrillic "Т" (U+0422) was meant. Runtime tests passed because the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:63:alarm logic was correct; the channels just never matched anything.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:64:Caught by adversarial LLM review (Codex). Regression test added.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:65:Full story: [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:66:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:67:`docs/design-system/` rules (RULE-COLOR / RULE-TYPO) carry similar
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:68:Latin/Cyrillic invariants. Mention in MANIFEST.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:69:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:70:## Channel groups (current `alarms_v3.yaml`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:71:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:72:`uncalibrated` and `all_temp` groups **explicitly exclude** Т4
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:73:(Радиатор 2) and Т8 (Калибровка) — both physically disconnected on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:74:the production lab PC. Comment on `alarms_v3.yaml:32` reads
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:75:"Т4 (Радиатор 2), Т8 (Калибровка) — отключённые датчики, исключены".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:76:The exclusion is in master, not just per-machine; future deploys with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:77:Т4/Т8 connected need to add them back manually.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:78:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:79:## Known issue (open)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:81:`alarm_v2.py:252` raises `KeyError: 'threshold'` when evaluating the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:82:`cooldown_stall` composite alarm: one sub-condition is missing a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:83:required `threshold` field. Log spam every ~2 s; engine does not
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:84:crash. Fix candidates per ROADMAP:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:85:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:86:- Config-side: audit `config/alarms_v3.yaml` to add the missing
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:87:  threshold (or change the offending condition's type if `threshold`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:88:  is genuinely spurious for that condition shape).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:89:- Code-side: defensive `cond.get("threshold")` access in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:90:  `_eval_condition`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:91:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:92:Pending Codex-04 patch (overnight swarm 2026-04-24 stream).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:93:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:94:## Relationship to safety
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:95:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:96:The alarm engine is for **operator attention**, not source-control
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:97:authority. `SafetyManager` is the only thing that turns sources on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:98:or off. An alarm at CRITICAL level may trigger an interlock action
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:99:configured separately in `config/interlocks.yaml`, but the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:100:authority to act is still `SafetyManager`'s. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:101:[[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:102:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:103:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:104:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:105:- [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:106:- [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:107:- [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] — F10 (sensor diagnostics → alarm)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:2:source: docs/bug_B1_zmq_idle_death_handoff.md "Codex revised analysis"; CHANGELOG.md `be51a24` IV.6 partial mitigation; ROADMAP.md "IV.6 partial mitigation outcome"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:4:status: closed — adversarial review counter-example
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:7:# Codex H2 wrong hypothesis
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:9:A counter-example to "Codex high reasoning is right" worth keeping
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:10:visible. Pairs with [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:11:as a reminder that **tests > model confidence**.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:13:## Setup
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:14:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:15:By 2026-04-20 afternoon, B1 had:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:16:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:17:- Reproduced 100 % on macOS, also reproduced on Ubuntu 22.04
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:18:- Resisted a `TCP_KEEPALIVE` fix (commit `f5f9039`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:19:- Architect's H1 ("idle reaping") falsified by Linux's
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:20:  `tcp_keepalive_time = 7200 s` ruling out kernel-level reaping in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:21:  120 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:22:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:23:Architect handed the evidence dossier
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:24:(`docs/bug_B1_zmq_idle_death_handoff.md`) to Codex gpt-5.5/high
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:25:asking for a revised root cause.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:26:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:27:## What Codex said (H2)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:28:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:29:Codex's revised root cause:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:30:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:31:> The GUI-side subprocess command plane uses one long-lived REQ
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:32:> socket in `cmd_forward_loop()`. On both platforms, something
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:33:> eventually pushes that single connection into a bad state — on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:34:> macOS sparse cadence triggers it faster, on Ubuntu it appears
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:35:> connection-age or socket-state related rather than idle-related.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:36:> Once one REQ connection goes bad, the entire command plane
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:37:> degrades because every GUI command shares it.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:38:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:39:Cited specific file:line refs to `_new_req_socket()` in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:40:`zmq_subprocess.py`. Pointed to ZeroMQ Guide ch. 4 canonical
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:41:"poll / timeout / close / reopen" pattern. Recommended per-command
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:42:ephemeral REQ socket as the fix. Tone confident; reasoning clean.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:43:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:44:## What we did
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:45:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:46:IV.6 batch (`be51a24`) implemented the fix exactly as Codex
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:47:proposed:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:48:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:49:- Per-command ephemeral REQ socket in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:50:  `zmq_subprocess.cmd_forward_loop`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:51:- `REQ_RELAXED` and `REQ_CORRELATE` removed (unnecessary with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:52:  ephemeral sockets).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:53:- Launcher-side `command_channel_stalled()` watchdog in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:54:  `_poll_bridge_data`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:55:- `TCP_KEEPALIVE` reverted on command + PUB paths (kept on SUB
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:56:  drain as orthogonal safeguard).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:58:60/60 unit tests green; full subtree 1775/1776 (1 unrelated flaky).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:59:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:60:## What happened
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:61:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:62:Post-fix diag runs reproduced B1 with structurally identical
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:63:timing to pre-fix master:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:64:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:65:| Test | Result |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:66:|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:67:| `diag_zmq_idle_hypothesis.py` SPARSE_0.33HZ | cmd #8 FAIL at uptime 56 s (pre-fix: cmd #10 FAIL at ~30 s) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:68:| `diag_zmq_bridge_extended.py` | cmd #48 FAIL at uptime 82 s, 0/3 recovery (pre-fix: cmd #28 FAIL at 92 s) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:69:| RAPID_5HZ path | 295/295 OK — same rate-dependence pattern as before |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:70:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:71:Removing shared REQ state did NOT eliminate the failure. **H2
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:72:falsified.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:73:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:74:## Why this is the lesson
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:75:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:76:Codex's reasoning was clean. The hypothesis was internally
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:77:consistent with all data given to it. The fix was *good* — it
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:78:implements the canonical reliable req-reply pattern, removes a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:79:brittle point in the design, and gives the launcher a real
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:80:command-channel watchdog. IV.6 stays in master as defense-in-depth.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:81:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:82:But the bug remained. The model was confidently wrong in a way
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:83:that only **diagnostic re-execution** could detect — not by another
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:84:model's reasoning, not by closer reading of the same file:line refs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:85:Codex cited.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:86:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:87:The lesson is not "don't use Codex". The lesson is:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:88:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:89:> Confidence in a model's review does not substitute for re-running
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:90:> the failing test after applying the fix. **Tests > model confidence**,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:91:> always, with no exceptions for "the reasoning looks airtight".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:92:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:93:## Where this applies in workflow
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:94:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:95:- [[50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract]] §4 (consultant invocation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:96:  protocol): synthesis must include "what experiment would
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:97:  distinguish the proposed root cause from alternatives", and CC
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:98:  must run that experiment after applying the fix.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:99:- [[50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation]] (skill): Codex's
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:100:  signature signals (FAIL/PASS verdict, file:line refs, terse
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:101:  critiques) are markers of *form*, not of *correctness*.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:102:- [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution]] §"Q4 equivalence
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:103:  check": post-merge adversarial review on R1 was specifically run
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:104:  *after* the fix landed, partly as discipline for not repeating
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:105:  this pattern.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:106:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:107:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:108:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:109:- [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]] — bug is still open
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:110:- [[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]] — what shipped from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:111:  H2 and why it stayed
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:112:- [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]] — sister
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis.md:113:  story about tests passing on a thing that didn't work

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/analytics/calibration.py; analytics/calibration_fitter.py; core/calibration_acquisition.py; CLAUDE.md "Снимок сверки"; README.md "Calibration v2"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:7:# Calibration v2
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:9:Three-mode calibration pipeline introduced as the v2 redesign of the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:10:LakeShore sensor calibration workflow.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:12:## Three-mode flow
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:14:| Mode | Surface | Action |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:15:|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:16:| **Setup** | GUI: `cryodaq-gui` Calibration tab | Operator picks reference channel, target channels, optional context. Engine starts the experiment with `calibration_acquisition: true`. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:17:| **Acquisition** | Engine: `CalibrationAcquisitionService` | Continuous SRDG (`Sensor Raw Data Grabbing`) capture during the experiment, alongside KRDG (calibrated kelvin readings). One transaction per poll cycle. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:18:| **Results** | Engine + GUI: `CalibrationFitter` post-run pipeline | Extract → downsample → breakpoints → Chebyshev fit → export. Operator reviews and applies. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:19:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:20:## Continuous SRDG acquisition
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:21:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:22:`CalibrationAcquisitionService` (`core/calibration_acquisition.py`) is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:23:activated by experiments with `calibration_acquisition: true`. The
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:24:scheduler calls `prepare_srdg_readings(krdg, srdg)` after each
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:25:LakeShore poll; the writer commits both KRDG and SRDG in one
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:26:transaction; `on_srdg_persisted(pending_state)` then applies the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:27:in-memory `t_min`/`t_max` updates AFTER the commit.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:28:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:29:The deferral matters: state mutation BEFORE persistence would let a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:30:crash leave `t_min`/`t_max` diverged from what's actually in SQLite.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:31:See [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] (Phase 2d invariant 15).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:32:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:33:`reference_channel` and `target_channels` are canonicalized through
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:34:`ChannelManager.resolve_channel_reference()` so per-rack semantics
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:35:("rack 1 sensor 3") resolve to the canonical Cyrillic Т-prefixed
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:36:names regardless of how the operator typed it.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:38:## Post-run pipeline
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:40:`CalibrationFitter` (`analytics/calibration_fitter.py`):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:42:1. **Extract.** Read SQLite over the experiment time range; pair each
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:43:   SRDG raw value with its time-aligned KRDG reference temperature
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:44:   (default `max_time_delta_s=2.0`). Returns
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:45:   `list[tuple[sensor_raw, reference_T_K]]`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:46:2. **Downsample.** Reduce dense pairs to representative pairs while
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:47:   preserving curve shape. Density along T axis matters more than
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:48:   along time axis.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:49:3. **Breakpoints.** `generate_breakpoints()` produces a sampled
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:50:   breakpoint list intended for export / inspection (e.g. preview of
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:51:   what the LakeShore `.340` table will look like). The actual
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:52:   Chebyshev zone boundaries are detected internally by the fitter
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:53:   (`CalibrationStore.fit_curve()` at `analytics/calibration.py`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:54:4. **Fit.** `CalibrationStore.fit_curve()` uses
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:55:   `numpy.polynomial.chebyshev` per zone; produces a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:56:   `CalibrationCurve` with coefficients per zone, fit metrics,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:57:   source session IDs, metadata.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:59:Result is a `CalibrationFitResult` with raw, downsampled, and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:60:breakpoint pair lists kept around for plotting / inspection.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:61:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:62:## Output formats
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:63:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:64:Exports go through `CalibrationStore` methods:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:65:`export_curve_json()`, `export_curve_table()` (CSV), `export_curve_330()`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:66:`export_curve_340()`. Targets:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:67:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:68:- **`.330`** — LakeShore controller calibration table format,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:69:  breakpoint-based. Contains sampled curve breakpoints, NOT polynomial
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:70:  coefficients. Older format.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:71:- **`.340`** — LakeShore newer breakpoint format, same family as
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:72:  `.330`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:73:- **JSON** — CryoDAQ-internal format with Chebyshev coefficients per
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:74:  zone, metrics, source session IDs, metadata. Source-of-truth
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:75:  artifact.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:76:- **CSV** — round-trip with external tooling via `export_curve_table()`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:77:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:78:A `.cof` raw-Chebyshev-coefficient export format was discussed
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:79:(architect note 2026-04-25) but is **not in the current code surface**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:80:— if and when it lands, this note must be updated.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:81:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:82:## Runtime apply policy
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:83:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:84:Calibration v2 supports per-channel runtime routing in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:85:`LakeShore218S` (`drivers/instruments/lakeshore_218s.py`):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:86:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:87:- **Global mode `on/off`** — enable / disable runtime calibration
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:88:  application across all LakeShore channels at once.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:89:- **Per-channel policy `inherit | off | on`** — overrides the global
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:90:  mode. `inherit` follows global; `off`/`on` pin per-channel behavior.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:91:- **Conservative fallback** — at runtime, if curve / assignment /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:92:  SRDG missing OR computation throws, the driver falls back to KRDG
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:93:  (calibrated reading from the LakeShore controller itself) and logs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:94:  the reason once per `(channel_key, reason)` to avoid log spam
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:95:  (`_runtime_warning_cache`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:96:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:97:Policy + fallback together mean a malformed curve never silently
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:98:breaks an experiment — operator sees KRDG, behavior degrades to v1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:99:semantics, log line tells them what happened.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:100:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:101:## Lab-verification gap
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:102:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:103:Per `README.md` "Известные ограничения": runtime apply policy is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:104:implemented and tested in mock; **per-channel behavior on the live
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:105:LakeShore requires separate lab verification** before being trusted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:106:as the active calibration in production runs.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:107:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:108:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:109:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:110:- [[20 Drivers/LakeShore 218S]] — driver runtime routing impl
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:111:- [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] — KRDG+SRDG one-transaction rule
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:112:- [[_meta/glossary]] — KRDG, SRDG definitions
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:113:- [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] — F8 (cooldown ML), F9 (TIM auto-report)

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:2:source: docs/bug_B1_zmq_idle_death_handoff.md; ROADMAP.md "Known broken" (B1); CHANGELOG.md (Unreleased Apr 20); decisions docs 2026-04-24
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:4:status: synthesized — bug OPEN
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:7:# B1 — ZMQ command channel idle-death
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:9:The single bug that blocks `v0.34.0` tag.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:10:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:11:## Symptom
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:13:The GUI ↔ engine command plane (REQ on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:14:`tcp://127.0.0.1:5556`, or `ipc://` since IV.7) stops responding
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:15:after some time of operation. Data plane (SUB on 5555 or
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:16:`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-pub.sock`) keeps publishing readings normally.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:17:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:18:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:19:RAPID 5Hz   (200ms idle): 291/291 OK, 0 fails    — high rate never fails
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:20:SPARSE 0.33Hz (3s idle):    9 OK, cmd #10 FAIL 35s — low rate fails
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:21:RECOVER 5Hz, post-fail:     cmd #1 FAIL immediately — permanent
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:22:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:23:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:24:Once a REQ socket has timed out once, fresh REQ sockets recreated
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:25:into the same context **also hang**. Engine REP itself answers a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:26:direct in-process Python client in 0.001 s while the bridge
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:27:subprocess is deep into B1 — engine is not wedged.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:28:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:29:| Platform | First failure |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:30:|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:31:| macOS Darwin (Python 3.14.3, pyzmq 25.x) | stochastic 4–92 s uptime |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:32:| Ubuntu 22.04 (Python 3.12.13, pyzmq 26.4.0) | exactly 120 s after subprocess start (single data point, may vary) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:33:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:34:Real Ubuntu lab PC reproduces in `./start.sh`, not just diag tools.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:36:## Hypothesis tree
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:38:| H | Hypothesis | Evidence | Status |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:39:|---|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:40:| H1 | macOS kernel idle reaping of loopback TCP | `tcp_keepalive_time = 7200s` on Linux, yet 120 s deterministic failure | **falsified 2026-04-20** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:41:| H2 | Single long-lived REQ socket accumulating bad state (Codex's primary) | IV.6 implemented per-cmd ephemeral REQ; B1 still reproduces | **falsified 2026-04-20** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:42:| H3 | b2b4fb5 hardening probe incompatible with `ipc://` | repro on revert + bind-time race confirmed | **partially confirmed** — explains 2026-04-23 IV.7 misattribution but does NOT explain B1 itself; B1 still fires after ~80 s on ipc:// |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:43:| H4 | pyzmq 25.x + Python 3.14 asyncio integration / engine REP task state under rapid REQ connect-disconnect churn | not yet tested in isolation | **untested** — D2 split-context falsification experiment scheduled |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:44:| H5 | engine REP state machine | not yet specifically probed | **untested** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:45:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:46:## What works
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:47:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:48:- Engine REP socket itself — direct Python REQ client gets 0.001 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:49:  reply mid-failure.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:50:- Engine internals: SQLite writes (~9000/10 min), heartbeats every
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:51:  30 s, plugin ticks, scheduler all stay healthy.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:52:- Raw subprocess without `ZmqBridge` wrapping (`diag_zmq_subprocess.py`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:53:  succeeds short-term on both `fork` and `spawn` mp start methods.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:54:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:55:## What's been tried
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:56:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:57:1. **`TCP_KEEPALIVE` on all four sockets** (commit `f5f9039`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:58:   reverted on cmd path in IV.6). Partial delay of failure on macOS
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:59:   only; Linux unaffected. Reverted on command + PUB paths since H1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:60:   was falsified; kept on `sub_drain_loop` SUB as orthogonal
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:61:   safeguard.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:62:2. **IV.6 ephemeral REQ + cmd-channel watchdog** (`be51a24`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:63:   Codex's H2 hypothesis. Implements ZeroMQ Guide ch.4 canonical
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:64:   "poll / timeout / close / reopen" pattern. **Did not fix B1.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:65:3. **IV.6 watchdog cooldown hotfix** — solves restart-storm
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:66:   regression (30–40 restarts/min on Ubuntu) but is not a B1 fix;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:67:   produces "works ~60–120 s, one restart, works again" cycle as
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:68:   workaround.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:69:4. **IV.7 ipc:// transport experiment.** Branch
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:70:   `experiment/iv7-ipc-transport`. Switches loopback default to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:71:   Unix-domain sockets on macOS / Linux. ipc:// runs normally for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:72:   ~80 s then B1 fires anyway — not a transport-layer fix, but
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:73:   useful as a diagnostic constant.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:74:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:75:## Diagnostics in tree
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:76:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:77:`tools/diag_zmq_*.py` retained for regression after fix:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:78:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:79:- `tools/diag_zmq_subprocess.py` — subprocess alone, short.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:80:- `tools/diag_zmq_bridge.py` — full ZmqBridge 60 s soak.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:81:- `tools/diag_zmq_bridge_extended.py` — 180 s past-first-failure.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:82:- `tools/diag_zmq_idle_hypothesis.py` — rate-dependence reproducer.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:83:- `tools/diag_zmq_b1_capture.py` — canonical B1 capture CLI
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:84:  (post-D4a, post-R1).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:85:- `tools/_b1_diagnostics.py` — reusable helpers (bridge_snapshot,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:86:  direct_engine_probe).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:87:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:88:`/tmp/diag_iv6_*.log` and `/tmp/engine_iv6_debug.log` preserved for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:89:architect review.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:90:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:91:## Why this still matters
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:92:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:93:`v0.34.0` cannot ship while B1 reproduces — the user-visible
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:94:symptom is "GUI buttons don't work after a couple of minutes; have
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:95:to restart the bridge subprocess". The IV.6 watchdog cooldown
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:96:provides a usable workaround (60–120 s cycle with single restart),
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:97:not an acceptable production state.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:98:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:99:## Next experiments
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:100:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:101:- **D2 — H4 split-context falsification** on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:102:  `experiment/iv7-ipc-transport` worktree.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:103:- If H4 / H5 both falsified: target may be lower (libzmq 4.x
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:104:  loopback handling, kernel-level interaction). Would justify a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:105:  switch to a different IPC mechanism entirely (mp.Pipe, UNIX
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:106:  socket without zmq, etc.).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:107:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:108:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:109:
