/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/drivers/instruments/thyracont_vsp63d.py; drivers/transport/serial.py; CLAUDE.md "Драйверы"; CHANGELOG aabd75f validate_checksum; CHANGELOG 2026-04-20 ModemManager
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:7:# Thyracont VSP63D
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:9:Vacuum gauge. Single pressure channel.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:10:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:11:## Interface and channels
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:13:- **Transport:** RS-232 / USB-Serial (`drivers/transport/serial.py`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:14:  via `pyserial-asyncio`). Default 9600 baud.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:15:- **Default poll interval:** 2.0 s.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:16:- **Channels:** 1 — pressure (mbar).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:17:- **Driver class:** `ThyracontVSP63D` — also handles VSM77DL and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:18:  similar V1/V2-protocol siblings.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:19:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:20:## Two-protocol auto-detection
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:21:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:22:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:23:VSP63D (default):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:24:  9600 baud
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:25:  command:  "MV00\r"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:26:  response: "status,value\r"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:28:Thyracont Protocol V1 (VSM77DL family):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:29:  115200 baud
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:30:  command:  "<addr>M^\r"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:31:  response: "<addr>M<6digits><checksum>\r"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:32:  6-digit value ABCDEF: pressure = (ABCD/1000) × 10^(EF − 20) mbar
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:33:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:34:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:35:`connect()` probes both. `_FALLBACK_BAUDRATES = {9600: 115200,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:36:115200: 9600}` controls the fallback once per attempt. Actual probe
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:37:order in `thyracont_vsp63d.py:120`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:38:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:39:1. Try Protocol V1 probe (`_try_v1_probe`) at the configured baudrate.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:40:2. If fail, try MV00 probe (`_try_mv00_probe`) at the configured baudrate.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:41:3. If both fail, close transport and reopen at the alternate baudrate
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:42:   (per `_FALLBACK_BAUDRATES`); repeat the V1-then-MV00 sequence.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:43:4. If still nothing responds, raise.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:44:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:45:## Status map
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:46:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:47:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:48:_STATUS_OK         = 0  → ChannelStatus.OK
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:49:_STATUS_UNDERRANGE = 1  → ChannelStatus.UNDERRANGE
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:50:_STATUS_OVERRANGE  = 2  → ChannelStatus.OVERRANGE
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:51:_STATUS_SENSOR_ERROR = 3 → ChannelStatus.SENSOR_ERROR
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:52:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:53:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:54:OVERRANGE / UNDERRANGE persist as `±inf` per Phase 2d invariant 10
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:55:(see [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]]).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:56:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:57:## V1 checksum quirk
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:59:V1 protocol response contains a 1-character checksum at the end.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:60:`_verify_v1_checksum(response)` validates; on checksum failure the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:61:driver returns a `Reading` with `value=NaN`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:62:`status=ChannelStatus.SENSOR_ERROR`, and checksum-failure metadata
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:63:attached, rather than parsing partial garbage. The reading object
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:64:still flows downstream so the safety / alarm layer can react.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:65:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:66:The checksum formula differs slightly between VSP63D and VSP206
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:67:hardware (lab PC has VSP206). `validate_checksum: false` in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:68:`config/instruments.local.yaml` (Ubuntu lab PC) bypasses this check.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:69:The YAML key was previously **silently ignored** by the engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:70:loader — driver defaulted to `True` regardless. Wiring fixed in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:71:`aabd75f` (2026-04-20). Without the fix, the operator could turn
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:72:the toggle off and the engine would still strict-validate.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:73:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:74:## Probe-vs-read inconsistency (open issue)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:75:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:76:Per CHANGELOG "Open / known issues carrying into 0.34.0":
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:77:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:78:> `_try_v1_probe` always succeeds; read checksum-validates. Driver
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:79:> can "connect" and emit NaN forever on non-VSP63D hardware.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:81:Pending hardening fix. Intended approach: V1 probe should read at
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:82:least one full message and validate checksum before reporting
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:83:"connected".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:84:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:85:## Lab-PC-specific operational hazard
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:86:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:87:`ModemManager` (Ubuntu) was transiently grabbing `/dev/ttyUSB0`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:88:when the Thyracont was plugged in. Disabled on the lab PC
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:89:2026-04-20. Documented in CHANGELOG. Future deployments should
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:90:disable `ModemManager` (or systemd-mask it) as part of the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:91:operator-PC bring-up runbook.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:92:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:93:## Mock mode
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:94:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:95:`_MOCK_BASE_PRESSURE_MBAR = 1.5e-6`. Random walk around base value;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:96:realistic enough for [[10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture|`vacuum_trend`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:97:plugin]] testing and GUI plot exercise.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:98:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:99:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:100:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:101:- [[20 Drivers/LakeShore 218S]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:102:- [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:103:- [[10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture]] — vacuum_trend plugin lives
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:104:  here
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:105:- [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] — F13 (vacuum leak rate estimator)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:2:source: CLAUDE.md persistence section; PROJECT_STATUS.md invariant 2; src/cryodaq/core/scheduler.py; src/cryodaq/storage/sqlite_writer.py
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:7:# Persistence-first ordering
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:9:CryoDAQ's most-load-bearing invariant.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:10:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:11:## The invariant
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:13:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:14:InstrumentDriver.read_channels()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:15:  → Scheduler
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:16:     1. SQLiteWriter.write_immediate()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:17:     2. THEN DataBroker.publish_batch()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:18:     3. THEN SafetyBroker.publish_batch()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:19:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:20:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:21:> If `DataBroker` has a reading, it has already been written to SQLite.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:22:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:23:The same property holds for `SafetyBroker`. Engine subscribers see a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:24:reading **strictly after** that reading has committed to durable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:25:storage.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:26:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:27:## Why it matters
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:28:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:29:Cryogenic experiments are long (days–weeks) and expensive. After any
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:30:unexpected engine termination (crash, SIGKILL, power loss) the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:31:post-mortem reconstruction MUST be able to answer "what did the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:32:operator's GUI see at time T?". If the broker had a reading the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:33:SQLite did not, the operator's last-observed state diverges from the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:34:durable record — that's the failure mode this invariant rules out.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:36:## Implementation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:38:`Scheduler._process_readings()` calls writer first, then publishers.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:39:The order is unconditional and not switchable — there is no
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:40:"performance mode" override.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:42:`SQLiteWriter` uses WAL mode. Engine startup verifies WAL is actually
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:43:on:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:44:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:45:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:46:if pragma_journal_mode != 'wal':
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:47:    raise RuntimeError("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL did not apply")
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:48:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:49:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:50:(Phase 2d invariant 14 — "WAL mode verification".)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:51:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:52:> **Durability caveat.** SQLite uses `synchronous=NORMAL` by default
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:53:> in WAL mode, which can lose roughly the last ~1 s of writes on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:54:> abrupt power loss (see source comments at
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:55:> `src/cryodaq/storage/sqlite_writer.py:256`). Production lab
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:56:> deployment expects either UPS coverage or `CRYODAQ_SQLITE_SYNC=FULL`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:57:> environment override; see Phase 2d C.3 deferral
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:58:> ([[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] / `PROJECT_STATUS.md`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:59:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:60:## GUI latency tradeoff
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:61:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:62:The cost is the SQLite `INSERT` time per poll cycle on the engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:63:loop's hot path. Measured cost: a few ms per batch under WAL on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:64:spinning disk; sub-ms on SSD. Operator-perceived latency is the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:65:LakeShore poll interval (2 s) anyway, so the SQLite write does not
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:66:dominate.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:67:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:68:GUI-side caching mitigates per-tick latency: the dashboard widgets
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:69:hold local rolling history (`channel_buffer.py`). But all that data
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:70:ultimately came from the broker, which came from a committed write.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:71:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:72:## Crash recovery and atomic sidecars
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:73:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:74:Persistence-first is part of a wider fail-closed pattern (Phase 2d
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:75:invariants):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:76:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:77:- **Atomic file writes** (`core/atomic_write.py`) for experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:78:  metadata sidecars and calibration index/curve files. `os.replace()`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:79:  is the durability primitive.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:80:- **Calibration KRDG+SRDG persist in one transaction per poll cycle.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:81:  State mutation deferred to `on_srdg_persisted` so a write failure
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:82:  does not leave `t_min`/`t_max` diverged from the actual persisted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:83:  data (Jules Round 2 Q3). See [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:84:- **Cancellation shielding** on post-fault paths (Phase 2d):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:85:  `emergency_off`, `fault_log_callback` (BEFORE optional broker
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:86:  publish), `_ensure_output_off` in `_safe_off`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:87:- **OVERRANGE / UNDERRANGE persist** as `±inf` in REAL SQLite columns;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:88:  NaN-valued statuses (SENSOR_ERROR / TIMEOUT) are filtered out
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:89:  before write because SQLite treats NaN as NULL (Phase 2d
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:90:  Codex finding).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:91:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:92:## Daily rotation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:93:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:94:`SQLiteWriter` rotates to a new `data_YYYY-MM-DD.db` file at midnight.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:95:Old files are read-only with respect to the writer. Replay
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:96:(`storage/replay.py`) reads any of them.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:97:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:98:## What this is NOT
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:99:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:100:- **Not synchronous block.** The publisher tasks return as soon as
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:101:  the writer call returns; they don't wait for any subscriber.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:102:- **Not a transactional barrier across drivers.** Two drivers polling
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:103:  in parallel each enforce ordering individually; there is no
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:104:  cross-driver "all-or-nothing" semantics.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:105:- **Not write-replicated.** A single SQLite per day; backup is via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:106:  external file copy or the optional Parquet cold rotation (F17 in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:107:  [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]]).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:108:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:109:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:110:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:111:- [[40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant]] — the synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:112:  ADR for this rule
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:113:- [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]] — cancellation shielding details
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:114:- [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]] — KRDG+SRDG one-transaction rule
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/analytics/base_plugin.py; analytics/plugin_loader.py; config/plugins.yaml
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:7:# Plugin architecture
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:9:Hot-reloadable analytics SDK for derived metrics
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:10:(`R_thermal`, `cooldown_eta_s`, custom user metrics).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:12:## Two-class API
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:14:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:15:class AnalyticsPlugin(ABC):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:16:    plugin_id: str
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:17:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:18:    @abstractmethod
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:19:    async def process(self, readings: list[Reading]) -> list[DerivedMetric]: ...
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:20:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:21:    def configure(self, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:22:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:23:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:24:`DerivedMetric` is a frozen dataclass with `timestamp`, `plugin_id`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:25:`metric`, `value`, `unit`, `metadata`. `DerivedMetric.now(...)` is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:26:a factory that auto-stamps UTC.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:28:A minimal plugin:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:29:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:30:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:31:class MyPlugin(AnalyticsPlugin):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:32:    async def process(self, readings):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:33:        return [DerivedMetric.now(self.plugin_id, "my_metric", 42.0, "arb")]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:34:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:36:## PluginPipeline lifecycle
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:38:`PluginPipeline` (`analytics/plugin_loader.py`):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:40:1. `start()` — subscribe to `DataBroker` as `plugin_pipeline`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:41:   `glob("*.py")` over `plugins_dir`, load each via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:42:   `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` + `exec_module`, find
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:43:   the first concrete `AnalyticsPlugin` subclass, optionally apply
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:44:   YAML config (sibling `<plugin_id>.yaml`), register.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:45:2. `_process_loop` — every `batch_interval_s` (default 1 s), drain
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:46:   the queue (max `_MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 500` readings per batch), call
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:47:   each plugin's `process()`, publish returned `DerivedMetric`s back
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:48:   into the broker as synthetic `Reading`s.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:49:3. `_watch_loop` — polls `_WATCH_INTERVAL_S = 5.0` s on file mtime;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:50:   reloads modified plugins, loads new files, removes deleted ones.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:51:4. `stop()` — cancels both tasks, unsubscribes.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:52:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:53:`PluginPipeline.start()` loads every `*.py` in `plugins_dir`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:54:unconditionally; the `config/plugins.yaml` `enabled` flag is for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:55:shipped analytics modules (sensor_diagnostics, vacuum_trend) wired
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:56:elsewhere, not a gate on filesystem plugin loading.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:58:## Exception isolation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:59:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:60:A plugin throwing inside `process()` is caught at the pipeline
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:61:boundary, logged as ERROR, and that batch is dropped for that plugin
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:62:only. Other plugins keep running. This is the rebuild after the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:63:original direct-import architecture (which would crash the engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:64:when a plugin threw — see [[30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild]]).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:65:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:66:`_load_plugin()` also wraps the entire load attempt in a try/except;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:67:a malformed file logs and is skipped, not failed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:68:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:69:## Hot reload semantics
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:70:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:71:- Add a new `.py` to `plugins/` → loaded within ~5 s.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:72:- Modify a plugin → unload + reload within ~5 s of mtime change.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:73:- Delete a plugin → removed within ~5 s.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:74:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:75:5 s mtime polling is intentional — `inotify`/`FSEvents` would be
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:76:faster but add a platform dependency for marginal benefit.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:77:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:78:## Plugin data flow
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:79:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:80:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:81:DataBroker  ─subscribe─►  PluginPipeline._queue
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:82:                          (asyncio.Queue[Reading])
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:83:                                │
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:84:                                ▼
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:85:                          _process_loop:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:86:                            batch ≤ 500 readings
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:87:                            ├─► plugin1.process()  ──► DerivedMetric[]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:88:                            ├─► plugin2.process()  ──► DerivedMetric[]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:89:                            └─► ...
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:90:                                │
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:91:                                ▼
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:92:                          publish each DerivedMetric
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:93:                          back into DataBroker as Reading
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:94:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:95:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:96:The "publish back as Reading" step means **live broker subscribers**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:97:— GUI, web, ZMQ, alarm, downstream plugins — treat derived metrics
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:98:uniformly with raw readings. Note: `SQLiteWriter` runs in immediate
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:99:mode from `Scheduler` (not as a broker subscriber) so synthetic
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:100:plugin metrics are NOT persisted via this path. Persisting plugin
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:101:output is a future wiring task.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:102:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:103:## Configuration
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:104:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:105:`config/plugins.yaml` carries feature flags for shipped analytics
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:106:modules (independent from filesystem plugin loading):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:107:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:108:- `sensor_diagnostics` — MAD / correlation health scoring
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:109:  (numpy exception per `CLAUDE.md`). `enabled: true` in shipped
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:110:  config.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:111:- `vacuum_trend` — BIC-selected pump-down extrapolation. `enabled: true`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:112:  in shipped config.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:113:- (cooldown predictor lives outside the plugin pipeline since it
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:114:  needs orchestration with `core/cooldown_service.py`.)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:115:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:116:The `plugins_dir` filesystem layer is independent: any `*.py` in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:117:the configured plugins directory is loaded by `PluginPipeline.start()`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:118:on engine launch; operator-authored plugins live there.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:119:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:120:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:121:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:122:- [[20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D]] — produces the pressure stream the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:123:  `vacuum_trend` plugin consumes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:124:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:125:## Related notes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:126:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:127:- [[30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild]] — why exception
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:128:  isolation is non-negotiable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:129:- [[40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC]] — synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:130:  rationale
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture.md:131:- [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] — F16 (plugin SDK + examples)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:2:source: src/cryodaq/core/zmq_bridge.py; src/cryodaq/core/zmq_subprocess.py; docs/bug_B1_zmq_idle_death_handoff.md; CHANGELOG IPC/REP hardening; ROADMAP "Known broken"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:7:# ZMQ bridge
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:9:The ZMQ bridge is the engine ↔ GUI IPC layer. It carries two
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:10:independent channels and lives partly in the engine (PUB / REP
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:11:sockets) and partly in a dedicated GUI-side subprocess that the GUI
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:12:can survive losing.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:14:## Topology
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:15:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:16:| Channel | Pattern | Address | Format | Purpose |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:17:|---|---|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:18:| Data plane | PUB / SUB | `tcp://127.0.0.1:5555` (default; `ipc://` experiment in IV.7) | msgpack | Continuous reading stream + alarm events + safety state |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:19:| Command plane | REP / REQ | `tcp://127.0.0.1:5556` | JSON | Operator commands, status queries |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:20:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:21:Topic for data plane is `b"readings"`. The engine PUBs are owned by
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:22:`ZMQPublisher` + `ZMQCommandServer` (`zmq_bridge.py`). Connection
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:23:order matters: SUB must `connect()` BEFORE `subscribe()`. The
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:24:inverse pattern produced zero received messages on macOS Python 3.14
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:25:+ pyzmq 25 (documented at `zmq_subprocess.py:97`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:26:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:27:## Subprocess isolation (GUI side)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:28:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:29:The GUI process never imports `zmq` directly. Instead, `mp.Process`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:30:spawns `zmq_bridge_main()` (`zmq_subprocess.py:56`), which owns ALL
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:31:ZMQ sockets on the GUI side and communicates with the GUI main process
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:32:through `mp.Queue` (data, cmd, reply). Rationale: libzmq
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:33:`signaler.cpp` assertion crash on Windows takes down only the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:34:subprocess; the GUI itself survives.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:36:Two threads inside the subprocess:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:38:- `sub_drain_loop` — owns SUB socket. Drains readings; emits
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:39:  heartbeat every 5 s into `data_queue` so the GUI can verify the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:40:  *data path* is alive (not just that the subprocess exists).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:41:- `cmd_forward_loop` — owns REQ socket. Pulls commands from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:42:  `cmd_queue`, sends, awaits reply with `RCVTIMEO=35s`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:43:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:44:## Per-command tiered handler timeouts (IV.3 Finding 7)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:45:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:46:Engine REP wraps each command handler in `asyncio.wait_for(timeout=
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:47:_timeout_for(cmd))` (`zmq_bridge.py:60`):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:48:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:49:- Fast envelope **2.0 s** for status polls.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:50:- Slow envelope **30.0 s** for stateful operations
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:51:  (`experiment_finalize`, `experiment_create`, `experiment_abort`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:52:  `calibration_curve_import/export`, `calibration_v2_fit/extract`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:53:  etc.).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:54:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:55:Handlers that exceed the envelope return `{"ok": False, "error":
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:56:"handler timeout"}` rather than silent exception — REP socket
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:57:state-machine sensitivity demands a paired send for every recv.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:59:## Bind retry (Phase 2b H.4)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:60:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:61:`_bind_with_retry()` (`zmq_bridge.py:84`) retries on `EADDRINUSE`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:62:with exponential backoff (`0.5 s → 10 s`, max 10 attempts). On
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:63:Windows a socket from a SIGKILL'd engine can hold the port for up
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:64:to 240 s (TIME_WAIT). Caller must set `LINGER=0` BEFORE calling, or
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:65:`close()` will hold the address even after retry succeeds.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:66:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:67:## IV.6 ephemeral REQ + watchdog (`be51a24`, 2026-04-20)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:68:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:69:Codex's revised root-cause hypothesis for B1 was that one long-lived
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:70:REQ socket in `cmd_forward_loop()` accumulates state and becomes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:71:unrecoverable. IV.6 implemented the canonical ZeroMQ Guide ch.4
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:72:"poll / timeout / close / reopen" pattern:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:73:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:74:- Per-command ephemeral REQ socket (`_new_req_socket()` per send).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:75:- `REQ_RELAXED` and `REQ_CORRELATE` removed (unnecessary with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:76:  ephemeral sockets).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:77:- Launcher-side `command_channel_stalled()` watchdog in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:78:  `_poll_bridge_data` — restarts the bridge subprocess if the cmd
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:79:  plane is stalled while data plane stays alive.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:80:- `TCP_KEEPALIVE` reverted on command + PUB paths (kept on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:81:  `sub_drain_loop` SUB as orthogonal safeguard).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:82:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:83:**Outcome:** code stays in master as defense-in-depth and matches
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:84:canonical reliable req-reply pattern, **but B1 still reproduces** at
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:85:~80 s uptime. Codex's shared-REQ-state hypothesis was empirically
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:86:falsified. See [[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]] and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:87:[[30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:88:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:89:## Watchdog cooldown hotfix (2026-04-20 evening)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:90:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:91:The IV.6 watchdog had a regression: `_last_cmd_timeout` persisted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:92:across watchdog-triggered subprocess restart, so the fresh
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:93:subprocess immediately saw a stale timeout signal on the next
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:94:poll and was restarted again — restart storm (30–40
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:95:restarts/minute observed on Ubuntu lab PC). Hotfix added 60 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:96:cooldown between command-watchdog restarts via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:97:`_last_cmd_watchdog_restart` timestamp + missing `return` after
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:98:restart so no further checks run in the same poll cycle. Does NOT
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:99:resolve B1; only prevents pathological amplification.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:100:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:101:## IV.7 ipc:// transport experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:102:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:103:Branch `experiment/iv7-ipc-transport` switches the loopback default
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:104:to Unix-domain sockets (`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-pub.sock`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:105:`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-cmd.sock`) on macOS / Linux; Windows retains
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:106:`tcp://`. Hypothesis: TCP-loopback layer is the remaining culprit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:107:since shared-REQ-state, mp.Queue back-pressure, engine REP wedge,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:108:and async cancellation have all been ruled out.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:109:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:110:Outcome 2026-04-23 was originally a runtime failure attributed to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:111:the transport. [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-b2b4fb5-investigation|H3]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:112:confirmed the failure was the b2b4fb5 hardening probe racing the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:113:ipc:// bind, NOT the transport. Repaired via R1 (bounded-backoff
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:114:retry). After the repair lands, ipc:// runs normally for ~80 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:115:then B1 fires anyway — IV.7 does not close B1, only shifts the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:116:failure surface.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:117:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:118:## Status (2026-04-26)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:119:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:120:- B1 ZMQ idle-death — still OPEN. Blocks `v0.34.0` tag. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:121:  [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:122:- IV.6 partial mitigation — landed, defense-in-depth.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:123:- IV.7 ipc:// — viable transport, but does not close B1. Next
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:124:  hypotheses: H4 (pyzmq 25.x + Python 3.14 asyncio integration),
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:125:  H5 (engine REP state machine).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:126:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:127:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:128:

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:131:- [[30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:132:- [[40 Decisions/ADR-002 R1 bounded-backoff probe retry]]

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:6:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:7:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:8:# ADR-2026-04-24 — D4a cherry-picks + D1 R1 repair execution
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:9:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:10:## Context
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:12:After [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-b2b4fb5-investigation]] confirmed H3,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:13:architect approved the **R1 repair** strategy: bounded-backoff retry
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:14:in the b2b4fb5 startup probe. Two streams of work needed to land:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:15:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:16:- **D4a** — 5 diag-tool cherry-picks from the agent-swarm branch onto
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:17:  master (reusable B1 helpers + canonical capture CLI).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:18:- **D1** — implement R1 on a feature branch on top of D4a.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:19:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:20:`D4b` (7 doc cherry-picks) and `D2` (H4 split-context falsification
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:21:on `experiment/iv7-ipc-transport`) were deferred to a later session
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:22:per architect "sequential, not parallel" directive.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:23:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:24:## Decision
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:25:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:26:### D4a — 5 cherry-picks landed on master with `-x` provenance

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:40:### D1 — R1 implementation on `feat/b2b4fb5-repair`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:42:- Branch: `feat/b2b4fb5-repair`, base = `62314be`, HEAD = `c3f4f86`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:43:- Scope: `tools/diag_zmq_b1_capture.py` + its test only (Codex-01
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:44:  Stream A scope fence respected).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:45:- New helper `_validate_bridge_startup(bridge, attempts=5,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:46:  backoff_s=0.2, sleep_fn=time.sleep)`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:47:  1. `bridge.is_alive()` single-shot subprocess-spawn-failure catch.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:48:  2. Loop up to `attempts` × `bridge.send_command({"cmd":
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:49:     "safety_status"})`. First OK reply passes.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:50:  3. Inter-attempt `sleep_fn(backoff_s)` with DEBUG log.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:51:  4. All non-OK → `RuntimeError(f"Bridge startup probe failed:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:52:     {last_reply!r}")` — same error surface as b2b4fb5 (downstream
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:53:     greppers unaffected).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:54:- Tests landed: cases 1–3 (dead bridge / transient non-OK + recover /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:55:  all non-OK with bounded sleeps). Cases 4–6 (live engine ipc://,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:56:  tcp://, delayed-REP harness) deferred to a hardware-validation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:57:  session, noted in test-file header.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:59:### DROP bucket — 4 commits, ledger only
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:60:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:61:Per architect Q3 (rationale via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:62:`gemini-04-safe-merge-eval.response.md`): no forwarding doc, no
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:63:cherry-pick, branch preservation on `codex/safe-merge-b1-truth-recovery`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:64:is the historical record:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:65:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:66:- `ab72c84` — pre-ORCHESTRATION roadmap-review prompts; superseded.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:70:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:71:### Q4 equivalence check — PASS
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:72:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:73:Post-merge Codex gpt-5.5/high adversarial equivalence review on the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:74:M2 merge-commit `89b4db1`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:75:- Zero findings at any severity. Only `ACCEPTABLE` category used,

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:77:- Codex ran inline Python predicate simulation across 7 edge-case
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:78:  inputs — R1 and b2b4fb5 behave identically.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:79:- Verified `ZmqBridge.send_command` + `zmq_subprocess.cmd_forward_loop`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:80:  no retry-induced REQ socket state leak (IV.6 ephemeral REQ
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:81:  invariant preserved).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:82:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:83:D1 loop formally closed. Branch `feat/b2b4fb5-repair` deleted local +
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:84:remote; `c3f4f86` reachable via second-parent of `89b4db1`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:85:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:86:## Consequences
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md:87:

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:2:source: docs/decisions/2026-04-24-b2b4fb5-investigation.md; docs/decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution.md; docs/ORCHESTRATION.md §7 hardening rule
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:4:status: closed — H3 confirmed, R1 fix shipped
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:7:# b2b4fb5 hardening race
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:9:The story of how a transport-agnostic hardening probe ended up
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:10:killing an unrelated transport experiment, and how it got blamed for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:11:something it didn't cause.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:12:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:13:## Timeline
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:14:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:15:- **2026-04-23 14:25** — Commit `b2b4fb5` ("harden B1 capture bridge
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:16:  startup validation") landed. Adds synchronous probe
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:17:  `_validate_bridge_startup()` to `tools/diag_zmq_b1_capture.py` that
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:18:  fails fast on `bridge.is_alive() == False` or first non-OK
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:19:  `safety_status` reply.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:20:- **2026-04-23 15:10** — Commit `157c4bc` (IV.7 `ipc://` transport
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:21:  experiment) landed 45 minutes later, on a different branch.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:22:- **2026-04-23 ~16:30** — IV.7 runtime test fails. Single-line
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:23:  evidence in `/tmp/diag_iv7_capture.log`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:24:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:25:  > B1 capture aborted: Bridge startup probe failed:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:26:  > `{'ok': False, 'error': 'Engine не отвечает (Resource temporarily unavailable)'}`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:28:  The IV.7 narrative becomes "ipc:// transport doesn't work".
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:29:- **2026-04-24 00:05** — Investigation session loads
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:30:  multi-model-consultation skill manually (registry snapshot at
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:31:  session start did not include it; skill was committed mid-session
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:32:  via `9a1a100`). Phase 1 self-analysis produces revised reading.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:33:- **2026-04-24 00:18** — Phase 3 empirical retest with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:34:  `git revert b2b4fb5` on the worktree. Fresh ipc:// engine, b2b4fb5
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:35:  hardening reverted: **20/20 captures OK**.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:36:- **2026-04-24** — H3 confirmed; R1 (bounded-backoff retry) chosen
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:37:  by architect; implemented on `feat/b2b4fb5-repair` (`c3f4f86`),
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:38:  Q4 equivalence check PASS, merged via `89b4db1`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:40:## What the probe was supposed to do
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:42:The hardening probe added a guard against subprocess-spawn-failure:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:43:if `bridge.is_alive() == False`, the diag tool aborts with a clear
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:44:error before trying to send commands. Useful intent — the previous
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:45:behavior was that a broken subprocess would surface as cryptic
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:46:timeout 35 s later.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:47:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:48:## What actually happened
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:49:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:50:`b2b4fb5`'s probe is technically transport-agnostic: it calls
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:51:`bridge.is_alive()` (on `multiprocessing.Process`) and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:52:`bridge.send_command()` (queue-dispatched), not anything
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:53:TCP-specific. But the probe is **synchronous** and fires
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:54:immediately after `bridge.start() + time.sleep(1.0)`. Under
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:55:`tcp://` loopback that's plenty of time — `connect()+send()+recv()`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:56:is effectively instantaneous. Under `ipc://` Unix-socket bind, the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:57:engine's REP socket may not be ready when the probe fires; the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:58:bridge subprocess returns `zmq.Again` to the diag, and the diag
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:59:treats it as a hard failure.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:60:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:61:Result: probe race against ipc:// bind timing. Tcp:// hides the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:62:race; ipc:// exposes it.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:63:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:64:## Empirical confirmation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:65:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:66:| Scenario | Hardening | Transport | Engine age at cmd #1 | Result |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:67:|---|---|---|---|---|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:68:| 2026-04-23 IV.7 failure | present | ipc:// | < 1 s | **abort at cmd #0** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:69:| `git revert b2b4fb5`, bridge_extended | reverted | ipc:// | 4 s | 44/44 OK then B1 idle-death cmd #45 |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:70:| `git revert b2b4fb5`, b1_capture fresh | reverted | ipc:// | 4 s | **20/20 OK** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:71:| pre-b2b4fb5 master, tcp:// | n/a | tcp:// | < 1 s | OK |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:72:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:73:The controlling variable is hardening + engine-age combo; revert
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:74:makes b1_capture pass on ipc://.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:75:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:76:## Repair (R1)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:77:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:78:`feat/b2b4fb5-repair` `c3f4f86` — bounded-backoff retry in the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:79:probe. Helper signature:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:80:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:81:```python
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:82:_validate_bridge_startup(bridge, attempts=5, backoff_s=0.2,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:83:                         sleep_fn=time.sleep)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:84:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:85:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:86:1. `bridge.is_alive()` single-shot — keeps subprocess-spawn-failure
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:87:   detection.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:88:2. Loop up to 5 attempts × `bridge.send_command({"cmd":
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:89:   "safety_status"})`. First OK reply passes.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:90:3. 0.2 s sleep between attempts (bounded; total wait ≤ 1.0 s).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:91:4. All non-OK after 5 → raises `RuntimeError(f"Bridge startup probe
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:92:   failed: {last_reply!r}")` — same error surface as b2b4fb5 so
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:93:   downstream greppers aren't broken.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:94:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:95:Q4 adversarial equivalence check (Codex gpt-5.5/high) PASS, zero
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:96:findings, R1 and b2b4fb5 predicates behave identically across 7
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:97:edge-case inputs.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:98:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:99:## What this taught us
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:100:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:101:- **Hardening + feature collision is a real failure mode**, not
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:102:  hypothetical. ORCHESTRATION.md §7 was added specifically because
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:103:  of this incident: if CC is about to land a hardening change and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:104:  there's an active feature branch, check whether the hardening
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:105:  could affect the feature.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:106:- **Tcp:// and ipc:// have different bind semantics.** Sync health
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:107:  probes that worked on TCP loopback may fail on ipc:// without
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:108:  any code path being "wrong" per se — just an unstated timing
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:109:  assumption.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:110:- **Misattribution is recoverable** if evidence is preserved.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:111:  `/tmp/diag_iv7_capture.log` was the single sentence that broke
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:112:  the false narrative.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:113:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:114:## Status
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:115:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:116:Closed. R1 in master; B1 still OPEN (separately) — see
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:117:[[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:118:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race.md:119:## See also

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:7:# Glossary
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:9:Compact reference for project-internal vocabulary. Linked from any
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:10:note that uses a term for the first time.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:12:## Bugs and incidents
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:14:- **B1** — ZMQ command channel idle-death bug. After ~80s of GUI idle,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:15:  command plane stops responding while data plane keeps publishing.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:16:  Cmd-only failure mode. See [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:17:- **b2b4fb5** — Misattribution incident: a runtime regression initially
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:18:  blamed on IV.7 ipc:// experiment turned out to be the b2b4fb5
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:19:  hardened-probe race vs. engine bind. R1 bounded-backoff retry was
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:20:  the fix. See [[30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:21:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:22:## Project numbering
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:23:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:24:- **IV.N** — Investigation / Infrastructure batch N. IV.4..IV.7 known.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:25:  IV.4 = safe-features batch (F1 Parquet UI + base dep, F2 debug toggle,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:26:  F6 auto-report verify, F11 shift handover) — see
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:27:  [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]]. IV.6 = cmd-plane hardening
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:28:  ([[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]]). IV.7 = ipc://
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:29:  transport experiment ([[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]]).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:30:- **F1..F18** — Feature numbers from `ROADMAP.md` F-table. F1, F2, F6,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:31:  F11 shipped in IV.4. See [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:32:- **H1..H5** — Hypotheses for B1 root cause. H1, H2, H3 falsified; H4
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:33:  partially consistent (D2 experiment pending); H5 untested. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:34:  [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:35:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:36:## Acronyms
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:38:- **ADR** — Architecture Decision Record. One file per decision; see
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:39:  [[40 Decisions/_index]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:40:- **FSM** — Finite State Machine (specifically `SafetyManager`'s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:41:  6-state FSM: SAFE_OFF → READY → RUN_PERMITTED → RUNNING →
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:42:  FAULT_LATCHED → MANUAL_RECOVERY → READY).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:43:- **SRDG** — Sensor Raw Data Grabbing. Continuous acquisition mode in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:44:  Calibration v2 (`CalibrationAcquisitionService`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:45:- **KRDG** — Kelvin Reading. LakeShore command for calibrated temperature.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:46:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:47:## Hardware
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:48:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:49:- **LakeShore 218S** — 8-channel cryogenic thermometer, GPIB / serial
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:50:  daisy-chain.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:51:- **Keithley 2604B** — Dual-SMU source-meter (channels `smua`, `smub`),
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:52:  GPIB / USB-TMC. Host-side P=const regulation.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:53:- **Thyracont VSP63D** — Vacuum gauge, RS-485 + V1/V2 protocol with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:54:  checksum quirks.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:55:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:56:## File / output formats
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:58:- **`.330`** — LakeShore controller calibration table format,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:59:  breakpoint-based (NOT polynomial coefficients). To be removed when
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:60:  `.cof` lands.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:61:- **`.340`** — LakeShore newer breakpoint format, same family as `.330`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:62:- **`.cof`** — Discussed (architect note 2026-04-25) raw-Chebyshev-
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:63:  coefficient export format. **Not present in current code surface.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:64:- **JSON (calibration)** — CryoDAQ-internal calibration format with
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:65:  Chebyshev coefficients per zone, metrics, source session IDs.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:66:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:67:## Vault-internal
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:68:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:69:- **Vault** — `~/Vault/CryoDAQ/` Obsidian knowledge base (this tree).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:70:- **Repo** — `~/Projects/cryodaq/` authoritative source code. Repo wins
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:71:  on conflict.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:72:- **`last_synced:`** — note frontmatter date showing when the note was
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/glossary.md:73:  last reconciled with repo. Older than 30 days → stale flag in source map.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:7:# Versions
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:9:The CryoDAQ tag history is **partial**. `pyproject.toml` carries the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:10:intended next version, but most package-state increments between
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:11:v0.13.0 (CLAUDE.md "Current package metadata") and the present have
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:12:not been tagged. Retroactive tagging (`v0.34.0..v0.38.0` plan) is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:13:queued per `docs/decisions/2026-04-23-cleanup-baseline.md`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:14:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:15:## Tag table

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:20:| `v0.34.0` | (planned) | **blocked** | IV.4 safe features batch (F1 Parquet, F2 debug toggle, F6 auto-report verify, F11 shift handover); Phase II.1–II.6/II.13 cleanup. **Blocked by B1 ZMQ idle-death** ([[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]]). | `7cb5634` (IV.4 close) — pending real `git tag` |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/60 Roadmap/Versions.md:21:| Retroactive tags v0.34.0..v0.38.0 | (planned) | postponed | Per [[40 Decisions/2026-04-23-cleanup-baseline]], pre-2026-04-20 dev increments may be retroactively tagged once v0.34.0 ships. (Wording is per source ledger; chronologically it covers a future tag-pass over historical work.) | n/a |
