/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:2:source: synthesized from CLAUDE.md persistence section, PROJECT_STATUS.md invariants 2/13/14, src/cryodaq/core/scheduler.py, Phase 2d retro
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:3:adr_id: ADR-001
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:4:date: 2026-04-26 (synthesized)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:5:status: accepted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:6:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:7:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:8:# ADR-001 — Persistence-first invariant
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:9:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:10:## Context
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:12:CryoDAQ runs multi-day cryogenic experiments. After any unplanned
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:13:engine termination the post-mortem reconstruction must answer "what
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:14:did the operator's GUI see at time T?". If the in-memory broker
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:15:publishes a reading that has not yet committed to durable SQLite
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:16:storage, the operator's last-observed state diverges from the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:17:durable record. That divergence makes incident analysis unreliable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:18:exactly when it matters most.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:19:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:20:CryoDAQ also runs alongside a safety subsystem
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:21:([[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]]) that consumes a separate broker and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:22:makes source-on/off decisions. If the safety subsystem ever sees a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:23:reading the writer didn't, the safety state and durable record can
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:24:diverge under fault.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:25:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:26:## Decision
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:28:The engine enforces a strict ordering at every poll cycle, in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:29:`Scheduler._process_readings()`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:30:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:31:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:32:InstrumentDriver.read_channels()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:33:  → 1. SQLiteWriter.write_immediate()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:34:    2. THEN DataBroker.publish_batch()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:35:    3. THEN SafetyBroker.publish_batch()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:36:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:37:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:38:The order is unconditional and not switchable.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:39:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:40:This is supported by:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:41:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:42:- **WAL mode verification at startup.** `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:43:  result is checked; engine refuses to start if WAL is not actually
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:44:  on (Phase 2d invariant 14).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:45:- **Atomic file writes** for sidecar artifacts (experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:46:  metadata, calibration index/curve) via `core/atomic_write.py`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:47:  using `os.replace()` (Phase 2d invariant 13).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:48:- **OVERRANGE / UNDERRANGE → ±inf** with `status` carried as REAL
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:49:  in SQLite. SENSOR_ERROR / TIMEOUT (NaN) filtered before write
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:50:  because SQLite treats NaN as NULL (Phase 2d invariant 10).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:51:- **Calibration KRDG+SRDG persist in one transaction per poll
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:52:  cycle.** State mutation deferred to `on_srdg_persisted` so that a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:53:  write failure does not leave `t_min`/`t_max` diverged from the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:54:  actual persisted data (Phase 2d invariant 15, Jules R2 Q3).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:55:- **Cancellation shielding** on post-fault paths: `emergency_off`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:56:  `fault_log_callback` (BEFORE optional broker publish — Jules R2),
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:57:  `_ensure_output_off` in `_safe_off` (Phase 2d invariant 11).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:58:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:59:## Consequences
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:60:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:61:- A few-ms SQLite write latency lands on the engine's hot path per
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:62:  poll cycle. On WAL on SSD this is sub-ms; on spinning disk a few
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:63:  ms. Operator-perceived latency is dominated by the LakeShore
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:64:  poll interval (2 s) anyway.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:65:- **Durability caveat.** SQLite WAL uses `synchronous=NORMAL` by
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:66:  default, which can lose roughly the last ~1 s on abrupt power
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:67:  loss. Production deployment expects UPS coverage or
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:68:  `CRYODAQ_SQLITE_SYNC=FULL` environment override; the latter
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:69:  trades durability for write throughput. Phase 2d C.3 deferral.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:70:- GUI dashboards must hold local rolling buffers (`channel_buffer.py`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:71:  to render high-frequency timeseries — but the buffer is fed from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:72:  the broker, which only sees committed readings.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:73:- "Performance mode" overrides are not allowed. Any future
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:74:  optimization that would skip or defer the write step has to clear
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:75:  this ADR.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:76:- The fail-closed config invariant (engine exit code 2 on missing
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:77:  / malformed `safety.yaml`, `alarms_v3.yaml`, `interlocks.yaml`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:78:  `housekeeping.yaml`, `channels.yaml`) extends the same default:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:79:  start in a state where durable behavior is guaranteed, refuse to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:80:  start otherwise.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:81:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:82:## Status
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:83:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:84:Accepted. Has been the operating contract since Phase 2d. The
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:85:`docs/decisions/*` ledgers do not contain a separate ADR for this
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:86:invariant because it predates the ledger system; the invariant is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:87:captured in `CLAUDE.md` and `PROJECT_STATUS.md`. This synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:88:ADR exists to make the rationale visible alongside the other ADRs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:89:in this vault.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:90:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:91:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:92:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:93:- [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] — implementation page
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:94:- [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]] — cancellation-shielded fault path
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:95:- [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]] — one-transaction rule
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:2:source: synthesized from src/cryodaq/analytics/base_plugin.py, analytics/plugin_loader.py, CLAUDE.md "Аналитика"
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:3:adr_id: ADR-003
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:4:date: 2026-04-26 (synthesized; underlying decision pre-2026)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:5:status: accepted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:6:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:7:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:8:# ADR-003 — Plugin isolation via ABC
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:9:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:10:## Context
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:12:Original analytics architecture imported plugin modules directly
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:13:into the engine and called their `process()` functions inline on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:14:the asyncio event loop. A plugin throwing an exception propagated
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:15:up the call chain, killed the scheduler task, and crashed the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:16:engine.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:17:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:18:For a system whose value proposition is "long-running, reliable,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:19:no-touch acquisition during multi-day experiments", a single
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:20:buggy operator-authored plugin should never be able to stop data
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:21:acquisition.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:22:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:23:## Decision
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:24:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:25:Two pieces:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:26:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:27:1. **`AnalyticsPlugin` ABC** (`analytics/base_plugin.py`). Defines
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:28:   the contract via `@abstractmethod async def process(self,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:29:   readings: list[Reading]) -> list[DerivedMetric]`. Plugin id is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:30:   set in `__init__`. Optional `configure(config: dict)` for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:31:   YAML parameter ingestion (default impl just stores the dict).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:32:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:33:2. **`PluginPipeline`** (`analytics/plugin_loader.py`). Owns the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:34:   plugin lifecycle:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:35:   - Loads `*.py` files dynamically via
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:36:     `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` + `exec_module`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:37:   - Subscribes once to `DataBroker` as `plugin_pipeline`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:38:   - **Per-batch `try/except` around each plugin's `process()`** —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:39:     plugin exceptions are logged at ERROR level and that batch is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:40:     dropped for that plugin only. Other plugins continue.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:41:   - Hot reload via 5 s mtime polling (`_watch_loop`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:42:   - File-load failures log + skip; one bad file does not poison
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:43:     the pipeline.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:44:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:45:`DerivedMetric` is a frozen dataclass with `timestamp`, `plugin_id`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:46:`metric`, `value`, `unit`, `metadata` and a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:47:`DerivedMetric.now(...)` factory for uniform UTC timestamping.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:48:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:49:## Consequences
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:50:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:51:- Engine survives plugin exceptions.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:52:- Plugins may use `numpy`/`scipy` (the no-numpy invariant applies
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:53:  only to `core/` and `drivers/`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:54:- 5 s mtime polling adds a small steady-state cost; chosen over
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:55:  `inotify` / `FSEvents` to avoid platform-specific dependencies.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:56:- Plugin authors get a tiny, predictable surface: implement
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:57:  `process()`, optionally `configure()`. Roughly 10 lines for a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:58:  minimal plugin.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:59:- Synthetic `Reading` republishing means **live broker subscribers**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:60:  (GUI, web, ZMQ, alarm subscribers) handle derived metrics uniformly.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:61:  No SQLite persistence in the current code surface
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:62:  (`SQLiteWriter` runs in immediate mode from `Scheduler`, not as a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:63:  broker subscriber); persisting plugin output is a future wiring
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:64:  task if needed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:65:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:66:## Status
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:67:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:68:Accepted. Pattern is the basis of all subsequent analytics work
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:69:(`sensor_diagnostics`, `vacuum_trend`, future F16 plugin SDK).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:70:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:71:The cooldown predictor (`core/cooldown_service.py`) is deliberately
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:72:**not** a plugin, because it needs orchestration with the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:73:experiment lifecycle that the plugin pipeline doesn't provide.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:74:That carve-out is fine; the ABC is for stateless, batch-driven
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:75:analytics.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:76:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:77:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:78:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:79:- [[10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture]] — implementation page
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:80:- [[30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild]] — story behind
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:81:  the rebuild
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:82:- [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] — F16 (plugin hot-reload SDK +
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC.md:83:  examples)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:1:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:2:source: CLAUDE.md "Архитектура"; PROJECT_STATUS.md "Архитектура"; README.md; CLAUDE.md persistence section
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:4:status: synthesized
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:5:---
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:6:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:7:# Architecture overview
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:8:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:9:CryoDAQ runs as three independent processes that communicate over
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:10:ZeroMQ. Each contour can be restarted without taking the others down.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:11:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:12:## Three runtime contours
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:13:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:14:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:15:                 ZMQ PUB :5555 (data, msgpack)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:16:                 ZMQ REP :5556 (commands, JSON)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:17:                          ↓
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:18:cryodaq-engine  ─────────────────────  cryodaq-gui (PySide6)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:19:(headless                                 ToolRail / TopWatchBar /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:20: asyncio)                                 Dashboard / Overlays /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:21:                                          BottomStatusBar
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:22:                          ↓
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:23:                  cryodaq.web.server:app
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:24:                  (FastAPI monitoring,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:25:                   :8080, optional)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:26:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:27:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:28:**Engine (`cryodaq-engine`).** Headless asyncio runtime. Owns
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:29:`Scheduler`, drivers, `SQLiteWriter`, `DataBroker`, `SafetyBroker`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:30:`SafetyManager`, alarms, interlocks, housekeeping, plugin loader,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:31:calibration acquisition. No PySide6 import. Rules:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:32:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:33:- No blocking I/O on the event loop (one documented exception:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:34:  `reporting/generator.py` shells out to `soffice` via sync
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:35:  `subprocess.run` for best-effort PDF — DEEP_AUDIT finding E.2)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:36:- No numpy/scipy in `drivers/` or `core/` (one documented exception:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:37:  `core/sensor_diagnostics.py` for MAD / correlation)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:38:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:39:**GUI (`cryodaq-gui` / `cryodaq` launcher).** Separate process. Subscribes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:40:to engine PUB stream; sends commands via REQ. Since v0.33.0 the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:41:canonical shell is `MainWindowV2` (Phase I.1 / Phase UI-1 v2):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:42:`TopWatchBar` + `ToolRail` + `BottomStatusBar` + overlay-based content.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:43:The legacy 10-tab `MainWindow` (`gui/main_window.py`) and the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:44:DEPRECATED Phase-I widget panels (alarm / archive / calibration /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:45:conductivity / instrument_status / sensor_diag_panel / keithley /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:46:operator_log / experiment_workspace / autosweep) were retired and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:47:deleted in Phase II.13. Remaining `gui/widgets/*` modules are
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:48:ancillary, non-overlay surfaces (overview_panel, temp_panel,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:49:pressure_panel, channel_editor, connection_settings, shift_handover,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:50:etc.); `widgets/common.py` is retained for shared helpers.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:51:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:52:**Web (`cryodaq.web.server:app`).** Optional FastAPI surface on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:53:`:8080`. Read-only monitoring. Loopback-only deployment is the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:54:expected pattern (auth deferred to F7 / G.1).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:55:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:56:## Persistence-first ordering
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:57:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:58:Hard ordering invariant:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:59:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:60:```text
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:61:InstrumentDriver.read_channels()
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:62:  → Scheduler
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:63:     1. SQLiteWriter.write_immediate()       ← commit first
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:64:     2. THEN DataBroker.publish_batch()      ← GUI / web see it
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:65:     3. THEN SafetyBroker.publish_batch()    ← safety FSM sees it
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:66:```
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:67:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:68:> If `DataBroker` has a reading, it has already been written to SQLite.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:69:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:70:This is non-negotiable for post-mortem reconstruction
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:71:([[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]]). It costs ~few-ms GUI latency
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:72:per poll; that tradeoff is intentional.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:73:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:74:## ZMQ topology
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:75:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:76:- **PUB/SUB :5555** — data plane (msgpack). Always-on stream of
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:77:  readings, alarm events, safety state.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:78:- **REP/REQ :5556** — command plane (JSON). Operator commands
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:79:  (start experiment, acknowledge fault, request status).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:80:- **Subprocess isolation.** Engine runs ZMQ in a child subprocess
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:81:  (`core/zmq_subprocess.py`); the engine main loop is shielded from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:82:  ZMQ crashes.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:83:- **Watchdog** with 60s cooldown after restart — prevents IV.6 restart
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:84:  storm ([[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]]).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:85:- **Transport experiment** `ipc://` (IV.7) is in progress — see
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:86:  [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]] and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:87:  [[30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:88:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:89:## Subsystem map
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:90:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:91:Active narrative pages live under `10 Subsystems/`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:92:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:93:- [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]] — single source on/off authority
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:94:- [[10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge]] — engine ↔ GUI command + data planes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:95:- [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] — write-before-publish invariant
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:96:- [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]] — continuous SRDG, Chebyshev fit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:97:- [[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]] — YAML config, phase-aware
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:98:- [[10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture]] — hot-reload analytics SDK
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:99:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:100:## Configuration files
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:101:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:102:All YAML, all under `config/`. Changes require engine restart
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:103:(fail-closed on malformed):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:104:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:105:- `instruments.yaml` (+ `*.local.yaml` overrides)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:106:- `safety.yaml`, `interlocks.yaml`, `alarms.yaml`, `alarms_v3.yaml`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:107:- `channels.yaml`, `housekeeping.yaml`, `cooldown.yaml`, `plugins.yaml`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:108:- `notifications.yaml`, `shifts.yaml`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:109:- `experiment_templates/*.yaml`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:110:- `themes/*.yaml` (six bundled theme packs since 2026-04-18)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:111:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:112:Five safety-adjacent configs (`safety`, `alarm`, `interlock`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:113:`housekeeping`, `channels`) raise subsystem-specific `ConfigError` →
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:114:engine exit code 2 → no auto-restart. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:115:[[40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant]] for the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:116:rationale on similar fail-closed defaults.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:117:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:118:## Concurrency model
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:119:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:120:Single asyncio event loop in the engine. Scheduler drives drivers
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:121:sequentially (each driver instance has its own poll task).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:122:`SafetyManager` re-entry guarded against concurrent `_fault()` calls
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:123:(early return if `state == FAULT_LATCHED`, added 2026-04-17).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:124:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:125:`reporting/generator.py` and `log_get` are the documented exits to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:126:synchronous I/O and read executors respectively.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:127:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:128:## See also
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:129:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:130:- [[00 Overview/Hardware setup]] — what's on the bus
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:131:- [[00 Overview/What is CryoDAQ]] — scope and motivation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:132:- [[40 Decisions/_index]] — accepted ADRs

     2	source: CLAUDE.md "Источник истины по UI/визуальному дизайну"; PROJECT_STATUS.md "Phase I.1 + Design System v1.0.1"; PROJECT_STATUS.md "Host Integration Contract"; CHANGELOG.md "Phase III.A/B/C"

     5	---
     6	
     7	# UI and design system
     8	
     9	Operator-facing UI architecture, design system canon, and the
    10	Host Integration Contract that ships overlays end-to-end.
    11	
    12	## Why this note exists
    13	
    14	Gemini's Phase 2 audit flagged a coverage gap: the Phase I.1 shell
    15	rebuild + Design System v1.0.1 + Host Integration Contract are a
    16	significant fraction of recent architectural effort but had no
    17	dedicated Overview note. This page closes that gap.
    18	
    19	## Design system canon
    20	
    21	Source of truth for all operator-facing UI is `docs/design-system/`
    22	(v1.0.1, 67 canonical .md files, 139 tokens). The folder structure:
    23	
    24	- `docs/design-system/README.md` — entry point, navigation
    25	- `docs/design-system/MANIFEST.md` — full index + 65 encoded decisions
    26	- `docs/design-system/rules/<category>-rules.md` — 79 enforcement rules
    27	- `docs/design-system/components/<widget>.md` — generic primitives
    28	- `docs/design-system/cryodaq-primitives/<widget>.md` — domain widgets
    29	- `docs/design-system/patterns/<pattern>.md` — composition patterns
    30	- `docs/design-system/accessibility/*.md` — WCAG 2.2 AA commitment
    31	- `docs/design-system/governance/*.md` — evolution rules
    32	
    33	Token semantics ship through `gui/theme.py` (139 foundation tokens).
    34	Hard rule: **all colors / sizes / radii / fonts come from `theme.py`.
    35	Hardcoded hex / px / font-size violates RULE-COLOR-010 / RULE-TYPO-007 /
    36	RULE-SPACE-001.**
    37	
    38	Deprecated tokens (STONE_*) are policy-tracked through
    39	`docs/design-system/governance/deprecation-policy.md`. New code uses
    40	canonical names (FOREGROUND, BACKGROUND, MUTED_FOREGROUND, etc.).
    41	
    42	## Phase I.1 — shell rebuild (v0.33.0)
    43	
    44	`MainWindowV2` replaced the legacy 10-tab `MainWindow` as primary
    45	shell. Layout:
    46	
    47	- `TopWatchBar` — engine indicator, experiment status, 4 vitals + mode
    48	  badge (Эксперимент / Отладка)
    49	- `ToolRail` — left-side icon navigation (9 slots, `Ctrl+[1-9]`
    50	  transitional)
    51	- `OverlayContainer` — central content host with overlay primitives
    52	- `BottomStatusBar` — safety state strip
    53	- `DashboardView` — 5-zone ambient information radiator (sensor grid,
    54	  temp plot, pressure plot, phase widget, quick log) used as the
    55	  default view
    56	
    57	Canonical mnemonic shortcuts (per AD-002): `Ctrl+L/E/A/K/M/R/C/D` for
    58	global navigation, plus `F5` and `Ctrl+Shift+X`. Sole owner of bindings
    59	since `gui/main_window.py` was retired in Phase II.13. The
    60	transitional `Ctrl+[1-9]` rail-slot numbering is scheduled to retire
    61	with the legacy widget cleanup.
    62	
    63	Overlay primitives under
    64	`gui/shell/overlays/_design_system/`: `ModalCard`, `BentoGrid`,
    65	`DrillDownBreadcrumb`. Showcase at `_showcase.py` for visual review.
    66	
    67	## Phase II — overlay rebuilds + Host Integration Contract
    68	
    69	| Block | Status (2026-04-19) |
    70	|---|---|
    71	| II.1 AnalyticsView | ✅ COMPLETE (`860ecf3`) |
    72	| II.2 ArchiveOverlay | ✅ COMPLETE (`e4a60f3`) |
    73	| II.3 OperatorLog | ✅ COMPLETE (`9676acc`) |
    74	| II.4 AlarmOverlay | ⚠️ PARTIAL (badge routing only) |
    75	| II.5 ConductivityOverlay | ⬜ NOT STARTED (next) |
    76	| II.6 KeithleyOverlay | ✅ COMPLETE (`96adf5a`) |
    77	| II.7 CalibrationOverlay | ⬜ NOT STARTED |
    78	| II.8 Instruments+SensorDiag | ⬜ NOT STARTED |
    79	| II.9 ExperimentOverlay v3 | ⚠️ PARTIAL (functional; visual pending) |
    80	| II.13 legacy cleanup | ✅ — DEPRECATED v1 widgets and `main_window.py` deleted |
    81	
    82	### Host Integration Contract
    83	
    84	Codified after the II.6 Keithley FAIL revealed a systemic risk:
    85	overlays with public push setters (`set_connected`,
    86	`set_current_experiment`, `set_safety_ready`, etc.) are useless if
    87	`MainWindowV2` never calls them. Unit tests on the overlay alone pass
    88	while production is broken.
    89	
    90	Contract — mandatory for every overlay with push setters:
    91	
    92	1. **`_tick_status()` mirror** for `set_connected(bool)`.
    93	2. **`_dispatch_reading()` state sinks** for stateful readings
    94	   (safety state, experiment status, finalized events).
    95	3. **`_ensure_overlay()` replay** on lazy open — push cached state
    96	   the moment the overlay is constructed, so the first paint is
    97	   correct.
    98	
    99	Tests: overlay unit tests AND host integration tests
   100	(`tests/gui/shell/test_main_window_v2_<block>_wiring.py`) that
   101	exercise `MainWindowV2` entry points end-to-end (signal fire / cache
   102	set / `_ensure_overlay` call) and assert overlay state.
   103	
   104	## Phase III — analytics + theming + accent decoupling
   105	
   106	- **III.A — DS accent / status decoupling.** `STATUS_OK` (safety
   107	  green) was rendering UI states (selected rows, active tabs, primary
   108	  buttons, mode badge) and reading to operators as "this is healthy".
   109	  Introduced `SELECTION_BG` and `FOCUS_RING` neutral interaction
   110	  tokens; required by `_theme_loader.REQUIRED_TOKENS` across all 12
   111	  bundled themes; 5 overlays + TopWatchBar mode badge migrated to
   112	  `ACCENT + ON_ACCENT`. ADR 002 captures the rationale +
   113	  hue-distance invariants.
   114	- **III.B — GlobalTimeWindow + shared PressurePlot + PredictionWidget.**
   115	  `TimeWindow` enum promoted from dashboard-local to
   116	  `cryodaq.gui.state.time_window` with `GlobalTimeWindowController`
   117	  singleton. Every historical plot subscribes — clicking 1мин / 1ч /
   118	  6ч / 24ч / Всё on any plot updates every plot. Prediction plots
   119	  do NOT subscribe; they have their own forward horizon (1/3/6/12/24/48ч).
   120	  `ScientificLogAxisItem` for log-Y tick labels.
   121	- **III.C — Phase-aware AnalyticsView rebuild.** 2×2 QGridLayout swap
   122	  per experiment phase via `config/analytics_layout.yaml`. Layout per
   123	  phase: preparation → temperature; vacuum → vacuum prediction +
   124	  temp/pressure; cooldown → cooldown prediction; measurement → R_thermal
   125	  + keithley power; warmup / disassembly own mappings. New widget
   126	  registry at `gui/shell/views/analytics_widgets.py`.
   127	
   128	## Theme switcher (2026-04-18)
   129	
   130	Six bundled YAML theme packs at `config/themes/`: `default_cool`,
   131	`warm_stone`, `anthropic_mono`, `ochre_bloom`, `taupe_quiet`,
   132	`rose_dusk`. Runtime theme loader at `gui/_theme_loader.py` —
   133	`theme.py` reads tokens from YAML packs. Settings → Тема menu uses
   134	`os.execv` restart pattern. Status palette (STATUS_OK, WARNING,
   135	CAUTION, FAULT, INFO, STALE, COLD_HIGHLIGHT) locked across all packs.
   136	Phase III.A added `SELECTION_BG` + `FOCUS_RING` to all 12 themes
   137	including the additional `braun` pack.
   138	
   139	## Governance
   140	
   141	Architect = Vladimir. Every design-system change goes through
   142	`docs/design-system/governance/contribution.md`. Every GUI change
   143	that touches visual rendering must start by reading the relevant
   144	design-system files. Codex / Gemini reviews check for token leakage
   145	(hardcoded values), pattern mismatches, and accessibility regressions.
   146	
   147	## See also
   148	
   149	- [[00 Overview/Architecture overview]] — three runtime contours
   150	- [[00 Overview/What is CryoDAQ]] — scope and motivation
   151	- [[40 Decisions/_index]] — accepted ADRs
   152	- `docs/design-system/README.md` — full source of truth
   153	- `docs/CODEX_SELF_REVIEW_PLAYBOOK.md` — block-commit review cadence

CHANGELOG.md:457:- **`src/cryodaq/gui/theme.py` — color tokens load from YAML pack at import.**
CHANGELOG.md:459:  tiers, accent scale, text variants) are now read from the active theme
CHANGELOG.md:460:  pack via `_theme_loader.load_theme()`. Non-color tokens (typography,
CHANGELOG.md:462:  stops) remain hardcoded — they do not theme. Downstream consumers still
CHANGELOG.md:463:  use the same `from cryodaq.gui import theme; theme.ACCENT` API.
CHANGELOG.md:465:- **Status palette — one-time semantic refresh (LOCKED across all themes).**
CHANGELOG.md:466:  `STATUS_CAUTION` shifts from `#c47a30` (amber) to `#b35a38` (red-orange)
CHANGELOG.md:473:  by `tests/gui/test_theme_loader.py::test_status_palette_identical_across_all_themes`.
CHANGELOG.md:479:  (`#11151d` / `#30363d` / `#141821` etc.) that bypassed theme.py and
CHANGELOG.md:480:  prevented theme packs from taking effect. They now inherit
CHANGELOG.md:481:  `theme.SURFACE_PANEL` / `theme.BORDER_SUBTLE` defaults. Semantic hexes
CHANGELOG.md:482:  (debug-panel amber, group-box status accents, `_set_bg` heat-tier
CHANGELOG.md:496:  использует amber attention styling потому что режим отключает
CHANGELOG.md:581:  spacing, 4px sharp radius. Backwards-compatible alias'ы в theme.py.
CHANGELOG.md:585:  warmer background `#0d0e12`, видимая card elevation, возврат к
CHANGELOG.md:588:  конкретные hex значения в `theme.py`.
CHANGELOG.md:597:  фаза подсвечена `theme.ACCENT`, прошедшие muted, будущие dim. Hero
CHANGELOG.md:633:  1280, `BentoGrid` row-span now affects rendered height (with geometry test),
CHANGELOG.md:637:- **theme.py** — полностью переработан под новые design tokens.
CHANGELOG.md:679:- **Phase UI-1 v1 theming foundation (блоки 1-7).** `theme.py`
CHANGELOG.md:681:  fonts bundled. 10 Lucide SVG icons. `pyqtdarktheme-fork`
CHANGELOG.md:738:- **Calibration panel instrument prefix bug (`621f98a`).** Pre-existing:
CHANGELOG.md:742:  format. Added `_strip_instrument_prefix()` helper applied to
CHANGELOG.md:762:- **Graphify skill 0.3.12 → 0.4.13.** First graph build indexed 294

CHANGELOG.md:1230:  при cooldown), `detector_warmup` переведён на T12.
CHANGELOG.md:1267:- **Release v0.12.0** — первый production release commit.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   308	  legacy tokens / zero emoji / zero hardcoded hex (pre-commit gates
   309	  clean). Legacy widget at
   310	  `src/cryodaq/gui/widgets/calibration_panel.py` marked DEPRECATED;
   311	  removal in Phase III.3.
   312	
   313	### Added
   314	
   315	- **Six new themes: signal, instrument, amber (dark); gost, xcode,
   316	  braun (light).** STATUS palette hue-locked with lightness unlocked
   317	  for light substrates per ADR 001
   318	  (`docs/design-system/adr/001-light-theme-status-unlock.md`). Dark
   319	  packs continue to ship the verbatim STATUS hex set; new light packs
   320	  (gost / xcode / braun) ship a shifted-lightness variant that
   321	  preserves hue and restores WCAG AA (≥4.5:1) contrast against their
   322	  light `SURFACE_CARD`. Semantic identity («amber = WARNING, red =
   323	  FAULT») preserved 1:1 across mode switches. Settings → Тема menu
   324	  now surfaces all 12 bundled packs in a dark-group / light-group
   325	  layout with a visual separator between groups. Full rationale,
   326	  per-pack design axis, metrics, and pre-release smoke points:
     1	config/themes/amber.yaml
     2	config/themes/anthropic_mono.yaml
     3	config/themes/braun.yaml
     4	config/themes/default_cool.yaml
     5	config/themes/gost.yaml
     6	config/themes/instrument.yaml
     7	config/themes/ochre_bloom.yaml
     8	config/themes/rose_dusk.yaml
     9	config/themes/signal.yaml
    10	config/themes/taupe_quiet.yaml
    11	config/themes/warm_stone.yaml
    12	config/themes/xcode.yaml

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract.md:68:`launcher.py` / `engine.py` / `safety_manager.py` / `zmq_*.py`.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:40:## Dispatch (proven 2026-04-24)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:46:# Codex — 5 in parallel
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:47:for n in 01 02 03 04 05; do
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:50:      -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:51:      --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:54:      > RESPONSES/codex-'"$n"'-...response.md 2>&1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:55:  ' </dev/null >/dev/null 2>&1 &
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:62:    gemini -m gemini-3.1-pro-preview --yolo \
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:64:      > "$response" 2>&1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:65:    sleep 30
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:67:' </dev/null >/dev/null 2>&1 &
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:70:Tracking PIDs go to a `.pids` file under the artifacts dir for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:73:## What worked (2026-04-24, 10/10)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:75:- Codex parallel held up. Each Codex (`gpt-5.5 / high`) ran in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:76:  ~1–10 min wall-clock per brief.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:77:- Codex `gpt-5.5` model name accepted on first try after a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:78:  3-token probe. §3.7 fallback to gpt-5.4 did not fire.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:83:- **Gemini parallel rate-limited.** 5 simultaneous gemini-2.5-pro
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:84:  calls exhausted per-minute capacity within ~20 s. Variants of
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:86:- **Gemini default approval mode blocked tool access.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:87:  `run_shell_command` not approved → Gemini couldn't read files /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:92:error response files, relaunch as a single serial chain with 30 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:93:gaps and `--yolo`. Single tracking PID.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:95:Cost: serial chain bottoms out at ~30–60 min for 5 jobs vs
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:96:Codex's true-parallel ~10–15 min. Acceptable for overnight.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:98:## Morning retrieval (Phase 6–9 of the prompt)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:100:1. **Verify completion.** `.pids` file shows wrapper exit. Lost /
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:101:   slop cases (skill §4.2: < 500 words, no file:line refs,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:103:2. **Per-stream synthesis.** Group Codex + Gemini briefs by topic
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:106:3. **Master summary.** `MASTER_SUMMARY.md` with stream decisions +
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:108:4. **Single commit** of the whole `artifacts/consultations/<date>/`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:111:## Adaptations from plan (§13.3 ledger format)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:114:`Plan said / Reality / Adapted / Outcome`. The 2026-04-24 launch

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern.md:128:- Vault audit gates (this 2026-04-26 build) — Codex literal +

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:13:## §0. When NOT to use
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:17:2026-04-21..23 swarm cycle that produced 12 untracked review
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:27:Use only when **all three** hold:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:28:1. Decision has meaningful downside cost if wrong (safety,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:30:2. CC's reasoning is not confident (competing hypotheses, missing
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:32:3. Architect has either explicitly asked for a cross-check OR is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:35:## §1. Routing
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:39:| **Codex (gpt-5.5 / high)** | Adversarial code review with file:line refs. Concurrency, race conditions, IPC, subprocess, socket state. Terse FAIL/PASS verdicts. | Diff review pre-commit; specific bug hypothesis test; ZMQ / asyncio / subprocess code; ≤ 500-line diffs. | Long-prose strategy; "review the entire repo"; > 10 files at once. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:40:| **Gemini (3.1 Pro Preview)** | Wide-context architectural analysis. 1M-token window. Multi-file drift, doc-vs-code reconciliation, cross-cutting patterns. | Wide audit; doc-vs-code reconciliation; second opinion when Codex too narrow; loading entire spec + src tree. | Short-scope bug review (use Codex); < 500-word output (Gemini overwrites); quick yes/no. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:41:| **GLM-5.1 (via Chutes)** | Cheap. RU↔EN translation, mechanical transforms, second opinion check. | Translation; mechanical-transform draft (CC verifies); batch rename / rewrite; one-claim cheap second opinion. | Anything safety-critical; anything that lands unchecked on master; primary coordination (always CC). |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:42:| **Kimi K2.6 (via Chutes)** | 256K context, math-heavy, language tasks; lower hallucination than GLM in known domain. | Long-document analysis; math; second opinion on language. | Narrow code debugging vs Codex; trust unverified introspection. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:44:## §1.x identity-leak hazard
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:51:## §2. Brief templates

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:55:Model: gpt-5.5

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:63:Model: gemini-3.1-pro-preview
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:68:## §3. Invocation patterns
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:77:§3.7 model-string probe: before any batch dispatch, send a 3-token
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:79:resolves. If `gpt-5.5` not yet released or rejected → fall back to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:80:`gpt-5.4`. Update skill as model versions evolve.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:82:## §4. Slop check / retry
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:84:§4.2 — junk response criteria: <500 words of actual content; no
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:89:## §5. Synthesis format
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:91:After all consultations: `synthesis.md` covers
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:100:## §6. Codex sandbox / response gotchas
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:102:- Codex defaults to **read-only** filesystem sandbox. Cannot write
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:104:  1. Pass `--sandbox workspace-write` (for write-allowed sessions).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:105:  2. Capture stdout via shell redirect:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:107:     overnight batches since 2026-04-24.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:108:- Codex prints full reasoning transcript (100–300 KB typical).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:110:  `tail -250` or parse for the last `Model: gpt-5.X` marker.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:111:- CLI v0.124.0+ requires `--sandbox read-only|workspace-write|
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:112:  danger-full-access` (rejects the older `workspace-read` value).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:116:## §7. Gemini operational notes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:118:- `--yolo` required when the brief asks Gemini to read repo files
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:119:  or run shell commands; default approval mode blocks
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:120:  `run_shell_command`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:121:- Per-minute rate limit: 5 parallel Gemini-3.1-pro calls exhaust
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:122:  capacity within ~20 s. Default pattern when ≥4 jobs:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:123:  **Codex parallel + Gemini serial chain with 30 s gaps**. See
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:124:  [[50 Workflow/Overnight swarm pattern]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:126:## §8. When the architect is unavailable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:128:§8 of ORCHESTRATION.md takes precedence. CC works on previously
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation.md:131:the active plan only.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:33:  → 1. SQLiteWriter.write_immediate()

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:48:- **OVERRANGE / UNDERRANGE → ±inf** with `status` carried as REAL
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:50:  because SQLite treats NaN as NULL (Phase 2d invariant 10).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:52:  cycle.** State mutation deferred to `on_srdg_persisted` so that a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/40 Decisions/ADR-001 Persistence-first invariant.md:53:  write failure does not leave `t_min`/`t_max` diverged from the

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:9:`SafetyManager` is the single authority for source-on/off decisions in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:10:the engine. Source OFF is the default; running requires continuous
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:15:Before `SafetyManager`, source-on/off authority was distributed across
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:18:1. Race conditions between subsystems firing emergency-off in different
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:20:2. No single place where "is it safe to run right now?" had a defensible
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:23:`SafetyManager` collapses authority into a single FSM, with a single
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:24:asyncio lock (`_cmd_lock`) serializing `_active_sources` mutations
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:26:CLI). See `safety_manager.py:130`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:38:- **SAFE_OFF** — default. No source active. Preconditions may not be met.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:40:  fresh, no active interlock). Operator may request a run.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:46:  RUNNING. Source forced OFF. No automatic recovery. Re-entry guard
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:47:  returns early on concurrent `_fault()` calls (added 2026-04-17).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:51:`request_run()` may skip `READY` and go directly toward `RUNNING`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:52:when all preconditions already hold, but it still records the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:53:`RUN_PERMITTED` step on the way (`safety_manager.py:314-321 → 366-370`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:57:`SafetyManager` consumes the `SafetyBroker` (dedicated channel,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:58:`overflow=FAULT`). Stale data — no reading on a critical channel for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:59:`stale_timeout_s` (default 10 s) — fires `_fault("stale data")`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:60:Active monitoring runs in **`RUN_PERMITTED` and `RUNNING`** (not
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:61:`RUNNING` only) so a stuck `start_source()` cannot silently disable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:62:the checks (`safety_manager.py:884`). Outside those two states stale
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:67:`dT/dt` is computed by [[_meta/glossary#FSM|`RateEstimator`]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:68:(`window_s=120.0`, `min_points=60` after Phase 2c CC I.3). When any
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:69:critical-channel rate exceeds `max_dT_dt_K_per_min` (default 5 K/min,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:70:configured in `safety.yaml`), `_fault()` fires.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:72:> Note: 5 K/min is the **configurable default**, not a hard-coded
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:75:`min_points=60` over the `window_s=120 s` window means at least 60
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:76:critical-channel readings must have accumulated within the last 2 min
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:77:before the rate estimator returns a non-`None` rate; under the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:78:default 2 s LakeShore poll cadence (`config/instruments.yaml`) that's
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:79:~120 s of arrivals, matching the window length.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:83:`Keithley2604B.connect()` forces `OUTPUT_OFF` on both SMU channels
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:85:CRITICAL and continues (no crash, but the safety guarantee weakens).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:86:Documented in [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:92:- `emergency_off`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:93:- `fault_log_callback` (ordered BEFORE optional broker publish — Jules
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:95:- `_ensure_output_off` in `_safe_off`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:103:A `_fault()` re-entry guard added 2026-04-17 (`A1 safety audit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:112:violations in the same poll cycle) from clobbering `_fault_reason`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:113:emitting duplicate events, or re-firing `emergency_off`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:117:`load_config()` raises `SafetyConfigError` (distinct class for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:120:- `safety.yaml` not found at the configured path
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:122:- `critical_channels` is missing, not a list, or empty
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:125:Engine exits with code 2; no auto-restart. Same fail-closed pattern
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:126:applies to `alarms_v3.yaml`, `interlocks.yaml`, `housekeeping.yaml`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:127:`channels.yaml`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:129:## What is NOT here
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:131:- **No firmware TSP watchdog.** P=const regulation is host-side in
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:132:  `keithley_2604b.py`; the `tsp/p_const.lua` draft is **not loaded**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:133:  on the device. Phase 3 plan, requires hardware verification.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:134:- **No GUI-side authority.** GUI is read-only with respect to safety
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:137:- **No automatic recovery from FAULT_LATCHED.** Operator
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:142:- [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] — readings reach SafetyBroker
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:143:  AFTER SQLite commit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:144:- [[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]] — separate from safety FSM;
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Safety FSM.md:146:- [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]] — crash-recovery guard implementation

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:84:Codified after the II.6 Keithley FAIL revealed a systemic risk:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:85:overlays with public push setters (`set_connected`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:86:`set_current_experiment`, `set_safety_ready`, etc.) are useless if
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:87:`MainWindowV2` never calls them. Unit tests on the overlay alone pass
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:90:Contract — mandatory for every overlay with push setters:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:92:1. **`_tick_status()` mirror** for `set_connected(bool)`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:93:2. **`_dispatch_reading()` state sinks** for stateful readings
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:95:3. **`_ensure_overlay()` replay** on lazy open — push cached state

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:131:`warm_stone`, `anthropic_mono`, `ochre_bloom`, `taupe_quiet`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:132:`rose_dusk`. Runtime theme loader at `gui/_theme_loader.py` —
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:133:`theme.py` reads tokens from YAML packs. Settings → Тема menu uses

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/UI and design system.md:135:CAUTION, FAULT, INFO, STALE, COLD_HIGHLIGHT) locked across all packs.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:7:# Alarm engine v2
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:9:YAML-driven, phase-aware alarm engine. Replaces v1's hardcoded
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:14:`core/alarm_v2.py` defines:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:16:- **`AlarmEvent`** — frozen dataclass: `alarm_id`, `level`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:17:  `message`, `triggered_at`, `channels`, `values`, `acknowledged`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:18:- **`AlarmTransition` = TRIGGERED | CLEARED`** — typed literal.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:19:- **`PhaseProvider` / `SetpointProvider`** — duck-typed protocols
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:21:- **`AlarmEvaluator`** — pure function from `(alarm_id, alarm_config)`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:22:  to `AlarmEvent | None`, dispatching by `alarm_type`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:23:- **`AlarmStateManager`** — manages active/cleared transitions,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:28:| `alarm_type` | Signal | Where used |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:30:| `threshold` | scalar `value > threshold` (or `<`) | static safety/operational limits |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:31:| `composite` | AND/OR of sub-conditions, each typed | physically-motivated multi-channel rules |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:32:| `rate` | `dX/dt` over a configurable window via `RateEstimator` | trend-based anomaly (cooldown stall, runaway warming) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:33:| `stale` | last reading age > threshold | sensor / link health |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:35:Composite sub-conditions support `above`, `below`, `rate_above`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:36:`rate_below`, `rate_near_zero`, plus `phase_elapsed_s` as a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:42:`get_phase_elapsed_s()` let alarm rules be active only during
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:43:specific experiment phases. Example: `cooldown_stall` is meaningful
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:46:`SetpointProvider` lets thresholds be parameterized by experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:47:template (e.g. T_target = 4.2 K vs 77 K) so the same rule fires for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:52:`config/alarms_v3.yaml` declares all alarms. `core/alarm_config.py`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:53:validates and parses. Fail-closed: malformed `alarms_v3.yaml` → engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:54:exit code 2.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:56:`src/cryodaq/core/alarm_providers.py` ships concrete `PhaseProvider`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:57:and `SetpointProvider` wired to `ExperimentManager`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:61:`config/alarms_v3.yaml` previously contained a Latin "T" where
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:62:Cyrillic "Т" (U+0422) was meant. Runtime tests passed because the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:65:Full story: [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:67:`docs/design-system/` rules (RULE-COLOR / RULE-TYPO) carry similar
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:70:## Channel groups (current `alarms_v3.yaml`)

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:83:required `threshold` field. Log spam every ~2 s; engine does not

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:87:  threshold (or change the offending condition's type if `threshold`

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:92:Pending Codex-04 patch (overnight swarm 2026-04-24 stream).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:97:authority. `SafetyManager` is the only thing that turns sources on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2.md:99:configured separately in `config/interlocks.yaml`, but the

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:9:The single bug that blocks `v0.34.0` tag.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:14:`tcp://127.0.0.1:5556`, or `ipc://` since IV.7) stops responding
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:15:after some time of operation. Data plane (SUB on 5555 or
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:16:`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-pub.sock`) keeps publishing readings normally.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:19:RAPID 5Hz   (200ms idle): 291/291 OK, 0 fails    — high rate never fails
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:20:SPARSE 0.33Hz (3s idle):    9 OK, cmd #10 FAIL 35s — low rate fails
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:21:RECOVER 5Hz, post-fail:     cmd #1 FAIL immediately — permanent
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:26:direct in-process Python client in 0.001 s while the bridge
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:31:| macOS Darwin (Python 3.14.3, pyzmq 25.x) | stochastic 4–92 s uptime |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:32:| Ubuntu 22.04 (Python 3.12.13, pyzmq 26.4.0) | exactly 120 s after subprocess start (single data point, may vary) |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:34:Real Ubuntu lab PC reproduces in `./start.sh`, not just diag tools.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:40:| H1 | macOS kernel idle reaping of loopback TCP | `tcp_keepalive_time = 7200s` on Linux, yet 120 s deterministic failure | **falsified 2026-04-20** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:41:| H2 | Single long-lived REQ socket accumulating bad state (Codex's primary) | IV.6 implemented per-cmd ephemeral REQ; B1 still reproduces | **falsified 2026-04-20** |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:42:| H3 | b2b4fb5 hardening probe incompatible with `ipc://` | repro on revert + bind-time race confirmed | **partially confirmed** — explains 2026-04-23 IV.7 misattribution but does NOT explain B1 itself; B1 still fires after ~80 s on ipc:// |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:43:| H4 | pyzmq 25.x + Python 3.14 asyncio integration / engine REP task state under rapid REQ connect-disconnect churn | not yet tested in isolation | **untested** — D2 split-context falsification experiment scheduled |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:48:- Engine REP socket itself — direct Python REQ client gets 0.001 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:50:- Engine internals: SQLite writes (~9000/10 min), heartbeats every
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:51:  30 s, plugin ticks, scheduler all stay healthy.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:52:- Raw subprocess without `ZmqBridge` wrapping (`diag_zmq_subprocess.py`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:53:  succeeds short-term on both `fork` and `spawn` mp start methods.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:57:1. **`TCP_KEEPALIVE` on all four sockets** (commit `f5f9039`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:58:   reverted on cmd path in IV.6). Partial delay of failure on macOS
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:59:   only; Linux unaffected. Reverted on command + PUB paths since H1
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death.md:60:   was falsified; kept on `sub_drain_loop` SUB as orthogonal

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:16:| **Setup** | GUI: `cryodaq-gui` Calibration tab | Operator picks reference channel, target channels, optional context. Engine starts the experiment with `calibration_acquisition: true`. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:17:| **Acquisition** | Engine: `CalibrationAcquisitionService` | Continuous SRDG (`Sensor Raw Data Grabbing`) capture during the experiment, alongside KRDG (calibrated kelvin readings). One transaction per poll cycle. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:18:| **Results** | Engine + GUI: `CalibrationFitter` post-run pipeline | Extract → downsample → breakpoints → Chebyshev fit → export. Operator reviews and applies. |
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:22:`CalibrationAcquisitionService` (`core/calibration_acquisition.py`) is
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Calibration v2.md:25:LakeShore poll; the writer commits both KRDG and SRDG in one

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:21:> If `DataBroker` has a reading, it has already been written to SQLite.
