/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:24:reading **strictly after** that reading has committed to durable
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:31:post-mortem reconstruction MUST be able to answer "what did the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:42:`SQLiteWriter` uses WAL mode. Engine startup verifies WAL is actually
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:50:(Phase 2d invariant 14 — "WAL mode verification".)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:52:> **Durability caveat.** SQLite uses `synchronous=NORMAL` by default
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:53:> in WAL mode, which can lose roughly the last ~1 s of writes on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:55:> `src/cryodaq/storage/sqlite_writer.py:256`). Production lab
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:57:> environment override; see Phase 2d C.3 deferral
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:58:> ([[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]] / `PROJECT_STATUS.md`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:62:The cost is the SQLite `INSERT` time per poll cycle on the engine
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:65:LakeShore poll interval (2 s) anyway, so the SQLite write does not
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:69:hold local rolling history (`channel_buffer.py`). But all that data
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:70:ultimately came from the broker, which came from a committed write.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:77:- **Atomic file writes** (`core/atomic_write.py`) for experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:81:  State mutation deferred to `on_srdg_persisted` so a write failure
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:82:  does not leave `t_min`/`t_max` diverged from the actual persisted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:83:  data (Jules Round 2 Q3). See [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:85:  `emergency_off`, `fault_log_callback` (BEFORE optional broker
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/Persistence-first.md:86:  publish), `_ensure_output_off` in `_safe_off`.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:15:                 ZMQ PUB :5555 (data, msgpack)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:16:                 ZMQ REP :5556 (commands, JSON)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:25:                   :8080, optional)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:28:**Engine (`cryodaq-engine`).** Headless asyncio runtime. Owns
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:29:`Scheduler`, drivers, `SQLiteWriter`, `DataBroker`, `SafetyBroker`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:30:`SafetyManager`, alarms, interlocks, housekeeping, plugin loader,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:31:calibration acquisition. No PySide6 import. Rules:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:33:- No blocking I/O on the event loop (one documented exception:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:34:  `reporting/generator.py` shells out to `soffice` via sync
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:35:  `subprocess.run` for best-effort PDF — DEEP_AUDIT finding E.2)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:36:- No numpy/scipy in `drivers/` or `core/` (one documented exception:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:37:  `core/sensor_diagnostics.py` for MAD / correlation)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:39:**GUI (`cryodaq-gui` / `cryodaq` launcher).** Separate process. Subscribes
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:40:to engine PUB stream; sends commands via REQ. Since v0.33.0 the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:41:canonical shell is `MainWindowV2` (Phase I.1 / Phase UI-1 v2):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:42:`TopWatchBar` + `ToolRail` + `BottomStatusBar` + overlay-based content.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:43:The legacy 10-tab `MainWindow` (`gui/main_window.py`) and the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:47:deleted in Phase II.13. Remaining `gui/widgets/*` modules are
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:50:etc.); `widgets/common.py` is retained for shared helpers.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:52:**Web (`cryodaq.web.server:app`).** Optional FastAPI surface on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:53:`:8080`. Read-only monitoring. Loopback-only deployment is the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:54:expected pattern (auth deferred to F7 / G.1).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:63:     1. SQLiteWriter.write_immediate()       ← commit first
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:64:     2. THEN DataBroker.publish_batch()      ← GUI / web see it
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/00 Overview/Architecture overview.md:65:     3. THEN SafetyBroker.publish_batch()    ← safety FSM sees it

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:2:source: CC_PROMPT_VAULT_BUILD_2026-04-26.md §5; this build's reflection
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:4:status: protocol — read on every session that may touch the vault
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:22:`source:` and `last_synced:` frontmatter on every note so staleness
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:30:- **`CLAUDE.md` modified** in a way that changes architecture,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:32:  `00 Overview/` or `10 Subsystems/` and reconcile. Always update
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:33:  `last_synced:` after a real reconciliation; do not bump the date
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:35:- **New file in `docs/decisions/`** → mirror as a new ADR under
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:36:  `40 Decisions/<filename without .md>.md`. Frontmatter `source:`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:37:  cites the original file and same date. Do NOT invent ADR-NNN
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:39:  committed source decision file.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:40:- **Investigation closes** (a `30 Investigations/` candidate gains a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:42:  update the corresponding `30 Investigations/` page; add a
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:43:  `status:` line saying *closed* / *open* / *partially confirmed*.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:45:- **`ROADMAP.md` F-table status change** (✅ → 🔧, 🔧 → ⬜, etc.) →
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:46:  update `60 Roadmap/F-table backlog.md` row + frontmatter
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:47:  `last_synced:`. Do not deep-rewrite ROADMAP detail — link to the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:49:- **New skill in `.claude/skills/`** → digest into `50 Workflow/`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:52:  `[[50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract]]` if the skill operates
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:54:- **New tag** (`v0.34.0`+) shipped → add row to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:55:  `60 Roadmap/Versions.md`. If the release closes a long-standing
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:56:  bug, also flip the corresponding `30 Investigations/` page status.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:58:## Triggers — when CC should NOT update
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:60:- **Every commit.** Too noisy; vault is curated, not mechanical.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:65:- **`CHANGELOG.md` "Unreleased" updates that aren't yet released.**
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:68:  `docs/foo.md` to `docs/runbooks/foo.md` doesn't change what the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:69:  vault knows; just update the `source:` line on next reconciliation.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:73:Every note's `last_synced:` should be within **30 days** of the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:77:session, regenerate the source map (`artifacts/vault-build/build_source_map.py`)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:78:and read it. Notes older than 30 days should be reconciled before
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:81:## Process for a small reconciliation
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:83:1. Identify the trigger (which file in repo changed, what changed).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:84:2. Read the relevant vault note(s) AND the changed source file(s).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:85:3. Edit the note inline; bump `last_synced:` to today's date; update
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:86:   `source:` if new files joined the source list.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:87:4. If the change is structurally significant (new section,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:90:5. Append a one-line entry to `[[_meta/build log]]` under the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:95:The 2026-04-26 build is the reference example. The CC_PROMPT for it
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:96:lives at `~/Projects/cryodaq/CC_PROMPT_VAULT_BUILD_2026-04-26.md`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:111:skip if the change is small (< 3 notes touched, < 50 lines diff).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:114:[[50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation]]. Don't run audits on
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/_meta/update protocol.md:115:every micro-edit.

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:3:last_synced: 2026-04-26
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:9:A small story about why an architectural change was needed.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:13:Plugins were direct imports — modules in `analytics/` imported and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:15:on the engine event loop. Configuration was via `config/plugins.yaml`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:21:asyncio call chain, killed the scheduler task, and **crashed the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:32:1. **`AnalyticsPlugin` ABC** (`analytics/base_plugin.py`). Defines
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:34:   id is set in `__init__`. Optional `configure(config)` for YAML
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:36:2. **`PluginPipeline`** (`analytics/plugin_loader.py`). Owns the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:39:     `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` + `exec_module`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:40:   - Subscribes once to `DataBroker` as `plugin_pipeline`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:43:   - Hot reload via 5 s mtime polling (`_watch_loop`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:51:- `numpy`/`scipy` allowed inside plugins (they live outside `core/`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:52:  and `drivers/`); the no-numpy rule for those layers stays.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:58:A minimal compliant plugin is ~10 lines:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:68:The pipeline gives the plugin a fresh batch every
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:69:`batch_interval_s` (default 1 s), capped at `_MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 500`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:70:readings. Returned metrics are republished into `DataBroker` as
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:71:synthetic `Reading`s; live broker subscribers (GUI, web, ZMQ, alarm
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:74:surface — `SQLiteWriter` runs in immediate mode from `Scheduler`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:79:- ABC: `analytics/base_plugin.py`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:80:- Pipeline: `analytics/plugin_loader.py`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:81:- Production plugin examples: `analytics/sensor_diagnostics.py`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:82:  `analytics/vacuum_trend.py`. (Cooldown predictor lives outside
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:84:  `core/cooldown_service.py`.)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:85:- Configuration: `config/plugins.yaml` controls which plugins are
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:92:must isolate exceptions explicitly — Python's default exception
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:93:flow is to crash the loop, and that is the wrong default for
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:98:- [[10 Subsystems/Plugin architecture]] — full subsystem page
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/30 Investigations/Plugin isolation rebuild.md:99:- [[40 Decisions/ADR-003 Plugin isolation via ABC]] — synthesized

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:18:| Data plane | PUB / SUB | `tcp://127.0.0.1:5555` (default; `ipc://` experiment in IV.7) | msgpack | Continuous reading stream + alarm events + safety state |

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:22:`ZMQPublisher` + `ZMQCommandServer` (`zmq_bridge.py`). Connection
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:23:order matters: SUB must `connect()` BEFORE `subscribe()`. The
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:24:inverse pattern produced zero received messages on macOS Python 3.14
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:25:+ pyzmq 25 (documented at `zmq_subprocess.py:97`).
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:29:The GUI process never imports `zmq` directly. Instead, `mp.Process`
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:30:spawns `zmq_bridge_main()` (`zmq_subprocess.py:56`), which owns ALL
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:32:through `mp.Queue` (data, cmd, reply). Rationale: libzmq
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:33:`signaler.cpp` assertion crash on Windows takes down only the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:38:- `sub_drain_loop` — owns SUB socket. Drains readings; emits
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:39:  heartbeat every 5 s into `data_queue` so the GUI can verify the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:41:- `cmd_forward_loop` — owns REQ socket. Pulls commands from
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:42:  `cmd_queue`, sends, awaits reply with `RCVTIMEO=35s`.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:44:## Per-command tiered handler timeouts (IV.3 Finding 7)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:47:_timeout_for(cmd))` (`zmq_bridge.py:60`):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:49:- Fast envelope **2.0 s** for status polls.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:50:- Slow envelope **30.0 s** for stateful operations

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:52:  `calibration_curve_import/export`, `calibration_v2_fit/extract`,

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:59:## Bind retry (Phase 2b H.4)

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:62:with exponential backoff (`0.5 s → 10 s`, max 10 attempts). On
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:64:to 240 s (TIME_WAIT). Caller must set `LINGER=0` BEFORE calling, or

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:71:unrecoverable. IV.6 implemented the canonical ZeroMQ Guide ch.4

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:85:~80 s uptime. Codex's shared-REQ-state hypothesis was empirically
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:86:falsified. See [[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]] and
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:87:[[30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis]].
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:89:## Watchdog cooldown hotfix (2026-04-20 evening)
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:91:The IV.6 watchdog had a regression: `_last_cmd_timeout` persisted
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:94:poll and was restarted again — restart storm (30–40
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:95:restarts/minute observed on Ubuntu lab PC). Hotfix added 60 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:97:`_last_cmd_watchdog_restart` timestamp + missing `return` after
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:98:restart so no further checks run in the same poll cycle. Does NOT
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:99:resolve B1; only prevents pathological amplification.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:101:## IV.7 ipc:// transport experiment
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:103:Branch `experiment/iv7-ipc-transport` switches the loopback default
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:104:to Unix-domain sockets (`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-pub.sock`,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:105:`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-cmd.sock`) on macOS / Linux; Windows retains
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:106:`tcp://`. Hypothesis: TCP-loopback layer is the remaining culprit
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:108:and async cancellation have all been ruled out.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:110:Outcome 2026-04-23 was originally a runtime failure attributed to
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:111:the transport. [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-b2b4fb5-investigation|H3]]
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:113:ipc:// bind, NOT the transport. Repaired via R1 (bounded-backoff
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:114:retry). After the repair lands, ipc:// runs normally for ~80 s
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:115:then B1 fires anyway — IV.7 does not close B1, only shifts the
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md:118:## Status (2026-04-26)

/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:23:VSP63D (default):
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:24:  9600 baud
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:29:  115200 baud
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:32:  6-digit value ABCDEF: pressure = (ABCD/1000) × 10^(EF − 20) mbar
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:35:`connect()` probes both. `_FALLBACK_BAUDRATES = {9600: 115200,
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:36:115200: 9600}` controls the fallback once per attempt. Actual probe
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:37:order in `thyracont_vsp63d.py:120`:
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:39:1. Try Protocol V1 probe (`_try_v1_probe`) at the configured baudrate.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:40:2. If fail, try MV00 probe (`_try_mv00_probe`) at the configured baudrate.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:41:3. If both fail, close transport and reopen at the alternate baudrate
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:42:   (per `_FALLBACK_BAUDRATES`); repeat the V1-then-MV00 sequence.
/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D.md:43:4. If still nothing responds, raise.

     1	"""CryoDAQ GUI theme tokens.
     2	
     3	Canonical source: docs/design-system/README.md
     4	Full token inventory: docs/design-system/MANIFEST.md
     5	Token naming convention: docs/design-system/governance/token-naming.md
     6	
     7	Runtime: color tokens are loaded at import time from a YAML pack via
     8	:mod:`cryodaq.gui._theme_loader`. Selection is persisted in
     9	``config/settings.local.yaml``; packs live in ``config/themes/*.yaml``.
    10	Non-color tokens (typography, spacing, radius, motion) stay hardcoded
    11	here — they don't theme.
    12	"""
    13	
    14	from __future__ import annotations
    15	
    16	import pyqtgraph as pg
    17	
    18	from cryodaq.gui._theme_loader import load_theme
    19	
    20	_pack = load_theme()
    21	
    22	
    23	def _hex_to_rgba(hex_color: str, alpha: float) -> str:
    24	    h = hex_color.lstrip("#")
    25	    r, g, b = int(h[0:2], 16), int(h[2:4], 16), int(h[4:6], 16)
    26	    return f"rgba({r}, {g}, {b}, {alpha})"
    27	
    28	
    29	# =============================================================================
    30	# COLORS — Base palette (from active theme pack)
    31	# =============================================================================
    32	
    33	BACKGROUND = _pack["BACKGROUND"]
    34	FOREGROUND = _pack["FOREGROUND"]
    35	
    36	SURFACE_PANEL = _pack["SURFACE_PANEL"]
    37	SURFACE_CARD = _pack["SURFACE_CARD"]
    38	SURFACE_ELEVATED = _pack["SURFACE_ELEVATED"]
    39	SURFACE_SUNKEN = _pack["SURFACE_SUNKEN"]
    40	SURFACE_MUTED = _pack["SURFACE_MUTED"]
    41	SURFACE_WINDOW = BACKGROUND
    42	SURFACE_BG = BACKGROUND
    43	SURFACE_OVERLAY_RGBA = _hex_to_rgba(BACKGROUND, 0.6)
    44	
    45	PRIMARY = SURFACE_PANEL
    46	SECONDARY = SURFACE_ELEVATED
    47	CARD = SURFACE_CARD
    48	MUTED = SURFACE_MUTED
    49	CARD_FOREGROUND = FOREGROUND
    50	
    51	BORDER = _pack["BORDER"]
    52	BORDER_SUBTLE = _pack["BORDER_SUBTLE"]
    53	BORDER_STRONG = BORDER
    54	
    55	ACCENT = _pack["ACCENT"]
    56	RING = ACCENT
    57	BORDER_FOCUS = ACCENT
    58	
    59	# =============================================================================
    60	# COLORS — Neutral interaction (Phase III.A, decoupled from status)
    61	# =============================================================================
    62	# For UI states that are NOT safety indicators: selected rows, focused
    63	# inputs, active tabs. Using STATUS_OK here creates semantic collision
    64	# (operator reads green as "safe" when it means "selected"). These
    65	# tokens are hue-neutral / luminance-shifted from surface.
    66	
    67	SELECTION_BG = _pack["SELECTION_BG"]
    68	FOCUS_RING = _pack["FOCUS_RING"]
    69	
    70	ON_PRIMARY = _pack["ON_PRIMARY"]

     6	
     7	# CryoDAQ Design System — Manifest
     8	
     9	**Generated:** 2026-04-17
    10	**Session:** Phase UI-1 v2 design system creation
    11	**Scope:** Batches 1-6 complete. Full design system v1.0.1 — foundation tokens + enforcement rules + generic components + CryoDAQ domain primitives + cross-surface patterns + accessibility commitments + governance policies.
    12	
    13	## Structure
    14	
    15	```
    16	design-system/
    17	├── README.md                           # Entry point, navigation, precedence rules
    18	├── MANIFEST.md                         # This file
    19	├── ANTI_PATTERNS.md                    # 40+ forbidden patterns with historical refs
    20	│
    21	├── tokens/                             # Foundation: 11 files, what exists and why
    22	│   ├── colors.md                       # 71 color tokens across 9 namespaces
    23	│   ├── typography.md                   # 36 typography tokens, Fira fonts, Cyrillic rules
    24	│   ├── spacing.md                      # 9 spacing tokens + semantic aliases
    25	│   ├── radius.md                       # 5 radius tokens, tight scale
    26	│   ├── layout.md                       # 5 layout tokens, coupled constants
    27	│   ├── chart-tokens.md                 # pyqtgraph integration
    28	│   ├── motion.md                       # PROPOSED — duration/easing tokens
    29	│   ├── elevation.md                    # zero-shadow policy + z-index levels
    30	│   ├── icons.md                        # Lucide bundle + emoji prohibition
    31	│   ├── breakpoints.md                  # desktop-only responsive
    32	│   └── keyboard-shortcuts.md           # PROPOSED — shortcut registry
    33	│
    34	├── rules/                              # Enforcement: 9 files, 79 rules with code examples
    35	│   ├── color-rules.md                  # COLOR-001..010
    36	│   ├── surface-rules.md                # SURF-001..010
    37	│   ├── typography-rules.md             # TYPO-001..010
    38	│   ├── spacing-rules.md                # SPACE-001..008
    39	│   ├── interaction-rules.md            # INTER-001..012
    40	│   ├── data-display-rules.md           # DATA-001..010
    41	│   ├── accessibility-rules.md          # A11Y-001..008
    42	│   ├── content-voice-rules.md          # COPY-001..008
    43	│   └── governance-rules.md             # GOV-001..003 (thin pointers to governance/*)
    44	│
    45	└── components/                         # Generic primitives: 14 files, anatomy + invariants + code
    46	    ├── card.md                         # generic rounded container
    47	    ├── button.md                       # secondary/ghost/destructive/icon/hold-confirm
    48	    ├── input-field.md                  # text/numeric/search/password + validation
    49	    ├── badge.md                        # filled/outline/count/inline/phase
    50	    ├── bento-grid.md                   # 8-column layout engine
    51	    ├── bento-tile.md                   # grid child primitive + KPI/DataDense/Live subclasses
    52	    ├── modal.md                        # centered overlay with backdrop
    53	    ├── popover.md                      # anchored floating panel
    54	    ├── dialog.md                       # title/body/actions Q&A
    55	    ├── drawer.md                       # edge-attached sliding panel
    56	    ├── toast.md                        # transient notifications
    57	    ├── breadcrumb.md                   # drill-down navigation trail
    58	    ├── tab-group.md                    # sibling view switcher
    59	    └── chart-tile.md                   # BentoTile + pyqtgraph variant
    60	
    61	└── cryodaq-primitives/                 # Domain-specific: 9 files, Phase II implementation guides
    62	    ├── top-watch-bar.md                # 4 vitals + mode badge (B.4 + B.4.5.2)
    63	    ├── tool-rail.md                    # left icon nav (Phase 0)
    64	    ├── bottom-status-bar.md            # system status strip
    65	    ├── sensor-cell.md                  # single-channel cell (B.3)
    66	    ├── phase-stepper.md                # 6-phase stepper (B.5/B.5.5/B.5.6)
    67	    ├── alarm-badge.md                  # header alarm indicator
    68	    ├── experiment-card.md              # active-experiment dashboard tile + overlay
    69	    ├── quick-log-block.md              # inline operator log widget
    70	    └── keithley-panel.md               # dual-channel SMU control
    71	
    72	└── patterns/                           # Cross-surface patterns: 9 files, composition recipes
    73	    ├── page-scaffolds.md               # 3 canonical scaffolds (Bento / Single-panel / Split)

    75	    ├── cross-surface-consistency.md    # 5 consistency dimensions + two-surface test
    76	    ├── responsive-behavior.md          # desktop-only, 3 viewport bands, what adapts

     1	---
     2	source: CLAUDE.md; README.md; PROJECT_STATUS.md (2026-04-19); pyproject.toml; live wc/find counts (2026-04-26)
     3	last_synced: 2026-04-26
     4	status: synthesized
     5	---
     6	
     7	# What is CryoDAQ
     8	
     9	## What it is
    10	
    11	CryoDAQ is the data acquisition and control stack for a cryogenic
    12	laboratory at АКЦ ФИАН (the Millimetron telescope project). It
    13	replaces the previous LabVIEW system with a Python 3.12+ codebase
    14	built on `asyncio` and `PySide6`. Operator-facing language is Russian.
    15	
    16	## What it does
    17	
    18	End-to-end laboratory workflow:
    19	
    20	- Continuous acquisition from three instrument families
    21	  ([[00 Overview/Hardware setup]])
    22	- Safety-critical source on/off authority via the
    23	  [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]] (single-authority `SafetyManager`,
    24	  source OFF by default, fail-on-silence in RUNNING)
    25	- Persistence-first data path
    26	  ([[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first]] — SQLite WAL commit before
    27	  any subscriber sees a reading)
    28	- ZMQ-bridged operator GUI ([[10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge]])
    29	- Experiment lifecycle (templates, phases, finalize, archive,
    30	  auto-report)
    31	- Calibration v2 — continuous SRDG capture, three-mode UI, post-run
    32	  Chebyshev fit ([[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]])
    33	- YAML-driven alarm engine v2 ([[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]]),
    34	  interlocks, housekeeping, telegram notifications
    35	- Optional FastAPI web dashboard for monitoring
    36	
    37	## Where it runs
    38	
    39	Three runtime contours, all on a single lab PC (Linux Ubuntu 22.04
    40	production / macOS dev):
    41	
    42	1. `cryodaq-engine` — headless asyncio runtime: drivers, scheduler,
    43	   persistence, safety, alarms, plugins. Survives GUI restart.
    44	2. `cryodaq-gui` (or `cryodaq` Windows launcher) — desktop operator
    45	   client. Connects to engine over ZMQ; can be killed/restarted
    46	   without stopping data.
    47	3. `cryodaq.web.server:app` — optional FastAPI monitoring on `:8080`.
    48	
    49	ZeroMQ topology: PUB/SUB `:5555` for data (msgpack), REP/REQ `:5556`
    50	for commands (JSON). The bridge subprocess isolates engine from GUI
    51	crashes ([[10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge]]).
    52	
    53	## Who maintains it
    54	
    55	- **Architect:** Vladimir (also project owner; user of this vault).
    56	- **Implementation:** Claude Code as primary implementer with
    57	  Codex / Gemini as adversarial reviewers
    58	  ([[50 Workflow/Multi-model consultation]],
    59	  [[50 Workflow/ORCHESTRATION contract]]).
    60	- **Pipeline:** CC tactical → Codex line-level review → optional
    61	  Jules architectural review on safety-critical changes
    62	  ([[50 Workflow/Calibration loops history]]).
    63	
    64	## Scale (2026-04-26)
    65	
    66	| Metric | Value | Source |
    67	|---|---|---|
    68	| Python files (`src/cryodaq/`) | **145** | `find` |
    69	| Lines of code (`src/cryodaq/`) | **47 610** | `wc -l` |
    70	| Test files (`tests/`) | **194** | `find tests -name '*.py' -not -path '*/__pycache__/*'` |
    71	| Test LOC (`tests/`) | **36 002** | `wc -l` |
    72	| Tests passing (post-IV.6) | 1 775 / 1 776 (1 pre-existing flaky) | `CHANGELOG.md` `be51a24` — full subtree count |
    73	| Tests passing (last formal measurement, older) | 1 368 / 2 skipped | `PROJECT_STATUS.md` 2026-04-19 |
    74	| Coverage (full suite, stale) | 66 % | last measured 2026-04-17 |
    75	| `pyproject.toml` version | 0.34.0.dev0 | `pyproject.toml` |
    76	| Latest version git tag | v0.33.0 | `git tag -l` (also: non-package marker `design-system-v1.0.1`) |
    77	| Design system | v1.0.1 (67 .md, 139 tokens) | `docs/design-system/` |
    78	| Python | 3.12+ (dev: 3.14.3) | `CLAUDE.md` |
    79	| Instruments | 3 LakeShore + 1 Keithley + 1 Thyracont | [[00 Overview/Hardware setup]] |
    80	
    81	LOC count is `wc -l` over `*.py` only and excludes `__pycache__/`.
    82	Test pass count is from the last published session ledger; live count
    83	likely higher (several feature batches merged since IV.4 close).
    84	
    85	## Where it lives
    86	
    87	- **Repo:** `~/Projects/cryodaq/` (this vault is read-only with
    88	  respect to the repo)
    89	- **Data:** `data/data_*.db` (SQLite WAL, daily rotation),
    90	  `data/experiments/<id>/`, `data/calibration/`
    91	- **Vault:** `~/Vault/CryoDAQ/` (this knowledge base)
    92	- **TSP scripts (drafts, not loaded):** `tsp/p_const.lua`
    93	
    94	## See also
    95	
    96	- [[00 Overview/Architecture overview]]
    97	- [[60 Roadmap/Versions]]
    98	- [[60 Roadmap/F-table backlog]]

PROJECT_STATUS.md:160:14 commits, +61 tests (829 → 890), zero regressions. Triple-reviewer pipeline (CC tactical + Codex second-opinion + Jules architectural) валидирован на Safety, Persistence и Config Fail-Closed subsystems.
PROJECT_STATUS.md:183:Codex FAIL on II.6 surfaced a systemic risk: overlays with public push setters (`set_connected`, `set_current_experiment`, `set_safety_ready`, etc.) are useless if `MainWindowV2` never calls them — the overlay opens in defaults and stays there. Unit tests on the overlay alone pass while production is broken.
PROJECT_STATUS.md:191:**Tests:** overlay unit tests AND host integration tests (`tests/gui/shell/test_main_window_v2_<block>_wiring.py`) that exercise `MainWindowV2` entry points end-to-end — firing the signal / setting the cache / calling `_ensure_overlay` and asserting overlay state.

CLAUDE.md:373:- **Amend-fix commits** (post-Codex-review surgical patches, 1-3 files changed, < 100 LOC delta) run ONLY targeted tests: `ruff check <touched files>` + `pytest <touched test files>`. The full suite naturally runs at the start of the next block's initial commit; regression detection is NOT lost, it's deferred by one block.
CLAUDE.md:375:- Exception: if the amend touches a module imported by many non-test files (e.g. `main_window_v2.py`, `engine.py`, `safety_manager.py`), run the full suite. Judgment call.
CLAUDE.md:379:- **Python source / Markdown / YAML source in repo** — UTF-8 **без BOM** (стандарт Python 3; все исходники в `src/`, `tests/`, `docs/`, `config/` свободны от BOM). Проверено `file src/cryodaq/gui/shell/overlays/*.py` и hex-head `head -c 3 file.py` → `"""` / `---`, не `EF BB BF`.
CLAUDE.md:381:- Не добавлять BOM к Python-файлам / markdown-спекам / YAML-конфигам. Если внешний обзор флагует BOM-инвариант для source — это misapplication; ссылка на этот раздел.

/opt/homebrew/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba '/Users/vladimir/Vault/CryoDAQ/10 Subsystems/ZMQ bridge.md' | sed -n '1,150p'" in /Users/vladimir/Projects/cryodaq
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	source: src/cryodaq/core/zmq_bridge.py; src/cryodaq/core/zmq_subprocess.py; docs/bug_B1_zmq_idle_death_handoff.md; CHANGELOG IPC/REP hardening; ROADMAP "Known broken"
     3	last_synced: 2026-04-26
     4	status: synthesized
     5	---
     6	
     7	# ZMQ bridge
     8	
     9	The ZMQ bridge is the engine ↔ GUI IPC layer. It carries two
    10	independent channels and lives partly in the engine (PUB / REP
    11	sockets) and partly in a dedicated GUI-side subprocess that the GUI
    12	can survive losing.
    13	
    14	## Topology
    15	
    16	| Channel | Pattern | Address | Format | Purpose |
    17	|---|---|---|---|---|
    18	| Data plane | PUB / SUB | `tcp://127.0.0.1:5555` (default; `ipc://` experiment in IV.7) | msgpack | Continuous reading stream + alarm events + safety state |
    19	| Command plane | REP / REQ | `tcp://127.0.0.1:5556` | JSON | Operator commands, status queries |
    20	
    21	Topic for data plane is `b"readings"`. The engine PUBs are owned by
    22	`ZMQPublisher` + `ZMQCommandServer` (`zmq_bridge.py`). Connection
    23	order matters: SUB must `connect()` BEFORE `subscribe()`. The
    24	inverse pattern produced zero received messages on macOS Python 3.14
    25	+ pyzmq 25 (documented at `zmq_subprocess.py:97`).
    26	
    27	## Subprocess isolation (GUI side)
    28	
    29	The GUI process never imports `zmq` directly. Instead, `mp.Process`
    30	spawns `zmq_bridge_main()` (`zmq_subprocess.py:56`), which owns ALL
    31	ZMQ sockets on the GUI side and communicates with the GUI main process
    32	through `mp.Queue` (data, cmd, reply). Rationale: libzmq
    33	`signaler.cpp` assertion crash on Windows takes down only the
    34	subprocess; the GUI itself survives.
    35	
    36	Two threads inside the subprocess:
    37	
    38	- `sub_drain_loop` — owns SUB socket. Drains readings; emits
    39	  heartbeat every 5 s into `data_queue` so the GUI can verify the
    40	  *data path* is alive (not just that the subprocess exists).
    41	- `cmd_forward_loop` — owns REQ socket. Pulls commands from
    42	  `cmd_queue`, sends, awaits reply with `RCVTIMEO=35s`.
    43	
    44	## Per-command tiered handler timeouts (IV.3 Finding 7)
    45	
    46	Engine REP wraps each command handler in `asyncio.wait_for(timeout=
    47	_timeout_for(cmd))` (`zmq_bridge.py:60`):
    48	
    49	- Fast envelope **2.0 s** for status polls.
    50	- Slow envelope **30.0 s** for stateful operations
    51	  (`experiment_finalize`, `experiment_create`, `experiment_abort`,
    52	  `calibration_curve_import/export`, `calibration_v2_fit/extract`,
    53	  etc.).
    54	
    55	Handlers that exceed the envelope return `{"ok": False, "error":
    56	"handler timeout"}` rather than silent exception — REP socket
    57	state-machine sensitivity demands a paired send for every recv.
    58	
    59	## Bind retry (Phase 2b H.4)
    60	
    61	`_bind_with_retry()` (`zmq_bridge.py:84`) retries on `EADDRINUSE`
    62	with exponential backoff (`0.5 s → 10 s`, max 10 attempts). On
    63	Windows a socket from a SIGKILL'd engine can hold the port for up
    64	to 240 s (TIME_WAIT). Caller must set `LINGER=0` BEFORE calling, or
    65	`close()` will hold the address even after retry succeeds.
    66	
    67	## IV.6 ephemeral REQ + watchdog (`be51a24`, 2026-04-20)
    68	
    69	Codex's revised root-cause hypothesis for B1 was that one long-lived
    70	REQ socket in `cmd_forward_loop()` accumulates state and becomes
    71	unrecoverable. IV.6 implemented the canonical ZeroMQ Guide ch.4
    72	"poll / timeout / close / reopen" pattern:
    73	
    74	- Per-command ephemeral REQ socket (`_new_req_socket()` per send).
    75	- `REQ_RELAXED` and `REQ_CORRELATE` removed (unnecessary with
    76	  ephemeral sockets).
    77	- Launcher-side `command_channel_stalled()` watchdog in
    78	  `_poll_bridge_data` — restarts the bridge subprocess if the cmd
    79	  plane is stalled while data plane stays alive.
    80	- `TCP_KEEPALIVE` reverted on command + PUB paths (kept on
    81	  `sub_drain_loop` SUB as orthogonal safeguard).
    82	
    83	**Outcome:** code stays in master as defense-in-depth and matches
    84	canonical reliable req-reply pattern, **but B1 still reproduces** at
    85	~80 s uptime. Codex's shared-REQ-state hypothesis was empirically
    86	falsified. See [[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]] and
    87	[[30 Investigations/Codex H2 wrong hypothesis]].
    88	
    89	## Watchdog cooldown hotfix (2026-04-20 evening)
    90	
    91	The IV.6 watchdog had a regression: `_last_cmd_timeout` persisted
    92	across watchdog-triggered subprocess restart, so the fresh
    93	subprocess immediately saw a stale timeout signal on the next
    94	poll and was restarted again — restart storm (30–40
    95	restarts/minute observed on Ubuntu lab PC). Hotfix added 60 s
    96	cooldown between command-watchdog restarts via
    97	`_last_cmd_watchdog_restart` timestamp + missing `return` after
    98	restart so no further checks run in the same poll cycle. Does NOT
    99	resolve B1; only prevents pathological amplification.
   100	
   101	## IV.7 ipc:// transport experiment
   102	
   103	Branch `experiment/iv7-ipc-transport` switches the loopback default
   104	to Unix-domain sockets (`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-pub.sock`,
   105	`ipc:///tmp/cryodaq-cmd.sock`) on macOS / Linux; Windows retains
   106	`tcp://`. Hypothesis: TCP-loopback layer is the remaining culprit
   107	since shared-REQ-state, mp.Queue back-pressure, engine REP wedge,
   108	and async cancellation have all been ruled out.
   109	
   110	Outcome 2026-04-23 was originally a runtime failure attributed to
   111	the transport. [[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-b2b4fb5-investigation|H3]]
   112	confirmed the failure was the b2b4fb5 hardening probe racing the
   113	ipc:// bind, NOT the transport. Repaired via R1 (bounded-backoff
   114	retry). After the repair lands, ipc:// runs normally for ~80 s
   115	then B1 fires anyway — IV.7 does not close B1, only shifts the
   116	failure surface.
   117	
   118	## Status (2026-04-26)
   119	
   120	- B1 ZMQ idle-death — still OPEN. Blocks `v0.34.0` tag. See
   121	  [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]].
   122	- IV.6 partial mitigation — landed, defense-in-depth.
   123	- IV.7 ipc:// — viable transport, but does not close B1. Next
   124	  hypotheses: H4 (pyzmq 25.x + Python 3.14 asyncio integration),
   125	  H5 (engine REP state machine).
   126	
   127	## See also
   128	
   129	- [[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]]
   130	- [[30 Investigations/IV.6 cmd plane hardening]]
   131	- [[30 Investigations/b2b4fb5 hardening race]]
   132	- [[40 Decisions/ADR-002 R1 bounded-backoff probe retry]]

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"""ZMQ-мост между engine и GUI.
     2	
     3	ZMQPublisher — PUB-сокет в engine, сериализует Reading через msgpack.
     4	ZMQSubscriber — SUB-сокет в GUI-процессе, десериализует и вызывает callback.
     5	ZMQCommandServer — REP-сокет в engine, принимает JSON-команды от GUI.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	from __future__ import annotations
     9	
    10	import asyncio
    11	import errno
    12	import json
    13	import logging
    14	import time
    15	from collections.abc import Callable
    16	from datetime import UTC, datetime
    17	from typing import Any
    18	
    19	import msgpack
    20	import zmq
    21	import zmq.asyncio
    22	
    23	from cryodaq.drivers.base import ChannelStatus, Reading
    24	
    25	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    26	
    27	DEFAULT_PUB_ADDR = "tcp://127.0.0.1:5555"
    28	DEFAULT_CMD_ADDR = "tcp://127.0.0.1:5556"
    29	DEFAULT_TOPIC = b"readings"
    30	
    31	# IV.3 Finding 7: per-command tiered handler timeout.
    32	# A flat 2 s envelope was wrong for stateful transitions —
    33	# experiment_finalize / abort / create and calibration curve
    34	# import/export/fit routinely exceed 2 s (SQLite writes + DOCX/PDF
    35	# report generation). When they timed out the outer REP reply path
    36	# still fired (the original code already returned {ok: False}), but
    37	# the operator saw a "handler timeout (2s)" error that was a lie:
    38	# the operation usually completed a few seconds later. Fast status
    39	# polls stay on the 2 s envelope; known-slow commands get 30 s.
    40	HANDLER_TIMEOUT_FAST_S = 2.0
    41	HANDLER_TIMEOUT_SLOW_S = 30.0
    42	
    43	_SLOW_COMMANDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
    44	    {
    45	        "experiment_finalize",
    46	        "experiment_stop",
    47	        "experiment_abort",
    48	        "experiment_create",
    49	        "experiment_create_retroactive",
    50	        "experiment_start",
    51	        "experiment_generate_report",
    52	        "calibration_curve_import",
    53	        "calibration_curve_export",
    54	        "calibration_v2_fit",
    55	        "calibration_v2_extract",
    56	    }
    57	)
    58	
    59	
    60	def _timeout_for(cmd: Any) -> float:
    61	    """Return the handler timeout envelope for ``cmd``.
    62	
    63	    Slow commands get ``HANDLER_TIMEOUT_SLOW_S``; everything else
    64	    gets ``HANDLER_TIMEOUT_FAST_S``. Unknown / malformed payloads
    65	    fall back to fast — a cmd that isn't in the slow set must not
    66	    trigger the longer wait by accident.
    67	    """
    68	    if not isinstance(cmd, dict):
    69	        return HANDLER_TIMEOUT_FAST_S
    70	    action = cmd.get("cmd")
    71	    if isinstance(action, str) and action in _SLOW_COMMANDS:
    72	        return HANDLER_TIMEOUT_SLOW_S
    73	    return HANDLER_TIMEOUT_FAST_S
    74	
    75	
    76	# Phase 2b H.4: bind with EADDRINUSE retry. On Windows the socket from a
    77	# SIGKILL'd engine can hold the port for up to 240s (TIME_WAIT). Linux is
    78	# usually fine due to SO_REUSEADDR but the same logic protects both.
    79	_BIND_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 10
    80	_BIND_INITIAL_DELAY_S = 0.5
    81	_BIND_MAX_DELAY_S = 10.0
    82	
    83	
    84	def _bind_with_retry(socket: Any, address: str) -> None:
    85	    """Bind a ZMQ socket, retrying on EADDRINUSE with exponential backoff.
    86	
    87	    Caller MUST set ``zmq.LINGER = 0`` on the socket BEFORE calling this
    88	    helper, otherwise close() will hold the address even after retry succeeds.
    89	    """
    90	    delay = _BIND_INITIAL_DELAY_S
    91	    for attempt in range(_BIND_MAX_ATTEMPTS):
    92	        try:
    93	            socket.bind(address)
    94	            if attempt > 0:
    95	                logger.info(
    96	                    "ZMQ bound to %s after %d retries",
    97	                    address,
    98	                    attempt,
    99	                )
   100	            return
   101	        except zmq.ZMQError as exc:
   102	            # libzmq maps EADDRINUSE to its own errno value.
   103	            is_addr_in_use = exc.errno == zmq.EADDRINUSE or exc.errno == errno.EADDRINUSE
   104	            if not is_addr_in_use:
   105	                raise
   106	            if attempt == _BIND_MAX_ATTEMPTS - 1:
   107	                logger.critical(
   108	                    "ZMQ bind FAILED after %d attempts: %s still in use. "
   109	                    "Check for stale sockets via lsof/netstat.",
   110	                    _BIND_MAX_ATTEMPTS,
   111	                    address,
   112	                )
   113	                raise
   114	            logger.warning(
   115	                "ZMQ bind EADDRINUSE on %s, retry in %.1fs (attempt %d/%d)",
   116	                address,
   117	                delay,
   118	                attempt + 1,
   119	                _BIND_MAX_ATTEMPTS,
   120	            )
   121	            time.sleep(delay)
   122	            delay = min(delay * 2, _BIND_MAX_DELAY_S)
   123	
   124	
   125	def _pack_reading(reading: Reading) -> bytes:
   126	    """Сериализовать Reading в msgpack."""
   127	    data = {
   128	        "ts": reading.timestamp.timestamp(),
   129	        "iid": reading.instrument_id,
   130	        "ch": reading.channel,
   131	        "v": reading.value,
   132	        "u": reading.unit,
   133	        "st": reading.status.value,
   134	        "raw": reading.raw,
   135	        "meta": reading.metadata,
   136	    }
   137	    return msgpack.packb(data, use_bin_type=True)
   138	
   139	
   140	def _unpack_reading(payload: bytes) -> Reading:
   141	    """Десериализовать Reading из msgpack."""
   142	    data = msgpack.unpackb(payload, raw=False)
   143	    return Reading(
   144	        timestamp=datetime.fromtimestamp(data["ts"], tz=UTC),
   145	        instrument_id=data.get("iid", ""),
   146	        channel=data["ch"],
   147	        value=data["v"],
   148	        unit=data["u"],
   149	        status=ChannelStatus(data["st"]),
   150	        raw=data.get("raw"),
   151	        metadata=data.get("meta", {}),
   152	    )
   153	
   154	
   155	class ZMQPublisher:
   156	    """PUB-сокет: engine публикует Reading для GUI и внешних подписчиков.
   157	
   158	    Использование::
   159	
   160	        pub = ZMQPublisher("tcp://127.0.0.1:5555")
   161	        await pub.start(queue)   # asyncio.Queue[Reading] от DataBroker
   162	        ...
   163	        await pub.stop()
   164	    """
   165	
   166	    def __init__(self, address: str = DEFAULT_PUB_ADDR, *, topic: bytes = DEFAULT_TOPIC) -> None:
   167	        self._address = address
   168	        self._topic = topic
   169	        self._ctx: zmq.asyncio.Context | None = None
   170	        self._socket: zmq.asyncio.Socket | None = None
   171	        self._task: asyncio.Task[None] | None = None
   172	        self._running = False
   173	        self._total_sent: int = 0
   174	
   175	    async def _publish_loop(self, queue: asyncio.Queue[Reading]) -> None:
   176	        while self._running:
   177	            try:
   178	                reading = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=1.0)
   179	            except TimeoutError:
   180	                continue
   181	            try:
   182	                payload = _pack_reading(reading)
   183	                await self._socket.send_multipart([self._topic, payload])
   184	                self._total_sent += 1
   185	            except Exception:
   186	                logger.exception("Ошибка отправки ZMQ")
   187	
   188	    async def start(self, queue: asyncio.Queue[Reading]) -> None:
   189	        self._ctx = zmq.asyncio.Context()
   190	        self._socket = self._ctx.socket(zmq.PUB)
   191	        # Phase 2b H.4: LINGER=0 so the socket doesn't hold the port open
   192	        # after close — relevant on Windows where TIME_WAIT can keep
   193	        # 5555 occupied for 240s after a SIGKILL'd engine.
   194	        self._socket.setsockopt(zmq.LINGER, 0)
   195	        # IV.6: TCP_KEEPALIVE previously added here on the idle-reap
   196	        # hypothesis (commit f5f9039). Codex revised analysis disproved
   197	        # that — Ubuntu 120 s deterministic failure with default
   198	        # tcp_keepalive_time=7200 s rules out kernel reaping. Keepalive
   199	        # reverted on the command path (REQ + REP); retained on the
   200	        # SUB drain path in zmq_subprocess.sub_drain_loop as an
   201	        # orthogonal safeguard for long between-experiment pauses.
   202	        _bind_with_retry(self._socket, self._address)
   203	        self._running = True
   204	        self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._publish_loop(queue), name="zmq_publisher")
   205	        logger.info("ZMQPublisher запущен: %s", self._address)
   206	
   207	    async def stop(self) -> None:
   208	        self._running = False
   209	        if self._task:
   210	            self._task.cancel()
   211	            try:
   212	                await self._task
   213	            except asyncio.CancelledError:
   214	                pass
   215	            self._task = None
   216	        if self._socket:
   217	            self._socket.close(linger=0)
   218	            self._socket = None
   219	        if self._ctx:
   220	            self._ctx.term()

     1	"""ZMQ bridge running in a separate process.
     2	
     3	This process owns ALL ZMQ sockets. If libzmq crashes (signaler.cpp
     4	assertion on Windows), only this subprocess dies. The GUI detects
     5	the death via is_alive() and restarts it.
     6	
     7	The GUI process never imports zmq.
     8	
     9	Threading model (see fix(gui): split bridge subprocess ...):
    10	- sub_drain owns the SUB socket, receives readings, emits heartbeats.
    11	  Heartbeat comes from this thread so it proves the *data* path is alive.
    12	- cmd_forward owns the REQ socket, sends commands and waits up to 3s
    13	  per reply. May block; does not affect sub_drain.
    14	- Main thread starts both threads and waits on shutdown_event.
    15	"""
    16	
    17	from __future__ import annotations
    18	
    19	import contextlib
    20	import json
    21	import logging
    22	import multiprocessing as mp
    23	import queue
    24	import threading
    25	import time
    26	from typing import Any
    27	
    28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    29	
    30	# Re-export constants so GUI code doesn't need to import zmq_bridge
    31	DEFAULT_PUB_ADDR = "tcp://127.0.0.1:5555"
    32	DEFAULT_CMD_ADDR = "tcp://127.0.0.1:5556"
    33	# Mirror of zmq_bridge.DEFAULT_TOPIC. Duplicated (not imported) because this
    34	# module is loaded in the GUI process, which must not import zmq/zmq_bridge
    35	# at module scope. Keep in sync with cryodaq.core.zmq_bridge.DEFAULT_TOPIC.
    36	DEFAULT_TOPIC = b"readings"
    37	
    38	
    39	def _unpack_reading_dict(payload: bytes) -> dict[str, Any]:
    40	    """Unpack msgpack Reading into a plain dict (picklable for mp.Queue)."""
    41	    import msgpack
    42	
    43	    data = msgpack.unpackb(payload, raw=False)
    44	    return {
    45	        "timestamp": data["ts"],
    46	        "instrument_id": data.get("iid", ""),
    47	        "channel": data["ch"],
    48	        "value": data["v"],
    49	        "unit": data["u"],
    50	        "status": data["st"],
    51	        "raw": data.get("raw"),
    52	        "metadata": data.get("meta", {}),
    53	    }
    54	
    55	
    56	def zmq_bridge_main(
    57	    pub_addr: str,
    58	    cmd_addr: str,
    59	    data_queue: mp.Queue,
    60	    cmd_queue: mp.Queue,
    61	    reply_queue: mp.Queue,
    62	    shutdown_event: mp.Event,
    63	) -> None:
    64	    """Entry point for ZMQ bridge subprocess.
    65	
    66	    Parameters
    67	    ----------
    68	    pub_addr:
    69	        Engine PUB address, e.g. "tcp://127.0.0.1:5555".
    70	    cmd_addr:
    71	        Engine REP address, e.g. "tcp://127.0.0.1:5556".
    72	    data_queue:
    73	        Subprocess → GUI: Reading dicts plus control messages
    74	        (``__type`` in {"heartbeat", "warning"}).
    75	    cmd_queue:
    76	        GUI → subprocess: command dicts to send via REQ.
    77	    reply_queue:
    78	        Subprocess → GUI: command reply dicts.
    79	    shutdown_event:
    80	        Set by GUI to signal clean shutdown.
    81	    """
    82	    import zmq
    83	
    84	    HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = 5.0  # seconds — keep generous vs is_healthy() threshold
    85	
    86	    ctx = zmq.Context()
    87	
    88	    dropped_counter = {"n": 0}
    89	
    90	    def sub_drain_loop() -> None:
    91	        """Own SUB socket; drain readings and emit periodic heartbeats.
    92	
    93	        Heartbeat comes from this thread (not the command thread) so
    94	        the GUI's heartbeat freshness check proves the *data* path is
    95	        alive, not just that the subprocess exists.
    96	        """
    97	        # Order matters: connect() BEFORE subscribe(). The inverse pattern
    98	        # (subscribe-before-connect with setsockopt_string(SUBSCRIBE, "")) produced
    99	        # zero received messages on macOS Python 3.14 pyzmq 25+.
   100	        sub = ctx.socket(zmq.SUB)
   101	        sub.setsockopt(zmq.LINGER, 0)
   102	        sub.setsockopt(zmq.RCVTIMEO, 100)
   103	        # 2026-04-20 idle-death fix: same keepalive as REQ side to
   104	        # survive macOS kernel idle reaping. SUB normally gets a
   105	        # stream of readings so idle is rare, but between-experiment
   106	        # quiet periods exist (scheduler paused, no active polls).
   107	        sub.setsockopt(zmq.TCP_KEEPALIVE, 1)
   108	        sub.setsockopt(zmq.TCP_KEEPALIVE_IDLE, 10)
   109	        sub.setsockopt(zmq.TCP_KEEPALIVE_INTVL, 5)
   110	        sub.setsockopt(zmq.TCP_KEEPALIVE_CNT, 3)
   111	        sub.connect(pub_addr)
   112	        sub.subscribe(DEFAULT_TOPIC)
   113	        last_heartbeat = time.monotonic()
   114	        try:
   115	            while not shutdown_event.is_set():
   116	                # SUB: blocking receive with 100ms RCVTIMEO. Keeps the loop
   117	                # responsive for shutdown and heartbeat emission.
   118	                try:
   119	                    parts = sub.recv_multipart()
   120	                    if len(parts) == 2:
   121	                        try:
   122	                            reading_dict = _unpack_reading_dict(parts[1])
   123	                        except Exception:
   124	                            reading_dict = None  # skip malformed
   125	                        if reading_dict is not None:
   126	                            try:
   127	                                data_queue.put_nowait(reading_dict)
   128	                            except queue.Full:
   129	                                dropped_counter["n"] += 1
   130	                                if dropped_counter["n"] % 100 == 1:
   131	                                    with contextlib.suppress(queue.Full):
   132	                                        data_queue.put_nowait(
   133	                                            {
   134	                                                "__type": "warning",
   135	                                                "message": (
   136	                                                    f"Queue overflow: "
   137	                                                    f"{dropped_counter['n']} readings dropped"
   138	                                                ),
   139	                                            }
   140	                                        )
   141	                except zmq.Again:
   142	                    pass
   143	                except zmq.ZMQError:
   144	                    if shutdown_event.is_set():
   145	                        break
   146	                    # Unexpected socket error — swallow and continue.
   147	                    time.sleep(0.01)
   148	
   149	                now = time.monotonic()
   150	                if now - last_heartbeat >= HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL:
   151	                    with contextlib.suppress(queue.Full):
   152	                        data_queue.put_nowait({"__type": "heartbeat", "ts": now})
   153	                    last_heartbeat = now
   154	        finally:
   155	            sub.close(linger=0)
   156	
   157	    def cmd_forward_loop() -> None:
   158	        """Forward GUI commands via a fresh REQ socket per command.
   159	
   160	        IV.6 B1 fix: each command creates, uses, and closes its own REQ
   161	        socket. Shared long-lived REQ accumulated state across commands
   162	        and became permanently unrecoverable after a platform-specific
   163	        trigger (macOS sparse cadence within ~minutes, Ubuntu 120 s
   164	        deterministic). Ephemeral REQ per command matches ZeroMQ Guide
   165	        ch.4 canonical "poll / timeout / close / reopen" reliable
   166	        request-reply pattern.
   167	
   168	        May block up to 35 s per timed-out REQ. That does not starve
   169	        the data path because SUB drain runs on a separate thread.
   170	        A timed-out REQ emits a structured ``cmd_timeout`` control
   171	        message via data_queue so the launcher watchdog can detect
   172	        command-channel-only failures and restart the bridge.
   173	        """
   174	
   175	        def _new_req_socket():
   176	            """Build a fresh per-command REQ socket.
   177	
   178	            IV.6: REQ_RELAXED / REQ_CORRELATE dropped — they were only
   179	            useful for stateful recovery on a shared socket, which the
   180	            ephemeral model has eliminated. TCP_KEEPALIVE dropped from
   181	            the command path (reverting the f5f9039 partial fix) —
   182	            Codex revised analysis confirmed idle-reap was not the
   183	            actual cause; keepalive is a no-op here and clutters
   184	            debugging of the real socket state.
   185	            """
   186	            req = ctx.socket(zmq.REQ)
   187	            req.setsockopt(zmq.LINGER, 0)
   188	            # IV.3 Finding 7: REQ timeout stays at 35 s so a slow
   189	            # server-side handler (experiment_finalize / report
   190	            # generation, tiered at 30 s) has room to reply before
   191	            # the REQ side gives up. Server's 30 s ceiling + 5 s slack
   192	            # stays inside the client's 35 s future wait
   193	            # (_CMD_REPLY_TIMEOUT_S), so timeouts at each layer fire
   194	            # in predictable order: server → subprocess → GUI future.
   195	            req.setsockopt(zmq.RCVTIMEO, 35000)
   196	            req.setsockopt(zmq.SNDTIMEO, 35000)
   197	            req.connect(cmd_addr)
   198	            return req
   199	
   200	        while not shutdown_event.is_set():
   201	            try:
   202	                cmd = cmd_queue.get(timeout=0.5)
   203	            except queue.Empty:
   204	                continue
   205	            rid = cmd.pop("_rid", None) if isinstance(cmd, dict) else None
   206	            cmd_type = cmd.get("cmd", "?") if isinstance(cmd, dict) else "?"
   207	
   208	            # Fresh socket per command — no shared state across commands.
   209	            req = _new_req_socket()
   210	            try:
   211	                try:
   212	                    req.send_string(json.dumps(cmd))
   213	                    reply_raw = req.recv_string()
   214	                    reply = json.loads(reply_raw)
   215	                except zmq.ZMQError as exc:
   216	                    reply = {"ok": False, "error": f"Engine не отвечает ({exc})"}
   217	                    with contextlib.suppress(queue.Full):
   218	                        data_queue.put_nowait(
   219	                            {
   220	                                "__type": "cmd_timeout",
   221	                                "cmd": cmd_type,
   222	                                "ts": time.monotonic(),
   223	                                "message": f"REP timeout on {cmd_type} ({exc})",
   224	                            }
   225	                        )
   226	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
   227	                    reply = {"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}
   228	            finally:
   229	                req.close(linger=0)
   230	
   231	            if rid is not None:
   232	                reply["_rid"] = rid
   233	            try:
   234	                reply_queue.put(reply, timeout=2.0)
   235	            except queue.Full:
   236	                with contextlib.suppress(queue.Full):
   237	                    data_queue.put_nowait(
   238	                        {"__type": "warning", "message": "Reply queue overflow"}
   239	                    )
   240

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	source: CLAUDE.md "Физическая установка"; config/instruments.yaml; PROJECT_STATUS.md
     3	last_synced: 2026-04-26
     4	status: synthesized
     5	---
     6	
     7	# Hardware setup
     8	
     9	CryoDAQ runs against a single physical rig at the Millimetron / АКЦ
    10	ФИАН cryogenic lab. Three instrument families on three transports.
    11	
    12	## Instruments
    13	
    14	| Instrument | Interface | Channels | Driver |
    15	|---|---|---|---|
    16	| LakeShore 218S × 3 (`LS218_1` `GPIB0::12::INSTR`, `LS218_2` `GPIB0::14::INSTR`, `LS218_3` `GPIB0::16::INSTR`) | GPIB | 24 temperature (Т1..Т24) | [[20 Drivers/LakeShore 218S]] |
    17	| Keithley 2604B (`Keithley_1` `USB0::0x05E6::0x2604::04052028::INSTR`) | USB-TMC | `smua` + `smub` | [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]] |
    18	| Thyracont VSP63D type [^vsp206] (`VSP63D_1` COM3 @ 9600 baud) | RS-232 (serial) | 1 pressure | [[20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D]] |
    19	
    20	[^vsp206]: Production lab PC actually has VSP206 hardware (different checksum formula); driver still loaded as `thyracont_vsp63d` type with `validate_checksum: false` per-machine override. See [[20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D]] and [[10 Subsystems/Alarm engine v2]].
    21	
    22	Configuration lives in `config/instruments.yaml`; per-machine overrides
    23	in `config/instruments.local.yaml` (gitignored).
    24	
    25	## Channel layout (LakeShore daisy-chain)
    26	
    27	Three controllers, eight channels each, grouped semantically:
    28	
    29	- **LS218_1** (GPIB::12) — `Т1 Криостат верх`, `Т2 Криостат низ`, `Т3 Радиатор 1`,
    30	  `Т4 Радиатор 2`, `Т5 Экран 77К`, `Т6 Экран 4К`, `Т7 Детектор`,
    31	  `Т8 Калибровка`
    32	- **LS218_2** (GPIB::14) — `Т9 Компрессор вход`, `Т10 Компрессор выход`,
    33	  `Т11 Теплообменник 1`, `Т12 Теплообменник 2`, `Т13 Труба подачи`,
    34	  `Т14 Труба возврата`, `Т15 Вакуумный кожух`, `Т16 Фланец`
    35	- **LS218_3** (GPIB::16) — `Т17 Зеркало 1`, `Т18 Зеркало 2`, `Т19 Подвес`,
    36	  `Т20 Рама`, `Т21..24 Резерв 1..4`
    37	
    38	The «Т» prefix is **Cyrillic Т (U+0422)**, not Latin T. This homoglyph
    39	caused a real config-evaluation bug; see [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]].
    40	
    41	## Per-machine field state (Ubuntu lab PC, 2026-04-20)
    42	
    43	- `Т4 Радиатор 2` is **physically disconnected** — open-circuit reads
    44	  ~380 K. `interlocks.yaml::overheat_cryostat` regex was tightened
    45	  from `Т[1-8] .*` to `Т(1|2|3|5|6|7|8) .*` to exclude it without
    46	  losing coverage on Т1..Т8.
    47	- Thyracont hardware on this PC is actually **VSP206**, not VSP63D —
    48	  uses a different checksum formula. `instruments.local.yaml` carries
    49	  `validate_checksum: false` (per-machine override; `aabd75f` wired
    50	  the YAML key through after it had been silently ignored).
    51	- `ModemManager` was disabled on the lab PC because it was transiently
    52	  grabbing `/dev/ttyUSB0`.
    53	
    54	## Polling cadence
    55	
    56	| Instrument | `poll_interval_s` |
    57	|---|---|
    58	| LakeShore 218S × 3 | 2.0 |
    59	| Keithley 2604B | 1.0 |
    60	| Thyracont VSP63D | 2.0 |
    61	
    62	Polling is host-driven by [[10 Subsystems/Persistence-first|`Scheduler`]];
    63	per-driver intervals are independent.
    64	
    65	## Out-of-band hardware
    66	
    67	- **Keithley TSP supervisor** — `tsp/p_const.lua` is drafted but **not
    68	  loaded** on the device. P=const feedback runs host-side in
    69	  `keithley_2604b.py`. TSP supervisor planned for Phase 3 (requires
    70	  hardware verification).
    71	
    72	## See also
    73	
    74	- [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]] — uses temperature channels for rate
    75	  limit / overheat interlock
    76	- [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]] — calibration applies to LakeShore
    77	  channels via per-channel policy
    78	- [[_meta/glossary]] — KRDG, SRDG, FSM definitions

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	source: src/cryodaq/drivers/instruments/lakeshore_218s.py; drivers/transport/gpib.py; CLAUDE.md "Драйверы"; config/instruments.yaml
     3	last_synced: 2026-04-26
     4	status: synthesized
     5	---
     6	
     7	# LakeShore 218S
     8	
     9	8-channel cryogenic temperature monitor. Read-only with respect to
    10	the system being measured (no source authority).
    11	
    12	## Interface and channels
    13	
    14	- **Transport:** GPIB (`drivers/transport/gpib.py` via PyVISA).
    15	- **Default poll interval:** 2.0 s (`config/instruments.yaml`).
    16	- **Daisy-chain:** three controllers in production
    17	  (`LS218_1` GPIB::12, `LS218_2` GPIB::14, `LS218_3` GPIB::16).
    18	  Combined channel count: 24, named Т1..Т24 (Cyrillic Т prefix).
    19	- **Driver class:** `LakeShore218S` — extends `InstrumentDriver` ABC.
    20	
    21	Per-channel labels are loaded from
    22	`config/instruments.yaml::instruments[*].channels` and used as the
    23	canonical channel names everywhere downstream.
    24	
    25	## Reading kinds
    26	
    27	| Method | Command | Output |
    28	|---|---|---|
    29	| `read_channels()` | `KRDG?` (or `KRDG? <ch>` per-channel) | calibrated kelvin reading from the LakeShore controller (uses on-device calibration table) |
    30	| `read_srdg_channels()` | `SRDG?` | sensor raw value (Ohm / V depending on input type), pre-calibration |
    31	| `read_calibration_pair()` | `KRDG? <ch>` + `SRDG? <ch>` | one (kelvin, raw) pair for calibration v2 acquisition |
    32	| `read_status()` | `RDGST?` | per-channel status bitmask (overrange / underrange / sensor-error) |
    33	
    34	KRDG and SRDG are surfaced as separate channels in the broker so
    35	calibration acquisition (`CalibrationAcquisitionService`) can pair
    36	them. See [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]].
    37	
    38	## IDN validation with retry-after-clear (Phase 2c Codex F.1)
    39	
    40	`connect()` validates `*IDN?` against expected vendor before assuming
    41	control. Earlier behavior (warn-and-proceed) allowed silent
    42	mis-routing — `KRDG?` would return numbers from the wrong instrument
    43	without anyone noticing. Current behavior: retry once after `*CLS`,
    44	then fail hard if IDN still doesn't match.
    45	
    46	## Per-channel runtime calibration (v2)
    47	
    48	`LakeShore218S` ships per-channel runtime routing for calibration v2:
    49	
    50	- Global mode `on/off`: enable / disable per-channel calibration
    51	  application across all channels.
    52	- Per-channel policy `inherit | off | on`: overrides the global mode.
    53	- Conservative fallback: if curve / assignment / SRDG missing OR
    54	  computation throws, fall back to KRDG (controller calibrated reading)
    55	  and log the reason once per `(channel, reason)` to avoid log spam
    56	  (`_runtime_warning_cache`).
    57	
    58	`_use_per_channel_krdg` and `_use_per_channel_srdg` are tri-state
    59	flags driven by `_krdg0_fail_count` and `_srdg0_fail_count`: if the
    60	batch `KRDG?`/`SRDG?` form fails N times, the driver switches to
    61	per-channel form for `_krdg_batch_retry_interval_s` (default 60 s),
    62	then attempts batch form again.
    63	
    64	## Mock mode
    65	
    66	`mock=True` produces realistic signals via `_mock_sensor_unit(t)`
    67	(`temp` → ohms approximation `1600/(T+15) + 0.08`) over a tuple of
    68	realistic base temperatures (4.2 K, 4.8 K, 77 K, 77.5 K, 4.5 K, 4.1 K,
    69	3.9 K, 300 K). Useful for engine smoke tests and GUI development.
    70	
    71	## Notable quirks
    72	
    73	- **GPIB bus serialization.** Multiple LakeShores on the same bus
    74	  (`GPIB0::*`) cannot be polled in parallel — NI GPIB-USB-HS does
    75	  not tolerate concurrent access even with `asyncio.Lock` +
    76	  `run_in_executor`. `Scheduler` groups same-bus drivers into one
    77	  sequential task. See `core/scheduler.py:67`.
    78	- **`OVERRANGE`/`UNDERRANGE` valid as ±inf.** `RDGST?` returns
    79	  status codes; the driver maps them to `ChannelStatus` enum.
    80	  OVERRANGE/UNDERRANGE persist as `±inf` in REAL SQLite columns;
    81	  SENSOR_ERROR/TIMEOUT (NaN) are filtered before write (Phase 2d
    82	  invariant 10).
    83	- **Cyrillic Т prefix homoglyph hazard.** Per-channel labels use
    84	  Cyrillic Т (U+0422). LLMs occasionally produce Latin "T" in
    85	  generated config; tests can pass while the channel never matches
    86	  any rule. See [[30 Investigations/Cyrillic homoglyph in alarm config]].
    87	
    88	## See also
    89	
    90	- [[20 Drivers/Keithley 2604B]]
    91	- [[20 Drivers/Thyracont VSP63D]]
    92	- [[10 Subsystems/Calibration v2]]
    93	- [[10 Subsystems/Safety FSM]] — Т1..Т8 are critical channels for
    94	  rate-limit and overheat interlock

489:                voltage = math.sqrt(runtime.p_target * resistance)
507:        runtime = self._channels["smua"]

    18	|---|---|---|---|---|
    19	| `v0.33.0` | (last released tag) | shipped | Phase I.1 shell + design system v1.0.1 + IPC/REP hardening + theme switcher | tag points at the v0.33.0 sha (see `git show v0.33.0`) |
    20	| `v0.34.0` | (planned) | **blocked** | IV.4 safe features batch (F1 Parquet, F2 debug toggle, F6 auto-report verify, F11 shift handover); Phase II.1–II.6/II.13 cleanup. **Blocked by B1 ZMQ idle-death** ([[30 Investigations/B1 ZMQ idle-death]]). | `7cb5634` (IV.4 close) — pending real `git tag` |
    21	| Retroactive tags v0.34.0..v0.38.0 | (planned) | postponed | Per [[40 Decisions/2026-04-23-cleanup-baseline]], pre-2026-04-20 dev increments may be retroactively tagged once v0.34.0 ships. (Wording is per source ledger; chronologically it covers a future tag-pass over historical work.) | n/a |
    22	| `design-system-v1.0.1` | 2026-04-15..17 | non-package marker | Design system release marker, not a package version. | `1c61268` (CRITICAL domain cleanup) |
    23	| `pre-tier1-merge-backup-2026-04-14` | 2026-04-14 | safety marker | Pre-merge backup tag from before Tier 1 work. | n/a |
    24	
    25	Current branch tip (`master`): `cabd854 docs: Q4 equivalence check
    26	synthesis + D1 closure`. `pyproject.toml` reads `0.34.0.dev0` (bumped
    27	in `5030682`).
    28	
    29	## Pending tag — v0.34.0
    30	
    31	Per `ROADMAP.md`, IV.4 closed at `7cb5634` (2026-04-20) but the
    32	**v0.34.0 git tag has not been issued** because B1 (ZMQ command
    33	channel idle-death) still reproduces. Once B1 is mitigated via the
    34	IV.7 ipc:// path or another route, v0.34.0 can ship.

    36	Confirmed since IV.4 close:
    37	- Q4 equivalence check PASS (Codex gpt-5.5/high) on the b2b4fb5 R1
    38	  repair ([[40 Decisions/2026-04-24-d1-d4a-execution]] 22:16
    39	  addendum). Branch `feat/b2b4fb5-repair` merged via `89b4db1`.

    41	  B1 but stays in master as defense-in-depth.

    43	- `xml_safe` sanitizer for python-docx (`74dbbc7`) — fixes auto-report
    44	  failure with real Keithley `\x00` resource strings.
    45	
    46	## Release discipline
    47	
    48	Per `CLAUDE.md` "Дисциплина релизов": each new tag updates
    49	`CHANGELOG.md` (Added / Changed / Fixed / Infrastructure / Known
    50	Issues / Test baseline / Tags / Selected commits) and only updates
    51	`README.md` / `CLAUDE.md` if user-facing facts changed. No re-tagging
    52	to fold post-tag docs updates; no auto-update hooks.

First tagged release after:

2. Phase 2e Stage 1: streaming Parquet archive
3. Three Tier 1 contract fixes:

   - tier1-b: DataBroker subscriber exception isolation

   + dashboard foundation (blocks B.1-B.2) with temperature and
   pressure plot widgets
